## PAGE 00406: (2026x) MagicDraw-specific extensions

- page_id: `254437385`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437385/2026x+MagicDraw-specific+extensions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:24.762+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x) REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1263120387

INLINE

1263120388

INLINE

1263120386

INLINE

**On this page**

1263120388

INLINE

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model manipulation' space=''] describes how REST API can be used to manipulate data in Teamwork Cloud at the Eclipse modeling framework (EMF) level. However, usingthe API at the EMF level may become difficult when working with larger projects and more complex compositions.

Therefore, to facilitate the most common model manipulation tasks, Teamwork Cloud REST API has MagicDraw-specific extensions that help minimize the number of calls necessary to find the primary MagicDraw UML model within project decomposition.

##### Inspecting model decomposition

Normally, MagicDraw has different kinds of models within Teamwork Cloud resources. The term “models” here refers to the data structure required to store MagicDraw-specific project information that the tool needs when loading up projects on the client-side. Currently, Teamwork Cloud REST API exposes two kinds of such models – **decomposition** and **UML**, as shown in the code below. You can retrieve a list of available models within the master branch and chosen resource revision by issuing GET to */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models*.

```text

```

You can further inspect the content of each of the models by issuing GET to */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/{modelId}*, where *modelId* is **decomposition** or **uml** respectively.

##### Decomposition model

This model lists all of the Used Project hierarchy within the chosen resource. The excerpt of the results after calling */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/decomposition* is shown below.

```text

```

The main root project which the call is executed against has a type of **models:PrimaryProject**. You can find all of its directly used projects within the **models:uses** array. Since the decomposition model covers the full hierarchy, it includes both locally used projects (usually concerning Standard/System profiles) and server projects.

Each of the directly used projects has a type of **models:UsedProject** that has its own decomposition, hence enabling the full breakdown of usage structure from the primary project perspective covering both, directly and indirectly, used projects.

The **id** attribute refers to the project ID of **esiproject:EsiProject** type, not the UML model root element.

##### UML model

Different from **decomposition** model previously described, the **UML** model provides a flat view of the structure of the target model. Note that the term **project** should not be confused with the term **UML** model here. The purpose of the **UML** model is to expose direct links to root elements of UML models in primary projects, and both of its, directly and indirectly, used projects.

The excerpt of the results after calling */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/uml*is shown below.

```text

```

The **models:PrimaryModel** type object provides a direct link to the main project’s UML model root element via the **models:root** attribute. Compared to the EMF-based approach described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model manipulation' space=''], using the above REST API extension is a matter of a single call and requires only the resource ID and revision number to be known in advance.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437283 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00407: (2026x) Managing code engineering sets

- page_id: `254437283`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437283/2026x+Managing+code+engineering+sets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:18.454+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Code engineering using API
- labels: ['code-engineering-manager', 'java-code-engineering-manager', 'java-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringManager* class provides static API methods for creating, removing, reversing, generating, and adjusting code-engineering settings. The *CodeEngineeringManager* class is used for managing code engineering sets.

When creating the code engineering set, the following fields are required:

- Language and dialect IDs. All languages and dialects IDs are stored in the com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringConstants class.
- The name of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Code engineering set' space=''] .
- The project, where code engineering set will be added.
- The working package.
- The working directory.

New *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringSet* can be created directly via *CodeEngineeringManager*using the *CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String,**Project, Package, java.lang.String)***method.

The language and dialect can be selected from the *CodeEngineeringConstants* class. The dialect is required for DDL and C++ languages.

**•**null *workingPackage*means, that the code engineering working package is *Model* by default

**•**null *workingDirectory*means, that the code engineering working directory is the modeling tool install root

To remove the instance of *CodeEngineeringSet* use the *CodeEngineeringManager.removeCodeEngineeringSet(**CodeEngineeringSet)***method.

All Code Engineering Sets can be retrieved by the *CodeEngineeringManager.getAllCodeEngineeringSets(**Project)***method.

To perform generation of the CodeEngineeringSet object use the *CodeEngineeringManager.generate(**Project**, boolean)***method.

To perform the reverse engineering use the *CodeEngineeringManager.reverse(**Project**, boolean)***method.

###### ****

###### **Languag****e Specific Options**

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.JavaCodeEngineeringManager* provides methods to set Java specific options.These options applie to all code engineering sets in the project.

To add a classpaths for the java code engineering sets, use *JavaCodeEngineeringManager.setJavaClasspath(**Project, java.lang.String[])*

Setting a new classpath will override the old one.

To get applied classpaths for java code engineering sets, use *JavaCodeEngineeringManager.getJavaClasspath(**Project)*.

To resolve collection generics when reversing the java code, *CodeEngineeringManager.setResolveCollectionGenerics(**Project**, boolean)*

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437297 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00408: (2026x) Managing compartments

- page_id: `254437297`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437297/2026x+Managing+compartments
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:19.326+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some presentation elements displays information in so called compartments. For example UML Class shape has attributes, operations, ports compartments.

Compartments can show only a subset of information. The rest information can be hidden. For example show only a couple of specific Operations on a Class shape in the diagram.

Use*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.CompartmentManager* for managing the visibility of compartment entries.

More information is available in javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\compartmentedit

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Session management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Some presentation elements displays information in so called compartments. For example UML Class shape has attributes, operations, ports compartments.</p><p>Compartments can show only a subset of information. The rest information can be hidden. For example show only a couple of specific Operations on a Class shape in the diagram.</p><p>Use<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.CompartmentManager</em> for managing the visibility of compartment entries.</p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\compartmentedit</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="becf631e-811b-484c-90d3-30aaa50ab94c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437105 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00409: (2026x) Managing hyperlinks

- page_id: `254437105`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437105/2026x+Managing+hyperlinks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:01.579+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Managing navigation in model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UML models may have a different kind of hyperlinks assigned to model elements. There is API for managing these hyperlinks - you can assign new hyperlinks, remove existing.

It is also possible to register your own hyperlink handler for some custom hyperlink type.

See the following pages for more information:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>UML models may have a different kind of hyperlinks assigned to model elements. There is API for managing these hyperlinks - you can assign new hyperlinks, remove existing. </p><p>It is also possible to register your own hyperlink handler for some custom hyperlink type.</p><p>See the following pages for more information:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8a88dff7-44c7-44b9-af31-4dfaeca31809" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437101 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00410: (2026x) Managing navigation in model

- page_id: `254437101`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437101/2026x+Managing+navigation+in+model

- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:00.230+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open API allows to change how navigation in a model is handled. Using that API you can create and manage hyperlinks, change the mouse double-click behavior on elements.

See these pages for more details:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing double-click behavior' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing hyperlinks' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Using that API you can create and manage hyperlinks, change the mouse double-click behavior on elements. </p><p>See these pages for more details:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing double-click behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing hyperlinks" /></ac:link> </li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437504 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00411: (2026x) MD Customization for SysML Profile

- page_id: `254437504`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437504/2026x+MD+Customization+for+SysML+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:36.935+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) SysML Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to import *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile* to use this API class.

**Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)**

Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.STEREOTYPE_PROPERTY_NAME”.

For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.NUMBEROWNER_PREFIX_PROPERTY returns a string of “prefix”.

****

**Get a stereotype element**

Usage includes:

- “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getStereotype()” - where project refers to the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.
- “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(element).getStereotype()” - where element refers to the element in the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.

For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getPartProperty() returns the reference to the «PartProperty» stereotype object.

****

**Check if an element is stereotyped**

Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isStereotype(Elem)” - where Elem is the element you would like to check.

For example, given an element “Elem”, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isValueProperty(Elem) returns True if the element “Elem” has «ValueProperty» stereotype applied, and returns false otherwise.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You need to import <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</em> to use this API class.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)</strong></p><p>Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.STEREOTYPE_PROPERTY_NAME”.</p><p>For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.NUMBEROWNER_PREFIX_PROPERTY returns a string of “prefix”.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Get a stereotype element</strong></p><p>Usage includes:</p><ul><li>“MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getStereotype()” - where project refers to the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.</li><li>“MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(element).getStereotype()” - where element refers to the element in the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.</li></ul><p>For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getPartProperty() returns the reference to the «PartProperty» stereotype object.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Check if an element is stereotyped</strong></p><p>Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isStereotype(Elem)” - where Elem is the element you would like to check.</p><p>For example, given an element “Elem”, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isValueProperty(Elem) returns True if the element “Elem” has «ValueProperty» stereotype applied, and returns false otherwise.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437679 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00412: (2026x) MDEL hyperlink

- page_id: `254437679`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437679/2026x+MDEL+hyperlink
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:52.046+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Managing navigation in model > (2026x) Managing hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw has a functionality to expose every element as URL. Later this URL can be added into external documents. Also that URL can be used to open/activate an element in a project.

For that purpose the **mdel://** protocol is introduced.

Use c*om.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils* to retrieve mdel URL of elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw has a functionality to expose every element as URL. Later this URL can be added into external documents. Also that URL can be used to open/activate an element in a project.</p><p>For that purpose the <strong>mdel://</strong> protocol is introduced. </p><p>Use c<em>om.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils</em> to retrieve mdel URL of elements.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437480 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00413: (2026x) MDP protocol

- page_id: `254437480`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437480/2026x+MDP+protocol
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:34.147+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Adding new functionality > (2026x) Working with icons
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw registers a custom URL protocol handler **mdp://**for loading resources (icons) from MagicDraw or plugins jars/classpath. Resources can also be loaded from attached files in an opened project.

Usage samples:

- Use the mdp protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some image from a plugin jar.
- Use the mdp protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some attached image from a project.

**URL syntax**

| mdp://resource/ | Use the "resource" host to load resources from jars/classpaths. For example, to load the activitydiagram.png icon from MagicDraw jar, use such URL:mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/icons/diagrams/activitydiagram.png |
| --- | --- |
| mdp://attachedFile/ | Use the "attachedFile" host to load resources from the attached file into the active project. For example, to load the icon from the attached file element with ID "_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288", use such URL:mdp://attachedFile#_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288 |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw registers a custom URL protocol handler <strong>mdp:// </strong>for loading resources (icons) from MagicDraw or plugins jars/classpath. Resources can also be loaded from attached files in an opened project. </p><div><p>Usage samples:</p><ul><li>Use the <em>mdp</em> protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some image from a plugin jar.</li><li>Use the <em>mdp</em> protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some attached image from a project.</li></ul><p> </p><p><strong>URL syntax</strong></p><table><tbody><tr><td>mdp://resource/</td><td><p>Use the &quot;<em>resource</em>&quot; host to load resources from jars/classpaths. For example, to load the <em>activitydiagram.png</em> icon from MagicDraw jar, use such URL:</p><pre><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/icons/diagrams/activitydiagram.png</span></pre></td></tr><tr><td>mdp://attachedFile/</td><td><p><span>Use the &quot;<em>attachedFile</em>&quot; host to load resources from the attached file into the active project. For example, to load the icon from the attached file element with ID &quot;<em>_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288</em>&quot;, use such URL:</span></p><pre><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">mdp://attachedFile#_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288</span></pre></td></tr></tbody></table></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437404 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00414: (2026x) Merging and differencing

- page_id: `254437404`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437404/2026x+Merging+and+differencing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:26.349+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The modeling tools provide APIs for project merging and differencing. There are two separate APIs:

- Merge - allows for project merging. The Merge API comes together with the Project Merge plugin, which requires a license. You can access the Merge API javadoc at <installation_directory>\openapi\docs\merge_javadoc.zip once you acquire and install the Project Merge plugin.
- Compare - allows for project differences comparison. The Compare API comes together with the internal Merge Core plugin, which is available pre-installed on the modeling tool. You can access the Compare API javadoc at <installation_directory>\openapi\docs\javadoc.zip once you have the modeling tool installed.

Project merging consists of three steps:

1. Discovering project changes (differences) . The ancestor (in a 3-way merge only) and source projects are loaded (if they are not loaded yet) for comparing.
2. Accepting / rejecting changes . All target changes that do not conflict source changes are applied (source changes conflicting with target changes are rejected). A 2-way merge produces source changes only.
3. Applying accepted changes . Changes are applied on the ancestor project (target and ancestor projects are the same in a 2-way merge).

There are APIs for standard and advanced project merging. The standard API encapsulates all three steps into a single call, while the advanced merge API allows for accessing the project differences and controlling what changes are accepted or rejected.

The following figure shows the domain model of the merging procedure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****You can find the code examples:

- for Merge API (requires the Project Merge plugin to be installed first): <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\merge
- for Compare API: < installation_direc tory>\openapi\examples\compare

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The modeling tools provide APIs for project merging and differencing. There are two separate APIs:</p><ul><li><strong>Merge</strong> - allows for project merging. The Merge API comes together with the Project Merge plugin, which requires a license. You can access the Merge API javadoc at <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\docs\merge_javadoc.zip</em> once you acquire and install the Project Merge plugin.</li><li><strong>Compare</strong> - allows for project differences comparison. The Compare API comes together with the internal Merge Core plugin, which is available pre-installed on the modeling tool. You can access the Compare API javadoc at <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\docs\javadoc.zip</em> once you have the modeling tool installed.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>Project merging consists of three steps:</p><ol><li><strong>Discovering project changes (differences)</strong>. The ancestor (in a 3-way merge only) and source projects are loaded (if they are not loaded yet) for comparing.</li><li><strong>Accepting / rejecting changes</strong>. All target changes that do not conflict source changes are applied (source changes conflicting with target changes are rejected). A 2-way merge produces source changes only.</li><li><strong>Applying accepted changes</strong>. Changes are applied on the ancestor project (target and ancestor projects are the same in a 2-way merge).</li></ol><p>There are APIs for standard and advanced project merging. The standard API encapsulates all three steps into a single call, while the advanced merge API allows for accessing the project differences and controlling what changes are accepted or rejected.</p><p>The following figure shows the domain model of the merging procedure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MergeDomain.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Merging and differencing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples:</p><ul><li>for Merge API (requires the Project Merge plugin to be installed first): <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\merge</em></li><li>for Compare API: &lt;<em>installation_direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\compare</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437387 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00415: (2026x) Model manipulation

- page_id: `254437387`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437387/2026x+Model+manipulation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:24.882+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x) REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1043546914

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1043546918

INLINE

1043546912

INLINE

**On this page**

33

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1043546917

INLINE

Teamwork Cloud operates at the EMF level, while MagicDraw operates at the UML level, which is on top of EMF. REST API, which is the Teamwork Cloud API, also operates at the EMF level.

Therefore, Teamwork Cloud is not affected by UML-specific implementation, and all derived properties may not be saved in the Teamwork Cloud database. The best-known derived property is *owner*. This is the result when there is no such “*setOwner”*in REST API.

Even though all non-derived properties are saved in the Teamwork Cloud database, they are saved as raw EMF data. The user is required to have knowledge about UML models to manipulate data. For example, an applied stereotype is saved as a hierarchy of *InstanceSpecification*, *Slot*, and *ValueSpecification*.

This section describes how to traverse into a UML model and create some elements in it.

##### MagicDraw project

In Teamwork Cloud terminology, a MagicDraw project is referred to as a *resource*. Although REST API can be used to create a Teamwork Cloud resource, it is only a bare project MagicDraw cannot read. A MagicDraw project is composed of many MagicDraw-specific meta-data such as project options, primary project, and used projects configuration. The best way to create a MagicDraw project is to create it by MagicDraw and submit it to the Teamwork Cloud server.

##### MagicDraw primary project

To traverse through a UML model, the primary project must be identified. The model data starts at the revision level. Issuing GET to */revisions/{revisionId}* shows the first-level object in the revision. UUIDs of the first-level object are listed in **rootObjectIDs**.

```text

```

Among these entries, the first element with the **esiproject:EsiProject** type specifies the primary project named **main decomposition project**. Other **esiproject:EsiProject** objects are the models (used projects) in a MagicDraw project. The main decomposition project is element **429f969a**. You need to load element **429f969a** and all their children. An element is represented in the JSONLD format. The attributes of the elements are in **kerml:esiData**. There will be an element named **UML Model**. The below code fragment shows the attributes of this element.

```text

```

Take the first **esiproject:EsiDataSection** from the feature's sections list, which is **b3e68e8e**. The excerpt of the **b3e68e8e** data is shown below.

```text

```

The first ID in the **rootElements** list of the data section will be the ID of the UML model root element in the resource. The root element should always be of the **uml:Package** type or derived from it.

##### Traversing to a MagicDraw model

Once a root project is retrieved, the whole hierarchy can be retrieved layer by layer. An element can be retrieved from */elements/{elementId}*. Child UUIDs of the resulting element are in **kerml:ownedElement**.

Multiple elements can be retrieved by POST to */elements*. In this case, you need to put UUID in the **uuid.txt****file to load elements.

```text

```

The command to load those elements is:

```bash

```

In most cases, loading multiple elements at once is substantially faster than loading them one by one.

##### Creating elements

To use REST API to create elements, you are required to have knowledge about UML models, especially how to set the owner. A combination of parent-child type yields differences in the owner attribute. For example, if you want to create a class under a class, you need to set the **UMLClass** attribute in the child class. For a class under a package, you need to set **owningPackage** in the child class. The easiest way to know which attribute is needed is to try creating it in MagicDraw and get it in REST API.

There are two URLs to create element(s), */resources/{resourceId}/elements* and */resources/{resourceId}/elements{elementId}*. Please note that the *elementId* in the latter form is not the parent. An Ecore is needed to create an element. It can be specified in the following ways:

- Specified from the *elementId* in */resources/{resourceId}/elements/*{elementId}**. This form is used in most cases, except from creating the first element in the project. The new element data type will be in the same namespace of this specified element. text
- Specified from kerml:nsURI .
- Specifying the whole Ecore in **kerml:ecore**. This mode is rarely used. text\r\n\r\n 
 <\/eClassifiers>\r\n<\/ecore:EPackage>\r\n"
}]]>

The following script creates an element:

```text

```

Multiple elements can be created at once by specifying the query parameter *batch=true* in the URL. The script is shown below:

```text

```

##### Applied stereotypes

Applied stereotypes stored in the **appliedStereotype** attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to stereotype objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype.

```text

```

To apply the stereotype, set PATCH to the element you are working on, as shown below.

```text

```

The UUID **8dbdc804** is an ID of the applied stereotype whose content is applied via the following command where the UUID 8df9f306 is an ID of the element to which the stereotype is applied:

```text

```

##### Tagged values

Tagged values stored in the **taggedValue** attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to tagged value objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype and tagged values.

```text

```

To add a tagged value, apply the stereotype to the target element first.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The model is incorrect if tagged values do not have their corresponding stereotypes.

To apply the tagged value, use the POST request with the following code:

```text

```

The command will be:

```bash

```

The best way to create a MagicDraw project is to create it by MagicDraw and submit it to the Teamwork Cloud server.</p><h3>MagicDraw primary project</h3><p>To traverse through a UML model, the primary project must be identified. The model data starts at the revision level. Issuing GET to <em>/revisions/{revisionId}</em> shows the first-level object in the revision. UUIDs of the first-level object are listed in <strong>rootObjectIDs</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56ba1f1d-55d3-42ed-8c75-d9773deaf9ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"commitType": "NORMAL",
    "branchID": "../..",
    "resourceID": "../../..",
    "@base": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/4615e8fa-81e5-40e0-a51b-8496a48caf18/revisions/5/elements",

    ],
    "pickedRevision": -1,
    "description": "Branch \"xx\" created",
    "@context": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schemas/revision",
    "directParent": 3,
    "dependencies": [],
    "rootObjectIDs": 
	[
      	"429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950",
		"ec6060a3-f3d9-482b-93a3-32af9e19202c",
		"ca9a0235-f0f7-46b7-a142-e79a67c2d00d",     
		"f7c3ae92-af44-4dab-8163-a199ca05c006",
		"ba3d0700-1062-4baf-a1df-a55a4f31ce54",
		"0f14cd2d-2fd0-4523-950c-627d59e1a43d",
      	"7cd22dea-aaf8-4e08-bd67-5bd975c3f06a",
     	"af1042fa-8b1b-4cf2-bb7d-98dd1b881da3",
		"6d24e5e7-cdff-4e9e-85b8-28b3088f85b6",
		"243020e5-da6c-4896-b32a-fcba0e93ac8d",
		"f7449238-5cd1-41eb-9025-040210b02d93",
      	"4d2459a1-49dc-4eb7-aa82-9bbb4a76b038",
		"b242613d-957e-4aec-9333-e5938f50b2ab",  
		"9b953064-e422-4391-b7d9-43a2d4f14a32",
		"fc997cfd-23c5-4d0b-9953-06667dcde0dd",
		"29d9416b-ead5-4a9d-b530-b23de836f1b8",
		"7af3f24b-2da9-4b31-94b3-a87f15747296"
	],
	"createdDate": "1533051367",
	"ID": "",
    "artifacts": "artifacts"
},]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Among these entries, the first element with the <strong>esiproject:EsiProject</strong> type specifies the primary project named <strong>main decomposition project</strong>. Other <strong>esiproject:EsiProject</strong> objects are the models (used projects) in a MagicDraw project. The main decomposition project is element <strong>429f969a</strong>. You need to load element <strong>429f969a</strong> and all their children. An element is represented in the JSONLD format. The attributes of the elements are in <strong>kerml:esiData</strong>. There will be an element named <strong>UML Model</strong>. The below code fragment shows the attributes of this element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a231d404-e71f-45b7-961f-0acc426c1902"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA["kerml:esiData":
{
	"name": "UML Model",
    "namespace": "com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model",
	"project": 
	{
		"@id": "429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950"
	},
	"internalVersion": "1",
	"version": "17.0",
	"sections": 
	[
		{
			"@id": "b3e68e8e-2253-4726-8d05-d8f74fd0ba5a"
        }
	]
},]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Take the first <strong>esiproject:EsiDataSection</strong> from the feature's sections list, which is <strong>b3e68e8e</strong>. The excerpt of the <strong>b3e68e8e</strong> data is shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60584186-0d26-423e-a1e2-d829ed92b6d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"kerml:name": "model",
	"@base": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/4615e8fa-81e5-40e0-a51b-8496a48caf18/revisions/5/elements",
	"kerml:nsURI": "http://www.nomagic.com/ns/magicdraw/esiproject/1.0",
	"@type": "esiproject:EsiDataSection",
    "kerml:owner": 
	{
		"@id": "429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950"
    },
    "kerml:revision": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/4615e8fa-81e5-40e0-a51b-8496a48caf18/revisions/5",
    "@context": 
	{
		"esiproject:EsiDataSection": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/esiproject/2014345/EsiDataSection",
		"kerml": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/kerml/20140325"
    },
    "kerml:ownedElement": [],
    "kerml:modifiedTime": "20180731223607ICT",
    "kerml:esiData": 
	{
		"featuredBy": 
		{
			"@id": "c9256728-4617-4097-8e9e-dc63e2823bf8"
		},
		"rootElements":
		[
			"ca9a0235-f0f7-46b7-a142-e79a67c2d00d"
		],
		"name": "model",
      	"project":
		{
			"@id": "429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950"
      	},
		"properties": []
	},
	"kerml:resource": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/4615e8fa-81e5-40e0-a51b-8496a48caf18",
    "kerml:esiID": "b3e68e8e-2253-4726-8d05-d8f74fd0ba5a",
	"@id": "#b3e68e8e-2253-4726-8d05-d8f74fd0ba5a"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The first ID in the <strong>rootElements</strong> list of the data section will be the ID of the UML model root element in the resource. The root element should always be of the <strong>uml:Package</strong> type or derived from it.</p><h3>Traversing to a MagicDraw model</h3><p>Once a root project is retrieved, the whole hierarchy can be retrieved layer by layer. An element can be retrieved from <em>/elements/{elementId}</em>. Child UUIDs of the resulting element are in <strong>kerml:ownedElement</strong>.</p><p>Multiple elements can be retrieved by POST to <em>/elements</em>. In this case, you need to put UUID in the <strong>uuid.txt</strong><em> </em>file to load elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33153f09-c2b7-48f5-a3ed-31c4b49e6b7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[4eeb2e6d-9cbc-4f59-9d74-69263e52ba54,520bc74d-b5b4-40d5-87cb-9d0b8aba5d2e,7a057ae7-6281-462d-9dcd-de9931633f5c,a6212656-5ad1-4de6-a47f-db656c2c25fd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The command to load those elements is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a018be71-340d-4466-a56e-6e1bb98c8511"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[curl -v -H "Content-Type: text/plain" -X POST -s --cookie cookie.txt --insecure -d @uuid.txt --insecure "https://server:8111/osmc/resources/${projectId}/elements"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In most cases, loading multiple elements at once is substantially faster than loading them one by one.</p><h3>Creating elements</h3><p>To use REST API to create elements, you are required to have knowledge about UML models, especially how to set the owner. A combination of parent-child type yields differences in the owner attribute. For example, if you want to create a class under a class, you need to set the <strong>UMLClass</strong> attribute in the child class. For a class under a package, you need to set <strong>owningPackage</strong> in the child class. The easiest way to know which attribute is needed is to try creating it in MagicDraw and get it in REST API.</p><p>There are two URLs to create element(s), <em>/resources/{resourceId}/elements</em> and <em>/resources/{resourceId}/elements{elementId}</em>. Please note that the <em>elementId</em> in the latter form is not the parent. An Ecore is needed to create an element. It can be specified in the following ways:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specified from the <em>elementId</em> in <em>/resources/{resourceId}/elements/<em>{elementId}</em></em>. This form is used in most cases, except from creating the first element in the project. The new element data type will be in the same namespace of this specified element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cda4bcb3-e1df-42bb-b58f-96a53a031c9f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@type": "uml:Class",
	"kerml:nsURI": "http://www.nomagic.com/magicdraw/UML/2.5.1",
	"kerml:esiData": 
	{
		"owningPackage": 
		{
			"@id": "757be712-f397-404d-a5ff-b97567eb240f"
        },
        "name": "c3"
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Specified from <strong>kerml:nsURI</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specifying the whole Ecore in <strong>kerml:ecore</strong>. This mode is rarely used.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="352b1dcd-d2c6-4040-970f-2dde4c18d7ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@type": "ikml:Container",
	"kerml:esiData": 
	{
		"name": "txdhdhdhd",
		"uri": "http://www.chula.ac.th",
    },
	"kerml:ecore": "<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\r\n<ecore:EPackage xmi:version=\"2.0\" xmlns:xmi=\"http://www.omg.org/XMI\" 
	xmlns:xsi=\"http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance\"\r\n
	xmlns:ecore=\"http://www.eclipse.org/emf/2002/Ecore\"
	name=\"ikml\" nsURI=\"http://www.nomagic.com/ikml/1.0\"
	nsPrefix=\"ikml\">\r\n 
	<eClassifiers xsi:type=\"ecore:EClass\" name=\"Element\" abstract=\"true\"><\/eClassifiers>\r\n<\/ecore:EPackage>\r\n"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following script creates an element:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abf6e33b-2609-416e-b360-e1c3d88c33f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cat file.txt | curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s --cookie cookie.txt --insecure -d @-https://server:8111/osmc/resources/${projectId}/elements]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Multiple elements can be created at once by specifying the query parameter <em>batch=true</em> in the URL. The script is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b654223-c3a5-495c-baa9-8180037d07db"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cat file.txt | curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s --cookie cookie.txt --insecure -d @-"https://server:8111/osmc/resources/${projectId}/elements/${elementId}?batch=true"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Applied stereotypes</h3><p>Applied stereotypes stored in the <strong>appliedStereotype</strong> attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to stereotype objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c58e346-18a9-4370-b75d-d3dac640b749"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "@id": "#bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"kerml:esiID": "bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"@type": "uml:Class",
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"ID": "_2021x_2_3360135_1630482378802_586147_17",
		"mdExtensions": [],
		"_representationText": null,
		"taggedValue": [
			{
				"@id": "edaa1dd8-583d-4a8b-8974-3111aef8d47b"
            }
		],
		"appliedStereotype": [
			{
				"@id": "8dbdc804-5eed-424d-9812-28e183a8dfdc"
            }
		]
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To apply the stereotype, set PATCH to the element you are working on, as shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e88402a-1b6d-42d0-8124-a5c56c4611c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@context": {
		"@base": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/workspaces/59068af0-a7ae-4663-
b972-3c58a2fde23f/resources/b0585059-c4e3-4d82-90d9-c5386045bb2e/branches/
5dfb978f-0984-47ea-aaec-bdbb5a945687/elements/",
		"kerml": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/kerml/20140325",
		"uml:Class": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/uml/2014345/Class"
	},
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"appliedStereotype": [
			{
				"@id": "8dbdc804-5eed-424d-9812-28e183a8dfdc"
            }
		]
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The UUID <strong>8dbdc804</strong> is an ID of the applied stereotype whose content is applied via the following command where the UUID 8df9f306 is an ID of the element to which the stereotype is applied:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a519b65-4594-4d3b-a2f9-5bf201fa4a7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$ curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s -c ..\get\cookie.txt -b
..\get\cookie.txt --insecure -d @- https://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/2663059e-8ef5-4f13-97b6-864a0353d2d0/elements/8df9f306-c4dc-41fb-ac9f-4a87fe71610a]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Tagged values</h3><p>Tagged values stored in the <strong>taggedValue</strong> attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to tagged value objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype and tagged values.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4790701f-dcda-46eb-9c21-43ede212c123"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@id": "#bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"kerml:esiID": "bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"@type": "uml:Class",
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"ID": "_2021x_2_3360135_1630482378802_586147_17",
        "mdExtensions": [],
		"_representationText": null,
		"taggedValue": [
			{
				"@id": "d9388ec6-ddca-4c18-9a5c-409d8649731f"
            },
            {
				"@id": "edaa1dd8-583d-4a8b-8974-3111aef8d47b"
            }
		],
		"appliedStereotype": [
			{
				"@id": "8dbdc804-5eed-424d-9812-28e183a8dfdc"
            }
        ]
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To add a tagged value, apply the stereotype to the target element first.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="048b897c-21c3-47d3-a4d2-a1796364e739"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The model is incorrect if tagged values do not have their corresponding stereotypes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To apply the tagged value, use the POST request with the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f2f7a78-453b-49dc-a35d-26fccf8dadc9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@type": "uml:StringTaggedValue",
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"tagDefinition": {
			"@id": "657a3511-d012-4134-af5d-411a6df78745"
        },
		"taggedValueOwner": {
			"@id": "515073dd-fe25-4327-bfc2-6466375a50f7"
        },
		"value": [
			"somevaluefromrest"
        ]
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The command will be:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e2fd526-78f4-4ca3-bc53-f94b7925d50e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s -c ..\get\cookie.txt -b ..\get\cookie.txt
--insecure -d @- https://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/2663059e-8ef5-4f13-97b6-864a0353d2d0/elements/8df9f306-c4dc-41fb-ac9f-4a87fe71610a]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9dbb4036-6351-4e7a-a701-2b65f4490680"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1043575942</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) REST APIs" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) General convention" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Authentication" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) MagicDraw-specific extensions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437216 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00416: (2026x) Model traversing, Visitor pattern

- page_id: `254437216`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437216/2026x+Model+traversing+Visitor+pattern
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:12.986+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Here is an example of how to collect all children from the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element* and avoid recursion using a simple *for*cycle:

```java
children = new ArrayList(); 
    Element current = ... 
    children.add(current);
    // if the current element has children, list will be increased. 
    for (int i = 0; i < children.size(); i++)
    {
        current = children.get(i);
        // add all children into end of this list, so it emulates a recursion. 
        children.addAll(current.getOwnedElement());
    }]]>
```

It is highly recommended to use more advanced collecting methods from *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder* utility:

- byScope()
- byType()
- byTypeRecursively()

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Here is an example of how to collect all children from the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em> and avoid recursion using a simple <em>for </em>cycle:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1480f664-32bf-4755-aa6c-63eb86e14427"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    List<Element> children = new ArrayList<Element>(); 
    Element current = ... 
    children.add(current);
    // if the current element has children, list will be increased. 
    for (int i = 0; i < children.size(); i++)
    {
        current = children.get(i);
        // add all children into end of this list, so it emulates a recursion. 
        children.addAll(current.getOwnedElement());
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><p>It is highly recommended to use more advanced collecting methods from <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder</em> utility:</p><ul><li><em>byScope()</em></li><li><em>byType()</em></li><li><em>byTypeRecursively()</em></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d87af5a4-da9b-49c0-aab8-6991bc3cdde8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="674518bd-42f1-4fec-be9d-51cc04f7a18e"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437218 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00417: (2026x) ModelHierarchyVisitor

- page_id: `254437218`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437218/2026x+ModelHierarchyVisitor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:13.108+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Working with UML model > (2026x) Model traversing, Visitor pattern
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor* is an enhanced implementation of the *Visitor*pattern and a subclass of *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelVisitor*, used for visiting model elements.

Every *visit<element_metatype>*method calls a *visit<element_metatype>*method for a super type.

Model elements *visit…*methods have an additional context parameter of the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.VisitorContext* type. The visitor context is used to track what super type *visit<element_metatype> m*ethods were already visited (to avoid multiple visits because some model elements have a multiple inheritance). Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation.

For example, you can put some code into *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor.visitClassifier(**Classifier)***and it will be executed for all subclasses of a *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier*.

```java
it = root.getOwnedElement().iterator();
    while (it.hasNext())
    {
        it.next().accept(myVisitor);
    }]]>
```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\statistics*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor</em> is an enhanced implementation of the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Visitor </em>pattern and a subclass of <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelVisitor</em>, used for visiting model elements. </p><p>Every <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; </em>method calls a <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; </em>method for a super type.</p><p>Model elements <em>visit… </em>methods have an additional context parameter of the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.VisitorContext</em> type. The visitor context is used to track what super type <em>visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; m</em>ethods were already visited (to avoid multiple visits because some model elements have a multiple inheritance). Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation.</p><p><br /></p><p>For example, you can put some code into <em>com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor.visitClassifier(</em><em>Classifier)</em><em> </em>and it will be executed for all subclasses of a <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier</em>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7085da27-f3ae-415f-9197-30427949c8f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ModelVisitor myVisitor = new ModelHierarchyVisitor()
    {
        public void visitClassifier(Classifier element, VisitorContext context)
        {
          	//this is my classifier
        }
    };
    Project project = ...;
    Package root = project.getPrimaryModel(); 
    Iterator<Element> it = root.getOwnedElement().iterator();
    while (it.hasNext())
    {
        it.next().accept(myVisitor);
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\statistics</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437091 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00418: (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide

- page_id: `254437091`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437091/2026x+Modeling+Tools+Developer+Guide
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-28T15:10:43.896+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the generated javadoc ([https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/)), you will find a detailed description of classes, their attributes, and operations. JavaDocs are located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\docs*.

This guide describes the Open Java API of MagicDraw or a Cameo Suite product, such as Cameo Systems Modeler, and provides instructions on how to implement custom plugins, add actions to the menus or toolbars, change UML model elements as well as create new patterns. Though MagicDraw is used in examples mainly, other modeling tools, since they are based on the MagicDraw modeling platform, perform the same.

The program core code and used libraries are packaged in jars files that are located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\lib* (and its subdirectories).

Some plugins also exposes Open API. Plugins libraries can be located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\plugins\<plugin_name>*

The code can be divided into the following three API scope groups:

- OpenAPI – a code for public usage, stable through builds and versions.
- InternalAPI – a code for the internal NoMagic usage only, may change through builds and versions without any restrictions.
- NotAPI – not an API code, it may change in each build.

Java annotations are used to distinguish which API scope in the provided code belongs to. Annotations of the type (class, interface, enum) declaration:

| Annotation | API Scope | Scope applies to |
| --- | --- | --- |
| @OpenApiAll | OpenAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |
| @OpenApi | OpenAPI | The annotated type declaration only. Each declared member, constructor is annotated explicitly with the @OpenApi or @InternalApi annotation (see the following table). |
| @InternalApi | InternalAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |
| @NotApi | NotAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |

Annotations of the member, constructor declaration of the @OpenApi type:

| Annotation | API Scope | Scope applies to |
| --- | --- | --- |
| @OpenApi | OpenAPI | The annotated declared member or constructor. |
| @InternalApi | InternalAPI | The annotated declared member or constructor. |

Only code from the OpenAPI scope should be used to extend the modeling tool. All the InternalAPI and NotAPI scope code are additionally annotated with the *@Deprecated* annotation. This allows seeing the illegal API usage in all modern IDEs without an additional configuration.

We provide a set of plugin samples in *<*modeling tool installation directory*>\openapi\examples*. By using these examples, you can find out on how to use an OpenAPI.

In the generated javadoc, you will find the detailed description of classes, their attributes, and operations. JavaDocs are located in *<*modeling tool installation directory*>\openapi\docs*.

<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To move a symbol on the diagram to another location, you can use the following method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a56154ac-0627-4845-9653-ee18d51a0f3a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElementManager.getInstance().movePresentationElement(PresentationElement view, Point location)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17822cd1-f81b-4e4a-953a-f7e105178d09"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElement view = ...; 
    SessionsManager.getInstance().createSession("Move symbol"); 
    PresentationsElementsManager.getInstance().movePresentationElement(view, new Point(50, 50));
    SessionsManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Similarly, a group of symbols can be moved by using the following method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab01b1f8-334a-42da-9009-3d61172a088e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    movePresentationElements(List<PresentationElement> views, Point location)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\symbolmove</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b483f3d-c1a6-45f7-9612-a04919e10de5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437346 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00421: (2026x) Multi-threading

- page_id: `254437346`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437346/2026x+Multi-threading
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:23.149+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The majority of the application code (including OpenAPI) is not thread safe. Only the UML model is thread safe for reading (the UML model can be queried safely by concurrent threads), but still the UML model writing (modification) is not thread safe.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Running action with progress' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Only the UML model is thread safe for reading (the UML model can be queried safely by concurrent threads), but still the UML model writing (modification) is not thread safe.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="484fea0b-5751-4dfb-9861-690ecfc262e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="830e6398-0ff5-46dd-b2fb-15df811ffd40" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Running action with progress" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.SymbolDrawNotification* notifies when the presentation element is drawn (added to a diagram):

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.SymbolDrawNotification</em> notifies when the presentation element is drawn (added to a diagram):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="efd26006-93c1-44dc-b100-15ef466ace73"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // An element removal listener
    final PropertyChangeListener removeListener = new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        { 
            if (ExtendedPropertyNames.REMOVE.equals(evt.getPropertyName()))
            {
                // symbol removed
            }
        }
    };
 
    // An element drawing listener
    SymbolDrawListener symbolDrawListener = new SymbolDrawListener()
    {
        public void symbolAdded(DiagramPresentationElement diagram, PresentationElement symbol, String actionID)
        {
            // A symbol added
            // A register listener
            symbol.addPropertyChangeListener(removeListener);
        }
    };
    
    // A register draw listener
    SymbolDrawNotification symbolDrawNotification =SymbolDrawNotification.getSymbolDrawNotification(project);
    symbolDrawNotification.addSymbolDrawListener(symbolDrawListener);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437185 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00423: (2026x) OAuth 2.0 authentication

- page_id: `254437185`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437185/2026x+OAuth+2.0+authentication
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:10.522+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x) REST APIs > (2026x) Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

Other web applications can integrate with Authentication server through OAuth 2.0 protocol to authenticate users and access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of those users.

Authentication server behaves as OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server. Web applications that need access to Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of an authenticated user should implement OAuth 2.0 Client functionality according to OAuth 2.0 protocol specification.

### OAuth 2.0 integration

OAuth 2.0 specification

Authentication server provides a JSON with all endpoints required for the integration. This JSON can be retrieved using *https://<server_host>:8443/authentication/.well-known/oauth2.0-configuration* endpoint. This JSON also returns supported response types and grant types.

Example JSON:

```bash

```

Authentication server supports the following authorization grants:

- Authorization code
- Implicit
- Resource Owner Password Credentials
- Device Code (OAuth 2.0 extension, see OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant for more information)

### OAuth 2.0 client registration

To register OAuth 2.0 clients:

1. 
  1. **Use the web UI:** go to the **Settings**app of Web Application Platform and search for **OAuth 2.0**tab in the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS']**OAuth clients******section.
  2. **Use *registration_endpoint* API:** (see the example JSON). 
Example client registration request when using API:

```bash

```

Limitations of the client registration endpoint:

- You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.
- Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.

However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS']******section of the **Settings**app (i.e. using the web UI).

### Teamwork Cloud REST API access

After the OAuth 2.0 client gets an access token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:

```text
]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4deecac1-fe49-4c9f-84b0-27c064f2168c"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>Other web applications can integrate with Authentication server through OAuth 2.0 protocol to authenticate users and access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of those users.</span></p><p><span>Authentication server behaves as OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server. Web applications that need access to Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of an authenticated user should implement OAuth 2.0 Client functionality according to OAuth 2.0 protocol specification.</span></p><h1><span style="font-size: 24.0px;letter-spacing: -0.01em;">OAuth 2.0 integration</span></h1><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7784a74b-75b6-4997-9a8b-3c40f14c492f"><ac:rich-text-body>See <a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749">OAuth 2.0 specification</a> for more details.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Authentication server provides a JSON with all endpoints required for the integration. This JSON can be retrieved using <em>https://&lt;server_host&gt;:8443/authentication/.well-known/oauth2.0-configuration</em> endpoint. This JSON also returns supported response types and grant types.</span></p><p>Example JSON:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf15d67d-779a-4d39-8131-3351b1520d4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{

  "response_types_supported": [

    "token",

    "code"

  ],

  "device_authorization_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/device_code",

  "jwks_uri": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/jwks.json",

  "grant_types_supported": [

    "urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code",

    "refresh_token",

    "password",

    "authorization_code"

  ],

  "registration_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/register",

  "token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported": [

    "client_secret_basic"

  ],

  "scopes_supported": [],

  "issuer": "https://localhost:8443/authentication",

  "authorization_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/oauth2/authorize",

  "token_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/token"

}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span>Authentication server supports the following authorization grants:</span></p><ul><li><span>Authorization code </span></li><li><span>Implicit</span></li><li><span>Resource Owner Password Credentials</span></li><li><span>Device Code (OAuth 2.0 extension, see </span><a href="https://oauth.net/2/grant-types/device-code/">OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant</a><span> for more information)</span></li></ul><p><span> </span></p><h1>OAuth 2.0 client registration</h1><p><span>To register OAuth 2.0 clients:</span></p><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ol><li><span><strong>Use the web UI:</strong> go to the <strong>Settings </strong>app of Web Application Platform and search for <strong>OAuth 2.0 </strong>tab in the<em> </em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:link-body><strong>OAuth clients</strong><strong> </strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>section.</span></li><li><span><strong>Use <em>registration_endpoint</em> API:</strong> (see the example JSON).<br />Example client registration request when using API:</span></li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c55467c-6f7f-40d2-b3d7-f9cb12e1a9ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[POST https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/register

BODY:

{

           client_name: “Client name”,

           redirect_uris: [“redirect uri”] // comma separated URIs

}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e29a054-c385-40f2-8c3d-5088c5c7cd68"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Limitations of the client registration endpoint:</p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.</span></li></ul><p>However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OAuth clients]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><strong> </strong>section of the <strong>Settings </strong>app (i.e. using the web UI).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1><span style="font-size: 24.0px;letter-spacing: -0.01em;">Teamwork Cloud REST API access</span></h1><p><span>After the OAuth 2.0 client gets an access token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f58c24d-34d3-4d65-9691-930fbebf89d4"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Authorization: Bearer <access token>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437594 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00424: (2026x) Open API Deprecation Policy

- page_id: `254437594`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437594/2026x+Open+API+Deprecation+Policy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:47.562+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Overview:** This policy outlines the procedures and guidelines for the deprecation of APIs within our system.

**Deprecation Process:**

1. **Notification:** APIs that are deprecated will be clearly marked as such in the API javadoc.
2. **Timeline:** Deprecated APIs can be removed in the next major version following their deprecation. For instance, if an API is marked as deprecated in version 2024x (or any 2024x RefreshX), it can be removed in version 2026x.
3. **Replacement APIs:** Deprecated APIs will have replacement APIs provided by the development team. Users are encouraged to migrate to the replacement APIs to ensure continued compatibility and support.

**Exceptional Cases:** In exceptional cases during major releases, Open APIs may be removed without prior deprecation. This may occur due to significant changes in data structures or profile modifications. While such instances are rare, users should be aware that, in exceptional cases, Open APIs may be subject to removal without prior notice.

**Review:** This policy will be periodically reviewed and updated to align with evolving technological and business requirements. Any changes to the policy will be communicated to relevant stakeholders in a timely manner.

**Effective Version:** This API deprecation policy is effective as of version 2024x Refresh1.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Overview:</strong> This policy outlines the procedures and guidelines for the deprecation of APIs within our system.</p><p><strong>Deprecation Process:</strong></p><ol><li><p><strong>Notification:</strong> APIs that are deprecated will be clearly <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="40a81d41-feed-4dc8-918b-8ac1e40460c9">marked as such <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">in the API javadoc</span></ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p></li><li><p><strong>Timeline:</strong> Deprecated APIs can be removed in the next major version following their deprecation. For instance, if an API is marked as deprecated <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="833d9cd5-af83-4d17-a89f-47dc8eb0c173">in version 2024x <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(or any 2024x RefreshX)</span>, it can be removed in version 2026x.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li><li><p><strong>Replacement APIs:</strong> Deprecated APIs will have replacement APIs provided by the development team. Users are encouraged to migrate to the replacement APIs to ensure continued compatibility and support.</p></li></ol><p><strong>Exceptional Cases:</strong> In exceptional cases during major releases, Open APIs may be removed without prior deprecation. This may occur due to significant changes in data structures or profile modifications. While such instances are rare, users should be aware that, in exceptional cases, Open APIs may be subject to removal without prior notice.</p><p><strong>Review:</strong> This policy will be periodically reviewed and updated to align with evolving technological and business requirements. Any changes to the policy will be communicated to relevant stakeholders in a timely manner.</p><p><strong>Effective Version:</strong> This API deprecation policy is effective as of version 2024x Refresh1.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437191 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00425: (2026x) OpenID Connect authentication

- page_id: `254437191`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437191/2026x+OpenID+Connect+authentication
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:10.863+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x) REST APIs > (2026x) Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

Other web applications can integrate with Authentication server through OpenID Connect (further referred to as OIDC) protocol to authenticate users and access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of these users.

Authentication server behaves as the OIDC Identity Provider. Web applications that need to access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of an authenticated user should implement OIDC Service Provider functionality according to OIDC protocol specification.

### OpenID Connect integration

OpenID Connect specification

Authentication server provides a JSON with all endpoints required for the integration. This JSON can be retrieved using *https://<server_host>:8443/authentication/.well-known/oidc-configuration* endpoint. This JSON also returns supported response types and grant types.

Example JSON:

```bash

```

Authentication server supports the following authorization flows:

- Authorization code flow
- Implicit flow
- Hybrid flow
- Device code flow (extension, see [Device Code Flow - Authorization](https://developers.onelogin.com/openid-connect/api/device-code-auth) for more information)

### OIDC client registration

To register OIDC clients:

1. 
  1. **Use the web UI:** go to the **Settings**app of Web Application Platform and search for **OpenID Connect**tab in the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS']**OAuth clients******section.
  2. **Use *registration_endpoint* API:** (see the example JSON). Example client registration request when using API:

```bash

```

Limitations of the client registration endpoint:

- You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.
- Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.

However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS']******section of the **Settings**app (i.e. using the web UI).

### Teamwork Cloud REST API access

After the OIDC client gets ID token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:

```bash
]]>
```

BODY:

{

           client_name: “Client name”,

           redirect_uris: [“redirect uri”] // comma separated URIs

}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1fe551d-0a65-4ad7-9275-0e44721b67a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Limitations of the client registration endpoint:</p><ul><li><span>You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.</span></li><li><span>Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.</span></li></ul><p>However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OAuth clients]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><strong> </strong>section of the <strong>Settings </strong>app (i.e. using the web UI).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h1><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Teamwork Cloud REST API access</span></h1><p><span>After the OIDC client gets ID token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d73af696-184e-4ad8-ade6-123651fb8611"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Authorization: Token <ID token>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
