<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437303 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00426: (2026x) Oracle DDL generation and customization

- page_id: `254437303`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437303/2026x+Oracle+DDL+generation+and+customization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:19.901+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Oracle DDL script generation is based on the Velocity engine. It provides an ability to change the generated DDL script by changing the velocity template. We explain how the Oracle DDL generation works in our modeling tools and how to change the template for specific elements.

Knowledge of the Velocity Template Language is necessary for understanding, editing, or creating templates. The Velocity documentation can be downloaded from [http://click.apache.org/docs/velocity/VelocityUsersGuide.pdf](http://click.apache.org/docs/velocity/VelocityUsersGuide.pdf).

For more information about Oracle DDL 11g, see [http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E25054_01/server.1111/e25789/sqllangu.htm](http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E25054_01/server.1111/e25789/sqllangu.htm).

The modeling tool Oracle DDL generation consists of the following three components:

- The Velocity engine. Oracle DDL generation is performed by the Velocity engine. The engine collects context variables and mergers them into a template.
- The template. The template is a user-defined document that provides the Velocity Template Language (VTL) syntax. The VTL syntax is used to manipulate the context variables for generating the text script. The template file can be changed in the CG Properties Editor dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the Properties command). The default Oracle DDL template is stored in < program installation folder>\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template folder.
- Context variables. Context variables are models (retrieved from OpenAPI), code engineering set information, and user-defined variables.

To identify the Oracle object, check applied Stereotypes and Tagged Values.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It provides an ability to change the generated DDL script by changing the velocity template. We explain how the Oracle DDL generation works in our modeling tools and how to change the template for specific elements.</p><p>Knowledge of the Velocity Template Language is necessary for understanding, editing, or creating templates. Oracle DDL generation is performed by the Velocity engine. The engine collects context variables and mergers them into a template.</li><li>The template. The template is a user-defined document that provides the Velocity Template Language (VTL) syntax. The VTL syntax is used to manipulate the context variables for generating the text script. The template file can be changed in the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">CG Properties Editor </strong>dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Properties </strong>command). The default Oracle DDL template is stored in &lt;<em>program </em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">installation folder&gt;\</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template </em>folder.</li><li>Context variables. Context variables are models (retrieved from OpenAPI), code engineering set information, and user-defined variables.</li></ul><p>To identify the Oracle object, check applied Stereotypes and Tagged Values.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25b2885a-064a-4fc0-bac0-35a022134e4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="03a84b5e-cf4a-4758-b490-c4d3fa411bf6"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437520 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00427: (2026x) OSLC API

- page_id: `254437520`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437520/2026x+OSLC+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:39.514+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Since version 2022x Refresh2, Teamwork Cloud OSLC API implementation has been moved to the Web Application Platform, resulting in a change of port and API URI patterns:

- OSLC API no longer uses port 8111 but relies on port 8443, as well as other Web Application Platform applications.
- OSLC root services document URI can be found using the following pattern - http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/rootservices .
- Each of the model elements is exposed using the following URI pattern - http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/oslc/am/{projectID}/{elementID} . Note that the elementID here is an Element Server ID.

**Note regarding migration**

The old pre-2022x Refresh2 root services URI and element URIs will be automatically redirected to the new URIs via HTTP 301 redirect. After migrating to 2022x Refresh2, you must set the WAP URI value in the *server*field of the Teamwork Cloud *esi.oslc* configuration block in the *application.conf* file.

Teamwork Cloud has OSLC support and exposes model element data following [OSLC Architecture Management (AM)](http://open-services.net/ns/am) and [OSLC Configuration Management](https://archive.open-services.net/wiki/configuration-management/index.html)vocabularies. Along with core OSLC services provided in Teamwork Cloud, this enables smooth integration with other OSLC-compatible tools by linking resources in Linked Data fashion.

We expose the following model element properties:

- rdf:type ( http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type ) - represents architecture resource type, which according to OSLC AM vocabulary is always a Resource ( http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource ).
- dcterms:modified ( http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified ) - stands for the last element modification date.
- dcterms:identifier ( http://purl.org/dc/terms/identifier ) - the Element Server ID as used in element's URI pattern.
- dcterms:title ( http://purl.org/dc/terms/title ) - the name of the element

```xml
May 16, 2018 2:08:52 PM
    50775427-bce2-4de4-b747-8ab82d294237
    Engine
  
]]>
```

- Currently, querying services are not supported.
- Model data can only be read through OSLC services, and editing capabilities are not supported.
- We offer OSLC UI previews through integration with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud' space='MCS'] . For this, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing from graphical user interface' space='MCS'] .
- We provide OSLC delegated dialog support via Selection Dialog service for navigating the model tree and linking to AM resources from external tools.

##### OSLC Configuration Management Support

OSLC Configuration Management support makes Teamwork Cloud OSLC services configuration-aware. This enables Teamwork Cloud resource discovery at the branch or tagged commit levels. The entry point for discovery of OSLC Configuration Management services is via the *<oslc_config:cmServiceProviders>* property. Teamwork Cloud concepts map to OSLC Configuration Management concepts in the following way:

| Teamwork Cloud concept | OSLC Configuration Management concept |
| --- | --- |
| Cameo Model (.Master Resource) | Component |
| Branch | Stream |
| Tagged Commit | Baseline |
| Used Project (Branch) | Contribution of a Stream to a Stream |

##### **OAuth 1.0a authentication**

To ensure secure access to server resources via OSLC, OAuth 1.0a authentication protocol is used.

OAuth 1.0a requires consumer key and secret to be known before starting the authentication process flow. These can be created through Teamwork CloudAdmin as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS'].

Alternatively, a pair of consumer key and secret can be generated manually via a service exposed in the root services document. The following HTTP POST request should be made to a consumer key generation service (jfs:oauthRequestConsumerKeyUrl):

```js

```

```js

```

Note, that keys generated following this approach will still need to be[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS']approved by an administrator through Teamwork CloudAdmin.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29e5060b-9718-47f8-8cd7-1427d5129831"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Since version 2022x Refresh2, Teamwork Cloud OSLC API implementation has been moved to the Web Application Platform, resulting in a change of port and API URI patterns:</span></p><ul><li>OSLC API no longer uses port 8111 but relies on port 8443, as well as other Web Application Platform applications.</li><li>OSLC root services document URI can be found using the following pattern - <em>http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/rootservices</em>.</li><li>Each of the model elements is exposed using the following URI pattern - <em>http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/oslc/am/{projectID}/{elementID}</em>.<br />Note that the <em>elementID</em> here is an Element Server ID.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Note regarding migration</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The old pre-2022x Refresh2 root services URI and element URIs will be automatically redirected to the new URIs via HTTP 301 redirect. After migrating to 2022x Refresh2, you must set the WAP URI value in the <em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f3ee46c6-ba1f-49b4-957f-b64ea1c9fea5">server</ac:inline-comment-marker> </em>field of the Teamwork Cloud <em>esi.oslc</em> configuration block in the <em>application.conf</em> file.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1fc8fdab-1c2c-4ca3-9015-33b7f61ad136">Teamwork</ac:inline-comment-marker> Cloud has OSLC support and exposes model element data following <a href="http://open-services.net/ns/am">OSLC Architecture Management (AM)</a> and <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><a href="https://archive.open-services.net/wiki/configuration-management/index.html">OSLC Configuration Management</a><span> </span></span>vocabularies. Along with core OSLC services provided in Teamwork Cloud, this enables smooth integration with other OSLC-compatible tools by linking resources in Linked Data fashion.</span></p><p>We expose the following model element properties:</p><ul><li>rdf:type (<a href="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type">http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type</a>) - represents architecture resource type, which according to OSLC AM vocabulary is always a Resource (<a href="http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource">http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource</a>).</li><li>dcterms:modified (<a href="http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified">http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified</a>) - stands for the last element modification date.</li><li>dcterms:identifier (<a href="http://purl.org/dc/terms/identifier">http://purl.org/dc/terms/identifier</a>) - the Element Server ID as used in element's URI pattern.</li><li>dcterms:title (<a href="http://purl.org/dc/terms/title">http://purl.org/dc/terms/title</a>) - the name of the element<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f154bf0-5a5c-4311-9f23-091bc174fbe7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample RDF/XML representation of element data</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<rdf:RDF
    xmlns:oslc="http://open-services.net/ns/core#"    
    xmlns:oslc_am="http://open-services.net/ns/am#" 
    xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/terms/"
    xmlns:dcterms="http://purl.org/dc/terms/"  
    xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
    xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#">
  <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://localhost:8443/oslc/api/oslc/am/b1acff2d-4396-4314-9dba-477d573ede16/50775427-bce2-4de4-b747-8ab82d294237">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource"/>
    <oslc:serviceProvider rdf:resource="https://localhost:8443/oslc/api/oslc/am/b1acff2d-4396-4314-9dba-477d573ede16/services"/>
    <dcterms:modified rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime">May 16, 2018 2:08:52 PM</dcterms:modified>
    <dcterms:identifier rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string">50775427-bce2-4de4-b747-8ab82d294237</dcterms:identifier>
    <dcterms:title rdf:parseType="Literal">Engine</dcterms:title>
  </rdf:Description>
</rdf:RDF>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ul><li><p>Currently, querying services are not supported.</p></li><li><p>Model data can only be read through OSLC services, and editing capabilities are not supported.</p></li><li>We offer OSLC UI previews through integration with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /></ac:link>. For this, you need to <ac:link ac:anchor="Publishing other types of resources"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Publishing from graphical user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[publish an OSLC resource]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We provide OSLC delegated dialog support via Selection Dialog service for navigating the model tree and linking to AM resources from external tools.</span></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">OSLC <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Configuration Management Support</span></span></h3><p>OSLC Configuration Management support makes Teamwork Cloud OSLC services configuration-aware. This enables Teamwork Cloud resource discovery at the branch or tagged commit levels. The entry point for discovery of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bb2c04dc-ef20-41c5-9764-299b17fab63d">OSLC Configuration Management services</ac:inline-comment-marker> is via the <em>&lt;oslc_config:cmServiceProviders&gt;</em> property. Teamwork Cloud concepts map to OSLC Configuration Management concepts in the following way:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 47.1682%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 46.5384%;" /><col style="width: 53.5495%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;">Teamwork Cloud concept</th><th style="text-align: center;">OSLC Configuration Management concept</th></tr><tr><td>Cameo Model (.Master Resource)</td><td>Component</td></tr><tr><td>Branch</td><td>Stream</td></tr><tr><td>Tagged Commit</td><td>Baseline</td></tr><tr><td>Used Project (Branch)</td><td>Contribution of a Stream to a Stream</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4d1be4db-c9ac-49ef-8221-e21e5639b4b7">OAuth 1.0a authentication</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><br /></strong></h3><p>To ensure secure access to server resources via OSLC, OAuth 1.0a authentication protocol is used.</p><p>OAuth 1.0a requires consumer key and secret to be known before starting the authentication process flow. These can be created through <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Teamwork Cloud </span>Admin as described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Alternatively, a pair of consumer key and secret can be generated manually via a service exposed in the root services document. The following HTTP POST request should be made to a consumer key generation service (jfs:oauthRequestConsumerKeyUrl):<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81dd2474-b1c0-4b12-9130-db9c5401d9dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">js</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
  "name": "consumerNameGoesHere",
  "secret": "OAuthSecretGoesHere"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a2e2db9-97a7-4b9d-98f6-073474ff885b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">js</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
  "key": "generatedConsumerKeyShouldBeHere"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Note, that keys generated following this approach will <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bd78a089-e091-4aa8-8802-67266a59924c">still need to be </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:link-body><span class="inline-comment-marker">approved</span> by an administrator</ac:link-body></ac:link> through <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Teamwork Cloud </span>Admin.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437382 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00428: (2026x) Parametric Helper as executing parametric simulation from an Activity

- page_id: `254437382`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437382/2026x+Parametric+Helper+as+executing+parametric+simulation+from+an+Activity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:24.641+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > (2026x) Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Parametric engine provides an API for a parametric execution with a runtime object of a Classifier. The runtime object of the Classifier will be passed to the API as an argument and the engine will execute the given object. With this API, you can use a scripting language to execute the parametric simulation, shown below:

```text

```

An argument object is a runtime object of a classifier to be executed. To obtain this particular runtime object, you can use some UML actions, e.g., ReadSelfAction, ReadStructuralFeatureValueAction, ValueSpecificationAction, or the Cameo Simulation Toolkit Open API. The following figure shows the Parametric Activity diagram in the *CylinderPipe.mdzip* sample. The **action : ExecuteParametric** is used to run the parametric execution. The runtime object, which will be executed, is obtained from the value Specification Action rawMaterialSupplier.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Parametric Activity diagram in the CylinderPipe.mdzip sample.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Parametric engine provides an API for a parametric execution with a runtime object of a Classifier. The runtime object of the Classifier will be passed to the API as an argument and the engine will execute the given object. With this API, you can use a scripting language to execute the parametric simulation, shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149034b5-c6af-4ed6-a2ae-39a65ae247a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.parametrics.utility.ParametricsHelper.executeObject(Object object);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>An argument object is a runtime object of a classifier to be executed. To obtain this particular runtime object, you can use some UML actions, e.g., ReadSelfAction, ReadStructuralFeatureValueAction, ValueSpecificationAction, or the Cameo Simulation Toolkit Open API. The following figure shows the Parametric Activity diagram in the <em>CylinderPipe.mdzip</em> sample. The <strong>action : ExecuteParametric</strong> is used to run the parametric execution. The runtime object, which will be executed, is obtained from the value Specification Action rawMaterialSupplier.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="567" ac:width="594"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavfe88aed5c24cf68dcde9f46c1f9f6e1a.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Parametric Helper as executing parametric simulation from an Activity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"> The Parametric Activity diagram in the CylinderPipe.mdzip sample.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437636 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00429: (2026x) Paths

- page_id: `254437636`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437636/2026x+Paths
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:49.262+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols)
- labels: ['supplier', 'client', 'suplier-piont', 'client-point', 'break-point']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

706205016

INLINE

706205019

INLINE

706205017

INLINE

Paths are presentation elements created for such model elements as Relationships. Path has the following attributes:

- Supplier – the presentation element of a Relationship supplier. Use method com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathElement.getSupplier() to access this element.
- Client – the presentation element of a Relationship client. Use method PathElement.getClient() to access this element.
- Supplier point – the connection point of a path and supplier element. Use method PathElement.getSupplierPoint() to access this point.
- Client point – the connection point of a path and client element. Use method PathElement.getClientPoint() to access this point.
- Break points – a list of path breaking points between supplier and client points. This list can be empty if path is straight. Use method PathElement.getBreakPoints() to access this list.

706205015

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with paths and relationships' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437179 space=DEVG version=3 -->
## PAGE 00430: (2026x) Personal access token authentication sample for Teamwork Cloud REST API

- page_id: `254437179`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437179/2026x+Personal+access+token+authentication+sample+for+Teamwork+Cloud+REST+API
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-22T12:18:13.026+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x) REST APIs > (2026x) Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To use Teamwork Cloud REST API withpersonal access token authentication

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating a personal access token' space='MCS'].
2. Copy the token and use it in the HTTP header attribute to perform REST API calls. The token is used in an Authorization header with the **Token** Type.

```text

```

#### NOTE: Token expiration and validation

Token expiration and validation

The personal access token does not have an expiration date, so use it with caution and ensure it is adequately protected.

To invalidate the personal access token, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating a personal access token' space='MCS'].

The personal access token should be regenerated after the SSL certificate is updated for Web Application Platform. This is because the authentication server uses the certificate for personal access token signing and later for token signature validation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To use Teamwork Cloud REST API with </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">personal access token authentication</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Generating a personal access token" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generate your personal access token]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Copy the token and use it in the HTTP header attribute to perform REST API calls.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11e1ad8f-eb11-44ad-a098-14fd04c40a9c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The token is used in an Authorization header with the <strong>Token</strong> Type.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3181bca5-20bc-424d-8575-3ad81b1a8a1c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP header attribute example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Authorization: Token
eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJBZG1pbmlzdHJhdG9yIiwiYXVkIjoidHdjLXJlc3QtYXBp
IiwiaXNzIjoiaHR0cHM6XC9cLzEyNy4wLjAuMTo4NTU1XC9hdXRoZW50aWNhdGlvbiIsImV4cCI6M
TYwNjI5OTc3NywiaWF0IjoxNjA2Mjk4ODc3fQ.bA-
S5hHeSlV8AFoQVzzfIseC3qlmqQoBQREiapHN6I5CcvwetKdSVztWKkssSGjm31Y1zqoULio7_1Ma
mtGBbbzvA1WWQYFRiYk0D612yNDv4uNHBbNLNEv61TYNLwdPwPh0atVRehkh-
LSgjipXTvXj4mZViE0NHKIG9U7htA9Zzvxvck2JDXe_eU2-
4TCNm8II89ROaEb1tZ5nD84ieRbzJWqrcVTdqU2YfbIUeew5Nir8obkLYgixBXFKWsTHi3jNuoBx3
KcAIyZqL6cjtsCER4wbk4PEEDC57UVsOcsXWr6yvXIoVdJMOiDHo_fJMkgOjDqSyIL-2B21O-Y-GA]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="131736ff-f46e-45c5-8264-10277bdcfc23"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Token expiration and validation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The personal access token does not have an expiration date, so use it with caution and ensure it is adequately protected.</p><p>To invalidate the personal access token, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Generating a personal access token" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[delete it in the My account application]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4db7d2f8-c925-4d68-881c-9d7c28ff0943"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The personal access token should be regenerated after the SSL certificate is updated for Web Application Platform. This is because the authentication server uses the certificate for personal access token signing and later for token signature validation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437433 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00431: (2026x) Plugin class loading

- page_id: `254437433`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437433/2026x+Plugin+class+loading
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:30.144+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins > (2026x) Detail information
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All modeling tool plugins (classes and runtime libraries) are loaded by the **one** classloader. If there are plugins that cannot be loaded by the same classloader (for example, conflicts appear in versions of plugin libraries or other), their descriptors should be defined to use own classloaders. In this case, the plugin classes are loaded by a separate classloader.

An optional property "class-lookup" controls how classes are loaded, if the plugin has its own classloader. If the value of this property is *LocalFirst*, the class is loaded from the plugin classpath even if such class is already loaded in the global core classloader of your modeling tool. This property is important if you want to use different versions of the same classes (libraries) used in the program core.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>All modeling tool plugins (classes and runtime libraries) are loaded by the <strong>one</strong> classloader. If there are plugins that cannot be loaded by the same classloader (for example, conflicts appear in versions of plugin libraries or other), their descriptors should be defined to use own classloaders. In this case, the plugin classes are loaded by a separate classloader.</p><p>An optional property &quot;class-lookup&quot; controls how classes are loaded, if the plugin has its own classloader. If the value of this property is <em>LocalFirst</em>, the class is loaded from the plugin classpath even if such class is already loaded in the global core classloader of your modeling tool. This property is important if you want to use different versions of the same classes (libraries) used in the program core.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437435 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00432: (2026x) Plugin classes

- page_id: `254437435`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437435/2026x+Plugin+classes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:30.311+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins > (2026x) Detail information
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin* is the base abstract class for any modeling tool plugin. A plugin written by a user must be extended from this class. Every plugin has its own descriptor set by a plugin's manager. A plugin has three special methods:

- a *init()* meth od is called on a program startup. The plugin must override this method and implement its own functionality there.
- a *close()* method is called on a program exit. The plugin must override this method and return the value true if the plugin is ready to exit. In other case, it should return the value false . If the plugin returns false , the program exit is canceled.
- a *isSupported()* method is called before the plugin initialization. If this method returns false , the plugin is not initialized. isSupported() may be used to check if the plugin can be started, for example, on this OS.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.PluginUtils* to get references to loaded plugins. This is useful if you have dependencies among plugins and want to get a reference to other plugin object.

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.PluginDescriptor* is the class that provides information loaded from the *plugin.xml* file (a plugin descriptor) to the plugin. Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin.getDescriptor() to get the descriptor.*

More information is available in javadoc.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin</em> is the base abstract class for any modeling tool plugin. A plugin written by a user must be extended from this class. Every plugin has its own descriptor set by a plugin's manager. A plugin has three special methods:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">a <em>init()</em> </span> meth</span>od is called on a program startup. The plugin must override this method and implement its own functionality there.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a </span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <em>close()</em> </span> method is called on a program exit. The plugin must override this method and return the value <em>true</em> if the plugin is ready to exit. In other case, it should return the value <em>false</em>. If the plugin returns <em>false</em>, the program exit is canceled.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a </span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <em>isSupported()</em> </span> method is called before the plugin initialization. If this method returns <em>false</em>, the plugin is not initialized. <em>isSupported()</em> may be used to check if the plugin can be started, for example, on this OS.</li></ul><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.PluginUtils</em> to get references to loaded plugins. This is useful if you have dependencies among plugins and want to get a reference to other plugin object.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline _mce_tagged_br" /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.PluginDescriptor </em> is</span> the class that provides information loaded from the <em>plugin.xml</em> file (a plugin descriptor) to the plugin. Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin.getDescriptor() to get the descriptor.</em></p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Plugins manager workflow" ac:alt="Plugins manager workflow"><ri:attachment ri:filename="plugins_manager_workflow.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Plugin classes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437417 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00433: (2026x) Plugin descriptor

- page_id: `254437417`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437417/2026x+Plugin+descriptor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:27.824+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins > (2026x) Detail information
- labels: ['plugin-descriptor']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A plugin descriptor is a file written in XML and named *plugin.xml*. Each descriptor contains properties of the one plugin. The descriptor file should contain the definitions of elements of the one plugin.

The *plugin.xml* file consists of the following elements:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugin descriptor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugin descriptor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugin descriptor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugin descriptor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugin descriptor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugin descriptor' space='']

The following tables describe them in more details.

plugin**plugin**

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | A plugin ID should be unique. It is used to identify a plugin by a plugin's manager internals and by requirements of other plugins. For example, my.first.plugin.0. |
| name | A plugin name. There is no strict rules applied to this attribute. For example, Example plugin. |
| version | A plugin version. The version can be used to check other plugins dependencies, if internalVersion is not defined. |
| internalVersion | A plugin internal version. It is used to check other plugins dependencies. |
| provider-name | A plugin provider name, which is a company or an author name. For example: No Magic. |
| class | A full qualified class name. The class must be derived from com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins. A plugin and stored in plugin runtime library. This class will be loaded and initialized by the plugin's manager. For example, myplugin.MyPlugin. |
| ownClassloader | Optional; the default value is false. Indicates, if to use an own plugin's classloader (separate from other plugins). All program plugins are loaded by the one classloader. If there are plugins that cannot be loaded by the same classloader (for example, because of conflicts in plugin libraries versions or other), their descriptors must be defined to use own class-loaders. |
| class-lookup | Optional; possible values are LocalFirst, GlobalFirst, the default value is GlobalFirst. Specifies the priority of a "parent" class loader, if a plugin uses ownClassloader. LocalFirst forces to load classes from the plugin class loader even if such classes exist in the modeling tool core class path. This option should be used, if, in your plugin, you want to use different versions of same libraries that are used in the core. |

**Nested elements**

| Element name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| requires | A modeling tool API version required by the plugin. Plugins and their versions required by the plugin. |
| runtime | Runtime libraries needed by a plugin. |

**requiresrequires** 
 **Nested elements**

| Element name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| api | A required modeling tool API version. |
| required-plugin | Plugin(s) that are required to run the plugin. |

**apiapi**

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| version | A required modeling tool API version. For example, 1.0. |

**required_pluginrequired-plugin**

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | The ID of a required plugin. For example, my.first.plugin.0. |
| name | A name of a required plugin. |
| internalVersion | An internal version of a required plugin. If it is not defined, the "version" is used to check if the required plugin is suitable. |
| version | A version of a required plugin. If it is not defined, any required plugin version is suitable. For example: 1.1. |
| optional | The default value of this attribute is false.If value is false (or attribute is skipped), the plugin will be loaded only if the required plugin is loaded.If value is true, the plugin will be loaded if the required plugin is loaded or required plugin is not installed. |

**runtimeruntime**

**Nested elements**

| Element name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| library | A runtime library for a running plugin. |

**librarylibrary**

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | A name (path) of the required library. The path is relative to the plugin descriptor directory (slash should be used to separate files in path).For example, "pluginlib.jar", "lib/otherlib.jar". |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A plugin descriptor is a file written in XML and named <em>plugin.xml</em>. Each descriptor contains properties of the one plugin. The descriptor file should contain the definitions of elements of the one plugin. </p><p>The <em>plugin.xml</em> file consists of the following elements:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="plugin"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="requires"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="api"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="required_plugin"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[required-plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="runtime"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="library"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li></ul><p>The following tables describe them in more details.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e79d307f-79c3-48e5-9673-99b09ef7bd83"><ac:parameter ac:name="">plugin</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>plugin</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Description</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><p>A plugin ID should be unique. It is used to identify a plugin by a plugin's manager internals and by requirements of other plugins. For example, <em>my.first.plugin.0</em>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>name</p></td><td><p>A plugin name. There is no strict rules applied to this attribute. For example, <em>Example plugin</em>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><p>A plugin version. The version can be used to check other plugins dependencies, if internalVersion is not defined.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>internalVersion</p></td><td><p>A plugin internal version. It is used to check other plugins dependencies.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>provider-name</p></td><td><p>A plugin provider name, which is a company or an author name. For example: <em>No Magic</em>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>class</p></td><td><p>A full qualified class name. The class must be derived from <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins</em>. A plugin and stored in plugin runtime library. This class will be loaded and initialized by the plugin's manager. For example, <em>myplugin.MyPlugin</em>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ownClassloader</p></td><td><p>Optional; the default value is <em>false</em>. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />Indicates, if to use an own plugin's classloader (separate from other plugins). All program plugins are loaded by the <strong>one</strong> classloader. If there are plugins that cannot be loaded by the same classloader (for example, because of conflicts in plugin libraries versions or other), their descriptors must be defined to use own class-loaders.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>class-lookup</p></td><td><p>Optional; possible values are <em>LocalFirst</em>, <em>GlobalFirst</em>, the default value is <em>GlobalFirst</em>. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />Specifies the priority of a &quot;parent&quot; class loader, if a plugin uses ownClassloader. LocalFirst forces to load classes from the plugin class loader even if such classes exist in the modeling tool core class path. This option should be used, if, in your plugin, you want to use different versions of same libraries that are used in the core.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Nested elements</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Element name</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Description</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">requires</span></p></td><td><p>A modeling tool API version required by the plugin. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437392 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00434: (2026x) Plugins

- page_id: `254437392`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437392/2026x+Plugins
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:25.446+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['plugin', 'plug-in']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Plugins are the only one way to change the functionality of a modeling tool. The main purpose of the plugin architecture is to add a new functionality to your modeling tool. Although there is a limited ability to remove an existing functionality using plugins. 
 A plugin must contain the following resources:

- A directory

- Compiled java files, packaged into a jar file

- A plugin descriptor file

- Optional files used by the plugin

Typically a plugin creates some GUI components allowing a user to use the plugin functionality. Generally, this is not necessary because the plugin can listen for some changes in a project and activate itself on the desired changes.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Plugins are the only one way to change the functionality of a modeling tool. The main purpose of the plugin architecture is to add a new functionality to your modeling tool. Although there is a limited ability to remove an existing functionality using plugins. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> A plugin must contain the following resources:</p><ul><li>A directory</li></ul><ul><li>Compiled java files, packaged into a jar file</li></ul><ul><li>A plugin descriptor file</li></ul><ul><li>Optional files used by the plugin</li></ul><p>Typically a plugin creates some GUI components allowing a user to use the plugin functionality. Generally, this is not necessary because the plugin can listen for some changes in a project and activate itself on the desired changes.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cafabac-b175-4310-8c9b-9d91fca265fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="aeea49da-d2c5-448f-972b-cc3702986582"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437396 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00435: (2026x) Plugins directories

- page_id: `254437396`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437396/2026x+Plugins+directories
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:25.629+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins > (2026x) Detail information
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Plugins are loaded from two locations on the program startup.

##### **Installation directory**

Plugins are loaded from the program installation directory (a global plugin directory) *<modeling tool installation directory>\plugins*.

This directory can be changed using a *md.plugins.dir* java system property and specifying paths separated with a semicolon (;) symbol.

To change the location of the plugins directory

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located in <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property: -Dmd.plugins.dir="absolute path to plugins directory1;absolute path to plugins directory2" Sample property value-Dmd.plugins.dir="C:\Program Files\MagicDraw\plugins;D:\MyPlugins" If you are loading plugins from a custom location, make sure the location contains extracted plugin files. Compressed plugin files (*.zip*) cannot be loaded.Besides the plugin itself, any other files (profiles, modelLibraries, samples, etc.) contained in the plugin *.zip* file are not added to the installation directory like they are when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools and plugins' space='IL'].

##### **Configuration files location**

Additionally, plugins are loaded from a configuration files location (*<Configuration files location>\plugins*). This allows to have global and user plugins.

See “MagicDraw Configuration Files Location” in the MagicDraw UserManual.pdf.

Another issue on Unix systems is related to user permissions to write. If a modeling tool is installed in a root, user is not allowed to write in a global plugin directory if a user has not such permissions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Plugins are loaded from two locations on the program startup.</p><h3><strong>Installation directory</strong></h3><p>Plugins are loaded from the program installation directory (a global plugin directory) <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\plugins</em>.</p><p>This directory can be changed using a <em>md.plugins.dir</em> java system property and specifying paths separated with a semicolon (;) symbol.</p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To change the location of the plugins directory</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em> file, which is located in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property:</p><pre>-Dmd.plugins.dir=&quot;absolute path to plugins directory1;absolute path to plugins directory2&quot;</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce50ee8e-88d6-4372-a529-306178a57c78"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample property value</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>-Dmd.plugins.dir=&quot;C:\Program Files\MagicDraw\plugins;D:\MyPlugins&quot;</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4fd1934-8587-47f7-8473-59f432781e76"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If you are loading plugins from a custom location, make sure the location contains extracted plugin files. Compressed plugin files (<em>.zip</em>) cannot be loaded.</li><li>Besides the plugin itself, any other files (profiles, modelLibraries, samples, etc.) contained in the plugin <em>.zip</em> file are not added to the installation directory like they are when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Installing modeling tools and plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[installing a resource]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3><strong>Configuration files location<br /></strong></h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Additionally, plugins are loaded from a configuration files location (<em>&lt;Configuration files location&gt;\plugins</em>). This allows to have global and user plugins. <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2852841e-5fcc-44bf-93d4-82d06a41d199"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> See “MagicDraw Configuration Files Location” in the MagicDraw UserManual.pdf.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Another issue on Unix systems is related to user permissions to write. If a modeling tool is installed in a root, user is not allowed to write in a global plugin directory if a user has not such permissions.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437125 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00436: (2026x) Plugins migration

- page_id: `254437125`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437125/2026x+Plugins+migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:05.772+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

We are keeping Open API stable as much as possible, but in some cases API changes in a newer application version. For example this happens if support for a new UML version is added or some very serious refactoring is done in components.

Sometimes it is enough to recompile plugin code with new libraries and adjust some minor API changes. Sometimes more serious migration is needed.

We recommend to recompile a plugin with new libraries to see the incompatibilities if any.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>We are keeping Open API stable as much as possible, but in some cases API changes in a newer application version. For example this happens if support for a new UML version is added or some very serious refactoring is done in components.</p><p>Sometimes it is enough to recompile plugin code with new libraries and adjust some minor API changes. Sometimes more serious migration is needed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2de9e02f-14fe-487a-90bf-e89620726db5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>We recommend to recompile a plugin with new libraries to see the incompatibilities if any.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6effda46-6001-4f01-b36f-045a9ad9dab4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ac344481-bbee-4176-81b9-ee4c059195f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437151 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00437: (2026x) Plugins migration to MagicDraw 15.0 and later Open API

- page_id: `254437151`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437151/2026x+Plugins+migration+to+MagicDraw+15.0+and+later+Open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:09.098+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open API in version 15.0 and later versions has changed together with changes in the UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification. You should read this chapter if you want to migrate your plugin created for earlier MagicDraw version.

Thereby we made a cleaner UML metamodel implementation API after the UML specification has been updated. There are no big changes in Open API, so migration will not be a long and complicate task for you.

The changes that have been made to Open API are the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) UML metamodel changes' space=''] .
- Some deprecated methods have been removed:

| Removed deprecated method | Substitution |
| --- | --- |
| BaseElement.getProject() | Project.getProject(BaseElement) |
| MainFrame.getDialogParent() | MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent() |
| MainFrame.setDialogParent(Frame) | MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().setDialogParent(Frame) |
| ElementListProperty.setStereotype(Stereotype) | ElementListProperty.setSelectableRestrictedElements(Collection) |

- Changes in libraries jars have been made. Open API classes now are packaged into md_api.jar and md_common_api.jar (was md.jar and md_common.jar ). If your plugin was using API classes from some program build-in plugin, a plugin jar is also renamed with a “ ProgramInitials_api.jar ” pattern.
- Changes in package names for build-in plugins have been made. In earlier program versions, some build-in plugins exposed their Open API, for example, patterns, transformations, emf, and etc. We have changed a package name pattern. Earlier API versions used a com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl. **** pattern, now these plugins are moved to com.nomagic.magicdraw.*.*, for example com.nomagic.magicdraw.patterns.* . A simple import statement change in your java source will solve this migration issue.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Open API in version 15.0 and later versions has changed together with changes in the UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification. You should read this chapter if you want to migrate your plugin created for earlier MagicDraw version. </p><p>Thereby we made a cleaner UML metamodel implementation API after the UML specification has been updated. There are no big changes in Open API, so migration will not be a long and complicate task for you.</p><p>The changes that have been made to Open API are the following:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) UML metamodel changes" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Some<strong> deprecated methods</strong> have been removed:</li></ul><table><thead><tr><th><div class="tablesorter-header-inner"><p><strong>Removed deprecated method</strong></p></div></th><th><div class="tablesorter-header-inner"><p><strong>Substitution</strong></p></div></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><pre>BaseElement.getProject()</pre></td><td><pre>Project.getProject(BaseElement)</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>MainFrame.getDialogParent()</pre></td><td><pre>MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent()</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>MainFrame.setDialogParent(Frame)</pre></td><td><pre>MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().setDialogParent(Frame)</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>ElementListProperty.setStereotype(Stereotype)</pre></td><td><pre>ElementListProperty.setSelectableRestrictedElements(Collection)</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><ul><li>Changes in<strong> libraries jars</strong> have been made. Open API classes now are packaged into <em>md_api.jar </em>and <em>md_common_api.jar </em>(was <em>md.jar </em>and <em>md_common.jar</em>). If your plugin was using API classes from some program build-in plugin, a plugin jar is also renamed with a “<em>ProgramInitials_api.jar</em>” pattern.</li><li>Changes in<strong> package names for build-in plugins</strong> have been made. In earlier program versions, some build-in plugins exposed their Open API, for example, patterns, transformations, emf, and etc. We have changed a package name pattern. Earlier API versions used a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl.</em><strong><em>**</em> </strong>pattern, now these plugins are moved to<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.*.*,</em> for example <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.patterns.*</em>. A simple import statement change in your java source will solve this migration issue.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93c2742d-0888-468c-8234-5dfbb95f3a8a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="90dfd71b-5ead-4567-bfcc-bb3acf2ac4f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437127 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00438: (2026x) Plugins migration to MagicDraw 17.0.1 and later Open API

- page_id: `254437127`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437127/2026x+Plugins+migration+to+MagicDraw+17.0.1+and+later+Open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:06.120+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you are migrating from earlier than MagicDraw 15.0 version, please read the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Plugins migration to MagicDraw 15.0 and later Open API' space=''] chapter first.

The changes that have been made to Open API are the following:

- UML metamodel changes . UML 2.4.1 specification introduced few changes in the UML metamodel itself. Some metamodel classes were added, some of them were removed, some metaclasses properties were changed. Most of these changes are not in a core UML, so they will affect you if your plugin is oriented to complex things in UML. For the details of changes, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project (decomposition) structure API changes' space=''].
- Changes in methods:
  - ***com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.options.ProjectOptionsConfigurator*.**The method *afterLoad(**ProjectOptions)* has been added. An empty implementation of this method must be added to a custom ProjectOptionsConfigurator implementation.
  - ***com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListener*.**The following methods have been added:

*projectActivatedFromGUI(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectActivatedFromGUI-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectCreated(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectCreated-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectOpenedFromGUI(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectOpenedFromGUI-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreActivated(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreActivated-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreClosed(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreClosed-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreDeActivated(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreDeActivated-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreReplaced(Project[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreReplaced-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreReplaced-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreSaved(Project, boolean)*

*projectPreClosedFinal(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreClosedFinal-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

An empty implementation of these methods must be added to the custom *ProjectEventListener*implementation. The code change is not required, if *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListenerAdapter* is extended.

- Changes in libraries jars have been made. New jar files have been introduced in the lib folder. Do not forget to update your [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compilation classpath' space=''] .

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437132 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00439: (2026x) Plugins migration to MagicDraw 18.0 and later Open API

- page_id: `254437132`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437132/2026x+Plugins+migration+to+MagicDraw+18.0+and+later+Open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:06.946+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Open API Changes

**UML 2.5 support related changes**

- UML metamodel interfaces have been adjusted to UML 2.5 support related changes.
- c om.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL2 and com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL3 classes have been merged into com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile.

**UI refactoring related changes**

UI of diagrams' windows management, symbols' compartments management, and elements creation in compartments have been refactored. As a consequence, some actions have been removed from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID* interface and new actions have been added.

IDs of the removed actions are as follows:

- ActionsGroups.NEXT_DIAGRAM_RELATED
- ActionsGroups.PREVIOUS_DIAGRAM_RELATED
- ActionsID.DIAGRAM_NAVIGATION_GROUP
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_ATTRIBUTE
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_EXTENSION
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_LITERAL
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERAND
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERATION
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_PORT
- ActionsID.LINK_EDITING
- ActionsID.METRICS
- ActionsID.OPEN_IN_NEW_TAB_ACTION
- ActionsID.OPEN_LAST_ActionsID.RECENT_DIAGRAMS
- ActionsID.REDO_LIST
- ActionsID.UNDO_LIST

**Project Merge related changes**

Types of parameters have been changed from Set to Collection, because of performance related improvements in methods of the following interfaces:

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.Change
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.MacroChange
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.RelatedChange
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.RelatedMacroChange

**Structured expressions related changes**

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.expressions.ExpressionHelper*class has been added. It provides utility methods to use in queries defined with the StructuredExpression language (used by smart packages, derived properties in DSL, and so forth).

**UML Interactions related changes**

The *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InteractionHelper* class has been added. It provides utility methods to access Interactions part of UML metamodel.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3 class="heading">Open API Changes</h3><p><strong>UML 2.5 support related changes</strong></p><ul><li>UML metamodel interfaces have been adjusted to UML 2.5 support related changes. </li><li>c<em>om.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL2</em> and <em>com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL3</em> classes have been merged into <em>com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile.</em></li></ul><p><strong>UI refactoring related changes</strong></p><p>UI of diagrams' windows management, symbols' compartments management, and elements creation in compartments have been refactored. As a consequence, some actions have been removed from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID</em> interface and new actions have been added.</p><p>IDs of the removed actions are as follows:</p><ul><li>ActionsGroups.NEXT_DIAGRAM_RELATED</li><li>ActionsGroups.PREVIOUS_DIAGRAM_RELATED</li><li>ActionsID.DIAGRAM_NAVIGATION_GROUP</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_ATTRIBUTE</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_EXTENSION</li><li> ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_LITERAL</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERAND</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERATION</li><li> ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_PORT</li><li>ActionsID.LINK_EDITING</li><li>ActionsID.METRICS</li><li> ActionsID.OPEN_IN_NEW_TAB_ACTION</li><li>ActionsID.OPEN_LAST_ActionsID.RECENT_DIAGRAMS</li><li> ActionsID.REDO_LIST</li><li>ActionsID.UNDO_LIST</li></ul><p><strong>Project Merge related changes</strong></p><p>Types of parameters have been changed from Set to Collection, because of performance related improvements in methods of the following interfaces:</p><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.Change</em></li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.MacroChange</em></li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.RelatedChange</em></li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.RelatedMacroChange</em></li></ul><p><strong>Structured expressions related changes</strong></p><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.expressions.ExpressionHelper </em>class has been added. It provides utility methods to use in queries defined with the StructuredExpression language (used by smart packages, derived properties in DSL, and so forth).</p><p><strong>UML Interactions related changes</strong></p><p>The <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InteractionHelper</em> class has been added. It provides utility methods to access Interactions part of UML metamodel.</p>
````
