## PAGE 00442: (2026x) Presentation element selection events

- page_id: `254437616`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437616/2026x+Presentation+element+selection+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:48.466+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Diagram events
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to register a *java.beans.PropertyChangeListener* on a project. Selection changes fire *java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent*.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You need to register a <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeListener</em> on a project. Selection changes fire <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82b2b534-023d-437c-bc7f-b110a6e43dcc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Project project = ;

Listener listener = new Listener();
SelectionProvider selectionProvider = SelectionProvider.getInstance(project);
selectionProvider.addSelectionChangedListener(listener);

private static class Listener implements SelectionProvider.SelectionChangedListener
{
	@Override
	public void selectionChanged(SelectionProvider.SelectionChangedEvent event)
	{
		// was selected
		SelectionProvider.Selection oldSelection = event.getOldSelection();
		
		// now selected
		SelectionProvider.Selection newSelection = event.getNewSelection();
		
		// do something
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437195 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00443: (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols)

- page_id: `254437195`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437195/2026x+Presentation+elements+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:11.072+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams
- labels: ['path-connector', 'setter', 'set', 'sset']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The UML semantic defines only the UML metamodel. The modeling tool has its own structure of classes for UML elements representation in the diagram. The base class of this structure is *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement*. A presentation element is a textual or graphical presentation of one or more model elements.

Presentation elements are sometimes called symbols also.

In the metamodel, a *PresentationElement* is the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement* that presents a set of model elements to a user. It is the base for all metaclasses used for the presentation. All other metaclasses with this purpose are indirect subclasses of *PresentationElement*.

A current version of Open API provides just a basic structure of presentation elements:

Every presentation element can have children. For example, *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement* has a collection of inner presentation elements. *PresentationElement* of some *Package* can have a collection of presentation elements for inner *Package* elements.

Current version *PresentationElement* API allows you to:

- Access element bounds PresentationElement.getBounds()
- Access a model com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element of a presentation element PresentationElement.getElement() . A presentation element can have no Element , for example, a com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.TextBoxView .
- Access children PresentationElement.getPresentationElements()
- Access properties of the element PresentationElement.getProperty(java.lang.String) , PresentationElement.getPropertyManager(). The sample of properties would be the Suppress Operations property for a class presentation element, the Autosize property for any com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement .
- Select/unselect or access a selection state of the presentation element ( PresentationElement.isSelected() , PresentationElement.setSelected(boolean) ) .

A subclass of presentation elements *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathConnector* provides information about connected paths to the presentation element. To get a collection of connected paths to the presentation element, use the method **PathConnector*.getConnectedPathElements()*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The UML semantic defines only the UML metamodel. The modeling tool has its own structure of classes for UML elements representation in the diagram. The base class of this structure is <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement</em>. A presentation element is a textual or graphical presentation of one or more model elements.</p><p>Presentation elements are sometimes called symbols also.</p><p>In the metamodel, a <em>PresentationElement</em> is the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement</em> that presents a set of model elements to a user. It is the base for all metaclasses used for the presentation. All other metaclasses with this purpose are indirect subclasses of <em>PresentationElement</em>.</p><p>A current version of Open API provides just a basic structure of presentation elements:</p><div class="WordSection1"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="PresentationElements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Presentation elements (symbols)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><p><br /></p><p>Every presentation element can have children. For example, <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement</em> has a collection of inner presentation elements. <em>PresentationElement</em> of some <em>Package</em> can have a collection of presentation elements for inner <em>Package</em> elements.</p><p>Current version <em>PresentationElement</em> API allows you to:</p><ul><li>Access element bounds <em>PresentationElement.getBounds()</em></li><li>Access a model <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em> of a  presentation element<em> PresentationElement.getElement()</em>. A presentation element can have no <em>Element</em>, for example, a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.TextBoxView</em>.</li><li>Access children <em>PresentationElement.getPresentationElements()</em></li><li>Access properties of the element <em>PresentationElement.getProperty(java.lang.String)</em>, <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> PresentationElement.getPropertyManager(). </em> The sample of properties would be the <em>Suppress Operations</em> property for a class presentation element, the <em>Autosize</em> property for any <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement</em>.</li><li>Select/unselect or access a selection state of the presentation element (<em>PresentationElement.isSelected()</em>, <em>PresentationElement.setSelected(boolean)</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">)</em>.</li></ul><p>A subclass of presentation elements <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathConnector</em> provides information about connected paths to the presentation element. To get a collection of connected paths to the presentation element, use the method <em><em>PathConnector</em>.getConnectedPathElements()</em>.</p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57a03e27-4e6f-4678-af4d-6459fd3881f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="96a7c21e-302a-4a6d-b539-6a039cbba25a"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437598 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00444: (2026x) Presentation elements modification events

- page_id: `254437598`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437598/2026x+Presentation+elements+modification+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:47.663+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Diagram events
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools provide an API for listening to all diagram changes in a single adapter:

1. The listener receives events from all opened diagrams.
2. The adapter delegates these events to your own listener.

The adapter works in a project scope.

****

To listen to diagram change events, you need to create the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramListenerAdapter* object, that is, to pass the property change listener as a delegator, which receives all events.

To create the *DiagramListenerAdapter* object, call:

```java

```

The *DiagramListenerAdapter* object is registered for a project on its creation.

To start using the adapter, install it. Uninstall it when it is no longer necessary, that is, you do not need to receive any events about diagram changes anymore.

Property names related to presentation elements modification are defined in *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ExtendedPropertyNames***class*.*

**Example: Listening to symbol creation / removal**

```java

```

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Our modeling tools provide an API for listening to all diagram changes in a single adapter:</p><ol><li>The listener receives events from all <em>opened </em>diagrams.</li><li>The adapter delegates these events to your own listener.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04930027-ff28-4880-92a6-0dd1eb6571da"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The adapter works in a project scope.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>            </strong></p><p>To listen to diagram change events, you need to create the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramListenerAdapter</em> object, that is, to pass the property change listener as a delegator, which receives all events.</p><p>To create the <em>DiagramListenerAdapter</em> object, call:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e521b8d-0412-4726-a1e6-ae12e9ae463f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    new DiagramListenerAdapter(propertyChangeListener)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <em>DiagramListenerAdapter</em> object is registered for a project on its creation.</p><p>To start using the adapter, install it. Uninstall it when it is no longer necessary, that is, you do not need to receive any events about diagram changes anymore.</p><p><br /></p><p>Property names related to presentation elements modification are defined in <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ExtendedPropertyNames</em><em> </em>class<em>.</em></p><p><strong>Example: Listening to symbol creation / removal</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ed5ff09-ede8-4f7d-bc3f-956244a75f95"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    DiagramListenerAdapter adapter = new DiagramListenerAdapter(new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        {
            String propertyName = evt.getPropertyName();
            if (ExtendedPropertyNames.VIEW_ADDED.equals(propertyName) || ExtendedPropertyNames.VIEW_REMOVED.equals(propertyName))
            {
                // an added / removed symbol
                PresentationElement presentationElement = (PresentationElement) evt.getNewValue();
            }
        }
    }
    
    adapter.install(project);
 
    // When the adapter is no longer needed, uninstall it. 
    adapter.uninstall(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre class="auto-cursor-target"> </pre></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437187 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00445: (2026x) Printing messages in Simulation Console

- page_id: `254437187`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437187/2026x+Printing+messages+in+Simulation+Console
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:10.636+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > (2026x) Java APIs > (2026x) Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can print different types of messages on the **Simulation Console** pane by using the following codes

- SimulationManager.logConsoleDebug ( Project project , String message)

Displays debugging information.

- SimulationManager.logConsoleInfo ( Project project , String message)

Displays information in general.

- SimulationManager.logConsoleWarn ( Project project , String message)

Displays warning messages.

- SimulationManager.logConsoleError ( Project project , String message)

Displays error messages.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can print different types of messages on the <strong>Simulation Console</strong> pane by using the following codes</p><p><br /></p><ul><li>SimulationManager.logConsoleDebug <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ef04b705-9d65-44d1-99f3-bd5e54f5d641">(</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="059ecca9-0690-4430-a621-ab53a63d87d7"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ef04b705-9d65-44d1-99f3-bd5e54f5d641">Project project</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker>, String message)</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Displays debugging information.</p><ul><li>SimulationManager.logConsoleInfo (<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c5afac16-1841-4b97-9e0c-4dded575558c"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e53f375f-2031-4bf1-97a7-4b27e9b063fd">Project project</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e53f375f-2031-4bf1-97a7-4b27e9b063fd">,</ac:inline-comment-marker> String message)</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Displays information in general.</p><ul><li>SimulationManager.logConsoleWarn (<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="87888861-8b59-4cc0-9d14-4efc4d4fa543"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8088163b-f3b1-4e9f-a968-dc72ec40ef77">Project project</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker>, String message)</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Displays warning messages.</p><ul><li>SimulationManager.logConsoleError (<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="03a8dd06-c036-4926-bf31-6bf2ab29409f"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e2891690-4874-4215-acbd-2b7d0cefec5a">Project project</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker>, String message)</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Displays error messages.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437129 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00446: (2026x) Project (decomposition) structure API changes

- page_id: `254437129`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437129/2026x+Project+decomposition+structure+API+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:06.462+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins migration > (2026x) Plugins migration to MagicDraw 17.0.1 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following figure shows the domain model of the project decomposition.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The core change is an introduction of *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IProject* - *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject* and *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject*. *IPrimaryProject* is a single instance in the scope of a project. *IPrimaryProject* can have attached (used) any number of *IAttachedProject* (used projects), while *IAttachedProject* itself can attach other used projects (*IAttachedProject*).

The earlier than 17.0.1 project structure API made deprecated in version 17.0.1. The deprecated classes from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules*package are:

*Committable*

*IProject*

*ModuleDescriptor*

*ModulesManager*

*MountHelper*

*MountInfo*

*MountTable*

*MountTableListener*

*ShareInfo*

*ShareTable*

There is no straight one to one mapping, but the set of deprecated classes are now generally replaced by the set of the following classes:

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IProject*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.mounting.IMountPoint*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.sharing.ISharePoint*

*ProjectUtilities* and *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService* utility classes are introduced to help working with a new project decomposition structure and should replace *MountHelper*.

*ModuleDescriptor* is replaced with a combination of *IAttachedProject* and *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration*. There is separate *ModuleDescriptor* for every (same) project usage in MagicDraw 17.0 or earlier versions, while there is a single *IAttachedProject* for the used project, but a separate *ProjectAttachmentConfiguration* for every project usage in the current version.

The example of the MagicDraw 17.0 and earlier version code:

```java
projectModules = project.getModulesManager().getMountTable().getModules()
	for (ModuleDescriptor moduleDescriptor : projectModules)
	{
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = moduleDescriptor.getAutoLoadType();
		final boolean loadIndex = moduleDescriptor.isLoadIndex();
		final boolean editable = moduleDescriptor.isEditable();
	}]]>
```

The example of a new MagicDraw 17.0.2 code:

```java
projectAttachedProjects = ProjectUtilities.getAttachedProjects(primaryProject);
	for (IAttachedProject attachedProject : projectAttachedProjects)
	{
		final ProjectAttachmentConfiguration attachmentConfiguration = ProjectUtilities.getAttachment(primaryProject, attachedProject);
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = ProjectUtilities.getAutoLoadKind(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean loadIndex = ProjectUtilities.isLoadIndex(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean editable = !attachmentConfiguration.isReadOnly();
	}]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The following figure shows the domain model of the project decomposition.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Domain model of project decomposition" ac:alt="Domain model of project decomposition"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Domain_model_of_project_decomposition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project (decomposition) structure API changes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><div class="WordSection1"><p><br /></p><p>The core change is an introduction of <em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IProject</em> - <em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject</em> and <em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject</em>. <em>IPrimaryProject</em> is a single instance in the scope of a project. <em>IPrimaryProject</em> can have attached (used) any number of <em>IAttachedProject</em> (used projects), while <em>IAttachedProject</em> itself can attach other used projects (<em>IAttachedProject</em>).</p><p>The earlier than 17.0.1 project structure API made deprecated in version 17.0.1. The deprecated classes from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules </em>package are:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Committable</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em> IProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em> ModuleDescriptor</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ModulesManager </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountHelper </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountInfo </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountTable </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountTableListener </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ShareInfo </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ShareTable</em></p></div><div class="WordSection2"><p><br /></p><p>There is no straight one to one mapping, but  the set of deprecated classes are now generally replaced by the set of the following classes:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.mounting.IMountPoint</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.sharing.ISharePoint</em></p><p><em>ProjectUtilities</em> and <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService</em> utility classes are introduced to help working with a new project decomposition structure and should replace <em>MountHelper</em>.</p><p><em>ModuleDescriptor</em> is replaced with a combination of <em>IAttachedProject</em> and <em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration</em>. There is separate <em>ModuleDescriptor</em> for every (same) project usage in MagicDraw 17.0 or earlier versions, while there is a single <em>IAttachedProject</em> for the used project, but a separate <em>ProjectAttachmentConfiguration</em> for every project usage in the current version.</p><p><br /></p><p>The example of the MagicDraw 17.0 and earlier version code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ea0081f-b05b-4ada-a258-f78dd22d3f07"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	final Collection<ModuleDescriptor> projectModules = project.getModulesManager().getMountTable().getModules()
	for (ModuleDescriptor moduleDescriptor : projectModules)
	{
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = moduleDescriptor.getAutoLoadType();
		final boolean loadIndex = moduleDescriptor.isLoadIndex();
		final boolean editable = moduleDescriptor.isEditable();
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The example of a new MagicDraw 17.0.2 code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c472fef9-e92a-462c-b715-593ad911b154"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	final IPrimaryProject primaryProject = project.getPrimaryProject();
	final Collection<IAttachedProject> projectAttachedProjects = ProjectUtilities.getAttachedProjects(primaryProject);
	for (IAttachedProject attachedProject : projectAttachedProjects)
	{
		final ProjectAttachmentConfiguration attachmentConfiguration = ProjectUtilities.getAttachment(primaryProject, attachedProject);
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = ProjectUtilities.getAutoLoadKind(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean loadIndex = ProjectUtilities.isLoadIndex(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean editable = !attachmentConfiguration.isReadOnly();
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437662 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00447: (2026x) Project concept

- page_id: `254437662`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437662/2026x+Project+concept
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:50.744+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with project
- labels: ['application', 'project', 'model', 'root-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project* represents the storage of all project specific data like the UML model, all diagrams, project specific options and etc.

Multiple projects can be opened at the same time, but just one Project can be active in UI. Data from one project can not reference data in other opened project. The active (current) project can be accessible in the following way:

```java

```

Project

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement (UML Element or Presentation Element)

```java

```

The project keeps references to a root *Model(s)*, also has references to all diagrams.

Project always have one primary root Model and may have zero or more attached Models from used projects. Attached models are available only in projects loaded from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Teamwork Cloud' space='MT']. Locally stored projects or projects have only a primary root model.

The primary root model can be accessed in the following way:

```java

```

All root models can be accessed in the following way:

```java
models = project.getModels();]]>
```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project structure decomposition' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) UML model' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project options' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project</em> represents the storage of all project specific data like the UML model, all diagrams, project specific options and etc.</p><p>Multiple projects can be opened at the same time, but just one Project can be active in UI. Data from one project can not reference data in other opened project. The active (current) project can be accessible in the following way:</p><div class="WordSection1"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64d0d6b5-8f4e-4580-85c5-7f67f8f84fac"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div class="WordSection1">Also, the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Project </em>is accessible for any other <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement (UML Element or Presentation Element)</em></div><div class="WordSection1"><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7032c3b8-1f0c-4778-8c65-d8b5e1366e57"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = Project.getProject(element);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p>The project keeps references to a root <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Model(s)</em>, also has references to all diagrams.</p><p>Project always have one primary root Model and may have zero or more attached Models from used projects. Attached models are available only in projects loaded from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Using Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Enterprise Dataware server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Locally stored projects or projects have only a primary root model.</p><p>The primary root model can be accessed in the following way:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fc25cc4-311a-4752-9d10-d328721fd47a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Package model = project.getPrimaryModel();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span>All root models can be accessed in the following way:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48570a3b-95d2-4b8a-8056-d004f14ce5fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    List<Package> models = project.getModels();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="579ea26b-cf13-4db3-98f0-b9e6944d72e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project structure decomposition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) UML model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UML Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437666 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00448: (2026x) Project descriptor

- page_id: `254437666`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437666/2026x+Project+descriptor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:50.984+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with project
- labels: ['projectdescriptorsfactory', 'teamworkutils', 'project-descriptors-factory', 'teamwork-utils']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor* represents a project (or a used project) as a resource for storing and loading. The same project can have multiple *ProjectDescriptors*.

There are two types of *ProjectDescriptors*:

- A local project descriptor. It represents a local ordinary project. A descriptor can be created for a project or file object.
- A r emote project descriptor. It represents a project stored in the server.

A server project has both descriptors (local and remote) because it can be saved locally.

Every *ProjectDescriptor* provides the following properties:

- URI. The location is a specific java.lang.String value that holds all information that is needed to access a project.
- Representation String. A string used for a project identification in the user interface (GUI).
- Remote flag. The remote flag indicates if the project descriptor represents a server project.

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptorsFactory*can create an appropriate *ProjectDescriptor* object,

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils***helps to find an existing [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Teamwork Cloud' space='MT'] (remote) project descriptors.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor</em> represents a project (or a used project) as a resource for storing and loading. The same project can have multiple <em>ProjectDescriptors</em>.</p><p>There are two types of <em>ProjectDescriptors</em>:</p><ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">A local project descriptor. </strong>It represents a local ordinary project. A descriptor can be created for a project or file object.</li><li><strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">A r</span></strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">emote project descriptor.</strong> It represents a project stored in the server.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00f130cd-2a48-40e4-9687-d59f7800d6f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A server project has both descriptors (local and remote) because it can be saved locally.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Every <em>ProjectDescriptor</em> provides the following properties:</p><ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">URI.</strong> The location is a specific <em>java.lang.String</em> value that holds all information that is needed to access a project.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Representation String.</strong> A string used for a project identification in the user interface (GUI).</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Remote flag. </strong>The remote flag indicates if the project descriptor represents a server project.</li></ul><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptorsFactory </em>can create an appropriate <em>ProjectDescriptor</em> object,</p><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils</em><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </em>helps to find an existing <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Using Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (remote) project descriptors.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437676 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00449: (2026x) Project life-cycle events

- page_id: `254437676`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437676/2026x+Project+life-cycle+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:51.951+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with project
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager.addProjectListener(ProjectEventListener)* to register *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListener*. The listener will be invoked on various project life-cycle related actions.

A recommendation is to register a listener in the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin.init()* method.

##### Save participant

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant* to plugin into a save/commit operation.

Register it using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(SaveParticipant[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Application.html#addSaveParticipant-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant-)*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Projects management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager.addProjectListener(ProjectEventListener)</em> to register <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListener</em>. The listener will be invoked on various project life-cycle related actions. </p><p>A recommendation is to register a listener in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin.init()</em> method.</p><h3>Save participant</h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant</em> to plugin into a save/commit operation. </p><p>Register it using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(SaveParticipant<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Application.html#addSaveParticipant-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant-">)</a></em>. </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e3f64e1-1fb4-457e-b9d9-bffd65c50979"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Projects management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437474 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00450: (2026x) Project loading and saving

- page_id: `254437474`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437474/2026x+Project+loading+and+saving
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:33.801+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with project > (2026x) Projects management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Projects are saved and loaded by using two methods in the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager* class.

- saveProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)
- loadProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)

“Save” means “Commit” for the server project.

A project cannot be saved using a descriptor, if the project isn’t specified, and a project cannot be loaded, if the file isn’t specified. In such cases *java.lang.IllegalStateException* is thrown.

The silent mode means that during the saving or load process no GUI interruptions for a user input is used, for example, there is no the **Commit Project**dialog box while committing a server project or there is no the **Save**dialog box while saving a new project (a project is saved into the last used directory).

##### **Save participant**

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant* to plugin into a save/commit operation.

Register it using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(**SaveParticipant)*.

Implementing *SaveParticipant.isReadyForSave(**Project, ProjectDescriptor)* you can "disable" the save/commit operation until some conditions are met.

###### Example #1. Saving an active project

```java

```

###### Example #2. Loading a project from the file

The project can be loaded, if the project’s file name is known:

```java

```

###### Example #3. Importing another project file

The project can be imported, if the project's file name is known:

```java

```

###### Example #4. Loading a server project

The project can be loaded, if the project’s qualified name is known:

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project descriptor' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>Projects are saved and loaded by using two methods in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager</em> class.</p><ul><li><em>saveProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)</em></li><li><em>loadProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)</em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42fdc785-9a78-4dce-ab21-2f094ef0cbd2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>“Save” means “Commit” for the server project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A project cannot be saved using a descriptor, if the project isn’t specified, and a project cannot be loaded, if the file isn’t specified. In such cases <em>java.lang.IllegalStateException</em> is thrown.</p><p>The silent mode means that during the saving or load process no GUI interruptions for a user input is used, for example, there is no the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Commit Project </strong>dialog box while committing a server project or there is no the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Save </strong>dialog box while saving a new project (a project is saved into the last used directory).</p><h3><strong>Save participant</strong></h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant</em> to plugin into a save/commit operation. </p><p>Register it using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(</em><em>SaveParticipant)</em>. </p><p>Implementing <em>SaveParticipant.isReadyForSave(</em><em>Project, ProjectDescriptor)</em> you can &quot;disable&quot; the save/commit operation until some conditions are met.</p><h4>Example #1. Saving an active project</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="402ef67b-d6b8-4112-8555-156fa0eff7dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager();
    // An active project
    Project project = projectsManager.getActiveProject();
 
    // Get a project descriptor
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.getDescriptorForProject(project);
    
    // Save a project 
    projectsManager.saveProject(projectDescriptor, true);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><h4>Example #2. Loading a project from the file</h4><p>The project can be loaded, if the project’s file name is known:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83f7a0b8-25be-4db9-92aa-df95ec7afe16"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager(); 
    File file = new File(projectFilePath);
    // Create a project descriptor 
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.createProjectDescriptor(file.toURI()); 
    projectsManager.loadProject(projectDescriptor, true);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example #3. Importing another project file</h4><p>The project can be imported, if the project's file name is known:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5347718-0fe2-45bb-ba94-786d7bb53ffb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager(); 
    File file = new File(projectFilePath);
    // Create a project descriptor 
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.createProjectDescriptor(file.toURI()); 
    projectsManager.importProject(projectDescriptor);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example #4. Loading a server project</h4><p>The project can be loaded, if the project’s qualified name is known:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="070a54dc-b403-4943-bb90-ef37f4e987e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager();
    // Create a project descriptor
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = TeamworkUtils.getRemoteProjectDescriptorByQualifiedName(remoteProjectQualifiedName);
    if (projectDescriptor != null)
    {
        projectsManager.loadProject(projectDescriptor, true);
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d623b2ab-c3d7-4b26-b554-42442b6e0377"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project descriptor" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437674 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00451: (2026x) Project options

- page_id: `254437674`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437674/2026x+Project+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:51.869+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with project
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every project has project related options. These options are stored together with the project data. Options are organized using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property* and *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager* classes.

Project options belong to three categories:

1. Visible in the Project Options UI window
2. Invisible in the Project Options UI window
3. Personal invisible in the Project Options UI window

Personal options are specific to every user in a server project. It means, every user may have different values of the same option if it is marked as "personal". The personal option is treated as a regular option in a local project.

##### **Retrieving Project Options**

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getOptions()**.*

##### **Retrieving Project Option value**

The following example shows how to access project option’s value:

```java

```

**Adding custom project options**

Configurators are used for defining a new project options.

The following example shows, how to add the project option’s configurator at the plugin’s *init()* method in order to have the additional project option ”Test Property” for every project.

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Properties' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Every project has project related options. These options are stored together with the project data. Options are organized using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property</em> and <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager</em> classes. </p><p>Project options belong to three categories:</p><ol><li>Visible in the Project Options UI window</li><li>Invisible in the Project Options UI window</li><li>Personal invisible in the Project Options UI window</li></ol><p>Personal options are specific to every user in a server project. It means, every user may have different values of the same option if it is marked as &quot;personal&quot;. The personal option is treated as a regular option in a local project.</p><h3><strong>Retrieving Project Options</strong></h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getOptions()</em><em>.</em></p><h3><strong>Retrieving Project Option value</strong></h3><p>The following example shows how to access project option’s value:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bab1580e-fad4-4325-9c3c-dfc0bac2e96a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    //retrieving a visible in UI option
    Property property = project.getOptions().getProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_GENERAL_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
    //retrieving an invisible in UI option
    Property property = project.getOptions().getProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_INVISIBLE_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
    //retrieving a personal invisible in UI option
    Property property = project.getOptions().getProperty(ProjectOptions.PERSONAL_INVISIBLE_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
    
    //retrieving an option value
    if (property != null)
    {
        Object value = property.getValue();
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Adding custom project options</span></strong></p><p class="WordSection1">Configurators are used for defining a new project options. </p><p class="WordSection1">The following example shows, how to add the project option’s configurator at the plugin’s <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">init()</em> method in order to have the additional project option ”Test Property” for every project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="240636e0-34c2-464e-9969-6154f03d6f38"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectOptions.addConfigurator(new ProjectOptionsConfigurator()
    {
        public void configure(ProjectOptions projectOptions)
        {
            com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property property = projectOptions.getProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_GENERAL_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
            if (property == null)
            {
                // Create a property, if it does not exist
                property = new StringProperty("TEST_PROPERTY_ID", "description");
                // Group
                property.setGroup("MY_GROUP");
                // The custom resource provider
                property.setResourceProvider(new PropertyResourceProvider()
                {
                    public String getString(String string, Property property)
                    {
                        if ("TEST_PROPERTY_ID".equals(string))
                        {
                            // Translate ID
                            return "Test Property";
                         }
                         if ("TEST_PROPERTY_ID_DESCRIPTION".equals(string))
                        {
                            // Translate a description
                            return "Test Property in My Group";
                        }
                        if ("MY_GROUP".equals(string))
                        {
                            // Translate a group
                            return "My Group";
                        }
						return string; 
					}
                });
          
                // Add a property
                projectOptions.addProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_GENERAL_PROPERTIES, property);
            }
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08f1f2bc-1687-469f-99c0-4be4352cdb03"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Properties" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437671 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00452: (2026x) Project structure decomposition

- page_id: `254437671`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437671/2026x+Project+structure+decomposition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:51.767+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with project
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Project structure is decomposed into several parts - a primary project and attached projects. The primary project part belong to a project itself. Attached project parts comes from "used projects" (modules). Used projects can use other projects, so there is a deep structure of attached projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities* utility class for retrieving more project decomposition related information.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService* or*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager* classes for managing attached projects.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Projects management' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Used project management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Project structure is decomposed into several parts - a primary project and attached projects. The primary project part belong to a project itself. Attached project parts comes from &quot;used projects&quot; (modules). Used projects can use other projects, so there is a deep structure of attached projects.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Project structure decomposition" ac:alt="Project structure decomposition"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project structure decomposition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project structure decomposition" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities</em> utility class for retrieving more project decomposition related information.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService</em> or<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager</em> classes for managing attached projects.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ebbfd8f-20ed-42c8-b119-db7efb958c35"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Projects management" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Used project management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00453: (2026x) Projects management

- page_id: `254437468`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437468/2026x+Projects+management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:33.507+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with project
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager* class is responsible for containment and management of projects.

Use the code below to access *ProjectsManager*

```java

```

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
*ProjectsManager* provides the API for *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project* creating, closing, saving, loading, and activating. A program can have multiple opened projects, but only one project can be active.

```java
projects = projectsManager.getProjects();
 
    //Gets an active project
    Project activeProject = projectsManager.getActiveProject();]]>
```

An active project can also be accessed directly from *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application*:

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\projects*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager</em> class is responsible for containment and management of projects.</p><p>Use the code below to access <em>ProjectsManager </em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9dc889d4-d9ef-44b1-bf8a-03d8aa227b4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ProjectsManager.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Projects management" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><em>ProjectsManager</em> provides the API for <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project</em> creating, closing, saving, loading, and activating. A program can have multiple opened projects, but only one project can be active.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae5055ea-ecbd-404d-b564-db739281968b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    //Gets all the projects
    List<Project> projects = projectsManager.getProjects();
 
    //Gets an active project
    Project activeProject = projectsManager.getActiveProject();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>An active project can also be accessed directly from <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a99927b-5af6-4b20-8e1e-06999fe453ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    //Gets an active project

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437356 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00454: (2026x) Properties

- page_id: `254437356`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437356/2026x+Properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:23.574+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['property-editor', 'property-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools include the Open API that provides a set of classes used as properties for diagrams’ symbols, project or environment options and etc.

Every property has two major attributes – the ID and value. A property has a value of the type *java.lang.Object*. Every specific property has a value of the specific type. For example, the value of *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.BooleanProperty* is*java.lang.Boolean*, the value of a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.StringProperty* is *java.lang.String*.

The property ID identifies the specific property in the properties set.

You must set some *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyResourceProvider* to your property instance in order to display a normal name, not the ID of the property in the UI of a modeling tool. *PropertyResourceProvider* has just one method *getString(java.lang.String , Property[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/properties/PropertyResourceProvider.html#getString-java.lang.String-com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property-), where a key is an ID of your property.*

The collection of properties is grouped by *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager*. Every *PropertyManager* has a name and a list of properties. It can return the property by the ID, properties with the same value, properties whose values are different.

For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Changing properties of presentation elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project options' space='']

````html

A property has a value of the type <em>java.lang.Object</em>. Every specific property has a value of the specific type. For example, the value of <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.BooleanProperty</em> is<em> java.lang.Boolean</em>, the value of a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.StringProperty</em> is <em>java.lang.String</em>.</p><p>The property ID identifies the specific property in the properties set.</p><div class="WordSection1"><p><br /></p><p>You must set some <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyResourceProvider</em> to your property instance in order to display a normal name, not the ID of the property in the UI of a modeling tool. <em>PropertyResourceProvider</em> has just one method <em>getString(java.lang.String , Property<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/properties/PropertyResourceProvider.html#getString-java.lang.String-com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property-">)</a>, where a key is an ID of your property.</em></p></div><p>The collection of properties is grouped by <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager</em>. Every <em>PropertyManager</em> has a name and a list of properties. It can return the property by the ID, properties with the same value, properties whose values are different.</p><p>For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fee02083-faa4-4e60-b526-a587d03e9b37"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Changing properties of presentation elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00455: (2026x) Property change event concept

- page_id: `254437589`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437589/2026x+Property+change+event+concept
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:47.480+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Event support
- labels: ['new-element-creation-event', 'elements-name-change-event']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UML model uses standard *java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent* events for notifying listeners about changes.

The main properties of the *PropertyChangeEvent*are as follows:

- Property name. The changed property name.
- New value. The new (current) changed property value.
- Old value. The old property value, which was before the property change.

Properties names are described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) UML model properties names' space=''] page

###### **Example of the element’s name change event**

```java

```

###### **Example of the new element creation event**

```java

```

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>UML model uses standard <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent</em> events for notifying listeners about changes.  </p><p>The main properties of the <em>PropertyChangeEvent </em>are as follows:</p><ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Property name. </strong>The changed property name.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">New value. </strong>The new (current) changed property value.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Old value. </strong>The old property value, which was before the property change.</li></ul><div class="WordSection1"><p>Properties names are described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) UML model properties names" /></ac:link> page</p><h4><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;">Example of the element’s name change event</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d8be409-b977-48ee-b9b4-c91bc4eba484"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    propertyChangeEvent.getPropertyName().equals(PropertyNames.NAME); 
    propertyChangeEvent.getNewValue() // will be new name of element; 
    propertyChangeEvent.getOldValue() // will be name of element before change.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><h4><strong>Example of the new element creation event</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83aa81ad-516b-41fd-a9b0-032f0a869c65"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    propertyChangeEvent.getPropertyName().equals(UML2MetamodelConstants.INSTANCE_CREATED); 
    propertyChangeEvent.getNewValue() // will be new created element; 
    propertyChangeEvent.getOldValue() // will be null.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41f3ca8f-847f-429b-91cd-0f08a231b578"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) UML model properties names" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00456: (2026x) Refactoring model elements

- page_id: `254437518`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437518/2026x+Refactoring+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:39.086+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To refactor an element in a model, use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring***class.

###### **Example #1: Converting an element to an interface**

```java

```

###### **Example #2: Replacing an element with another element**

```java

```

###### **Example #3: Extract refactoring**

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Extracting***class to create the extract manager for a symbol which you want to extract. Configure the extract refactoring by changing *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractSource* and *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractTarget*. Invoke the refactoring with *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractManager.extract()*. Review refactoring results by inspecting *ExtractSource* and *ExtractTarget*.

```java
allowedTargetDiagramTypes = extractTarget.getAllowedTargetDiagramTypes();
        extractTarget.setTargetDiagramType(allowedTargetDiagramTypes.get(0));
 
        // Modify reference names which link the extract refactor source to the target.
        List extends ExtractReference> references = extractTarget.getReferences();
 
        for (int i = 0; i < references.size(); i++)
        {
            ExtractReference reference = references.get(i);
            reference.setName(Integer.toString(i));
        }
 
        // We may control the extract refactor source by modifying the extract source.
        ExtractSource extractSource = extractManager.getExtractSource();
        extractSource.setElementName("sourceElementName");


        // Perform actual refactoring. 
        extractManager.extract();
        sessionManager.closeSession(project);


        // The element which was created in the source during refactoring. 
        Element sourceElement = extractSource.getElement();


        // The element which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        Element targetElement = extractTarget.getElement();


        // The diagram which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        DiagramPresentationElement targetDiagram = extractTarget.getDiagram();
    }]]>
```

###### **Example #4: Reverse relationship refactoring**

The relation reverse refactoring can be done using the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.RelationReversing.reverseRelationDirection(**[Element](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html))***method.

```java

```

###### **Example #5: Moving element with connected relationships to other owner**

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Moving.moveElementsWithRelation(java.util.Collection<**Element>, Element)* to move *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element* and all connected *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Relationship(s)* to a new owner.

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling tool installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\refactor*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Session management' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Checking element editing permissions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To refactor an element in a model, use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring</em><em> </em>class.</p><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example #1: Converting an element to an interface</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="996e118e-32cf-4ec3-84a3-d1a0befe5030"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element elementToConvert = ...;
    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Convert");
 
    // Converts the element to an interface.
    ConvertElementInfo info = new ConvertElementInfo(Interface.class);
 
    // Preserves the old element ID for the new element. 
    info.setPreserveElementID(true);
    Element conversionTarget = Refactoring.Converting.convert(elementToConvert, info);
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><strong>Example #2: Replacing an element with another element</strong></h4><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd05f199-c8c8-46d9-9379-1f52b2421087"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element elementToReplace = ...;
    Project project = ...; 
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Replace");
 
    ConvertElementInfo info = new ConvertElementInfo(elementToReplace.getClassType());
    info.setConvertOnlyIncomingReferences(true);
 
    Refactoring.Replacing.replace(element, elementToReplace, info);   
    
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection1"><h4><strong>Example #3: Extract refactoring</strong></h4><p>Use the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Extracting</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>class to create the extract manager for a symbol which you want to extract. Configure the extract refactoring by changing <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractSource</em> and <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractTarget</em>. Invoke the refactoring with <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractManager.extract()</em>. Review refactoring results by inspecting <em>ExtractSource</em> and <em>ExtractTarget</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b25ad79-2609-464f-a3f6-e20f4e785c68"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // Creates an extract refactor manager.
    ExtractManager extractManager = Refactoring.Extracting.createExtractManager(symbols);
    if (extractManager != null)
    {
        Project project = ...;
        // A session has to be started before refactoring. 
        SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance(); 
        sessionManager.createSession(project, "Extract Refactor Symbols");
 
        // We may control the extract refactor result by modifying extract target. 
        ExtractTarget extractTarget = extractManager.getExtractTarget();
 
        // Create a namespace to which we are going to refactor. 
        Project project = Project.getProject(symbols[0]);
        Package package1 = project.getElementsFactory().createPackageInstance();
        package1.setOwner(project.getPrimaryModel());


        // Set the namespace to which the extract result should go. 
        extractTarget.setTargetNamespace(package1);
 
        // Choose target diagram type from allowed diagram types if the default type does not suite.
        List<String>  allowedTargetDiagramTypes = extractTarget.getAllowedTargetDiagramTypes();
        extractTarget.setTargetDiagramType(allowedTargetDiagramTypes.get(0));
 
        // Modify reference names which link the extract refactor source to the target.
        List<? extends ExtractReference> references = extractTarget.getReferences();
 
        for (int i = 0; i < references.size(); i++)
        {
            ExtractReference reference = references.get(i);
            reference.setName(Integer.toString(i));
        }
 
        // We may control the extract refactor source by modifying the extract source.
        ExtractSource extractSource = extractManager.getExtractSource();
        extractSource.setElementName("sourceElementName");


        // Perform actual refactoring. 
        extractManager.extract();
        sessionManager.closeSession(project);


        // The element which was created in the source during refactoring. 
        Element sourceElement = extractSource.getElement();


        // The element which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        Element targetElement = extractTarget.getElement();


        // The diagram which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        DiagramPresentationElement targetDiagram = extractTarget.getDiagram();
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><h4><strong>Example #4: Reverse relationship refactoring</strong></h4><p>The relation reverse refactoring can be done using the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.RelationReversing.reverseRelationDirection(</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel">Element</a>)</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>method.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1967114d-d7fe-43aa-b363-92527bbea717"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[     // We have an arbitrary element or symbol which represents a relationship. 
    BaseElement baseElement = ...;
    Project project = ...; 
    // Relationship reversing should be wrapped with session create/close calls. 
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance(); 
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Reverse relation");  
 
    // Reverse the relationship. 
    Refactoring.RelationReversing.reverseRelationDirection(baseElement);
 
    // Close the session. 
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example #5: Moving element with connected relationships to other owner</strong></h4><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Moving.moveElementsWithRelation(java.util.Collection&lt;</em><em>Element&gt;, Element)</em> to move <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em> and all connected <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Relationship(s)</em> to a new owner.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in &lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\refactor</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b50b2c6-e274-460d-b17d-b7fdbdbe92c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Session management" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Checking element editing permissions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00457: (2026x) Registering an execution engine to the Simulation Manager

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To register your new execution engine to the Simulation Manager, pass a new instance of your execution engine descriptor to the Simulation Manager by typing the following code

```text

```

In general, a new execution engine is registered at <Plugin Class>.init().

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To register your new execution engine to the Simulation Manager, pass a new instance of your execution engine descriptor to the Simulation Manager by typing the following code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ac1e768-d1a9-4cdc-9d40-cb4dd54f9240"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[SimulationManager.registerEngine(new MyEngineDescriptor());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In general, a new execution engine is registered at &lt;Plugin Class&gt;.init().</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437123 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00458: (2026x) Registering custom hyperlink handler

- page_id: `254437123`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437123/2026x+Registering+custom+hyperlink+handler
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:05.431+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Managing navigation in model > (2026x) Managing hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to register a new custom hyperlink handler for a custom protocol. The custom hyperlink may have a specific UI editor for editing it's properties.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.addHandler(HyperlinkHandler[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/hyperlinks/HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.html#addHandler-com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkHandler-)* to add a custom handler.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\customhyperlink

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is possible to register a new custom hyperlink handler for a custom protocol. The custom hyperlink may have a specific UI editor for editing it's properties.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.addHandler(HyperlinkHandler<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/hyperlinks/HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.html#addHandler-com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkHandler-">)</a></em> to add a custom handler.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\customhyperlink</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
