<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437324 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00459: (2026x) Registering custom symbol renderer provider

- page_id: `254437324`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437324/2026x+Registering+custom+symbol+renderer+provider
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:22.085+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x) Custom presentation elements rendering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following example shows the custom symbol renderer provider, which provides the *SlotRenderer* for slot symbols, the *PackageRenderer* for package symbols, and the *DependencyRenderer* for a dependency link symbols. The created renderer provider must be registered into the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererManager*.

****

**Step 1. Creating the RendererProvider class**

```java

```

**Step 2. Registering RendererProvider**

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The following example shows the custom symbol renderer provider, which provides the <em>SlotRenderer</em> for slot symbols, the <em>PackageRenderer</em> for package symbols, and the <em>DependencyRenderer</em> for a dependency link symbols. The created renderer provider must be registered into the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererManager</em>.</p></div><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Step 1. Creating the RendererProvider class</strong> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd8b2e2c-d3fa-4500-9c26-1299c2ac27e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[     /**
    * A custom renderers provider.
    */
    class RendererProvider implements PresentationElementRendererProvider
    {
        private SlotRenderer slotRenderer;
        private DependencyRenderer dependencyRenderer;
        private PackageRenderer packageRenderer;
 
        RendererProvider()
        {
            slotRenderer = new SlotRenderer(); 
            dependencyRenderer= new DependencyRenderer(); 
            packageRenderer = new PackageRenderer();
        }
 
        public PresentationElementRenderer getRenderer(PresentationElement presentationElement)
        {
            if (presentationElement instanceof SlotView || presentationElement instanceof SlotListElementView)
            {
                // A slot renderer 
                return slotRenderer;
            }
 
            if (presentationElement instanceof DependencyView)
            {
                // dependency renderer 
                return dependencyRenderer;
            }
 
            if (presentationElement instanceof PackageView)
            {
                // package renderer 
                return packageRenderer;
            }
 
            return null;
       }
    };]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Step 2. Registering RendererProvider</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4088bf97-912e-4d4b-8ae4-9d5390636717"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElementRendererManager.getInstance().addProvider(new RendererProvider());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437225 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00460: (2026x) Reshaping shape elements

- page_id: `254437225`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437225/2026x+Reshaping+shape+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:13.629+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change bounds for the existing *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement*, use the method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.reshapeShapeElement(**ShapeElement, java.awt.Rectangle)*:

```java

```

If shape has the *Autosize*****property set to TRUE, bounds will be reduced to the preferred size.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Session management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>To change bounds for the existing <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement</em>, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.reshapeShapeElement(</em><em>ShapeElement, java.awt.Rectangle)</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3986e906-8a93-4bdf-a29d-21b2689561be"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ShapeElement element = ...;
    Rectangle newBounds = new Rectangle(100,100,80,50);
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test");
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().reshapeShapeElement(element, newBounds);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>Every shape element has a preferred size. The shape size cannot be smaller than the preferred size. If you will try to set smaller bounds, these bounds will be increased to the preferred size.</div><p>If shape has the <em>Autosize<strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </strong></em>property set to TRUE, bounds will be reduced to the preferred size.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e20c1f6d-3f0d-462a-adf0-f8b0691eec4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437573 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00461: (2026x) Resource dependent plugin

- page_id: `254437573`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437573/2026x+Resource+dependent+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:45.469+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins
- labels: ['is-plugin-required', 'get-plugin-name', 'get-plugin-version']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Starting with MagicDraw version 16.6, there is a new functionality to require to load plugins for a particular project. This feature was created to avoid an incorrect data load because of missing plugins. Every plugin can provide a name and version of a plugin to be required for the correct project load. 
To become a resource dependent plugin, your plugin class must implement the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.ResourceDependentPlugin* interface.

*ResourceDependentPlugin* has three special methods:

- a isPluginRequired [*(*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/plugins/ResourceDependentPlugin.html#isPluginRequired-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*Project)* method is called on saving a project. The plugin must return true, if a given project uses resources from the plugin.
- a *getPluginName()* method should return a plugin name.
- a *getPluginVersion()*** method should return a version of the plugin.

The following example illustrates an implementation of *ResourceDependentPlugin*:

```java

```

This plugin is required for a project, if the project contains a used project "*my_profile_filename*". The plugin name and version will be saved into the project's XMI file.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Starting with MagicDraw version 16.6, there is a new functionality to require to load plugins for a particular project. This feature was created to avoid an incorrect data load because of missing plugins. Every plugin can provide a name and version of a plugin to be required for the correct project load. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />To become a resource dependent plugin, your plugin class must implement the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.ResourceDependentPlugin</em> interface.</p><p><em>ResourceDependentPlugin</em> has three special methods:</p><ul><li>a <em>isPluginRequired</em><em><a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/plugins/ResourceDependentPlugin.html#isPluginRequired-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-"><em>(</em></a><em>Project)</em></em> method is called on saving a project. The plugin must return <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">true,</em> if a given project uses resources from the plugin.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a <em>getPluginName()</em></span> method should return a plugin name. </li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a <em>getPluginVersion()</em><em> </em></span>method should return a version of the plugin. </li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The following example illustrates an implementation of <em>ResourceDependentPlugin</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53f908de-266e-4022-a6a8-91416580f831"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[package myplugin;
import ...
public class MyPlugin extends Plugin implements ResourceDependentPlugin
{
   @Override
   public void init()
   {...}
   @Override
   public boolean close()
   {...}
   @Override
   public boolean isSupported()
   {...}
 
   @Override
   public boolean isPluginRequired(Project project)
   {
      return ProjectUtilities.findAttachedProjectByName(project, "my_profile_filename") != null;
   }
   @Override
   public String getPluginName()
   {
      return this.getDescriptor().getName();
   }
   @Override
   public String getPluginVersion()
   {
      return this.getDescriptor().getVersion();
   }
}


]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This plugin is required for a project, if the project contains a used project &quot;<em>my_profile_filename</em>&quot;. The plugin name and version will be saved into the project's XMI file.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437614 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00462: (2026x) Resource manager descriptor file

- page_id: `254437614`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437614/2026x+Resource+manager+descriptor+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:48.271+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Distributing Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you are planning to import and export your resources with the Resource Manager, you should read this section. For importing resources to your modeling tool, the resource manager descriptor file should be created. In this section you will find information about the descriptor file naming, location, and a sample of this file.

For more information about importing resources with Resource Manager, see*MagicDraw UserManual.pdf*.

##### Resource Manager descriptor file naming

The Resource Manager descriptor file has a specific naming. The structure of the name is*MDR_<type>_<plugin name>_<plugin id>_descriptor.xml.*For example, *MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xm**l*.

All spaces are replaced with the underscore symbol “_“.

##### Resource Manager descriptor file location

The file location is*<modeling tool installation directory>\data\resourcemanager*.

##### Resource Manager descriptor file content and sample

This section presents a basic sample of the Resource Manager descriptor file structure. This sample represents a plugin distribution, which also includes a custom diagram, profile, template, sample, and documentation.

You can also distribute custom diagrams, profiles, templates, or samples separately, you only need to change the “*type*“ value.

```xml
Enterprise
    Architect
    Standard
    Professional Java
    Professional C++
    Professional C#
    Professional ArcStyler
    Reader
    OptimalJ
 
    
        
        
        
        
        
        
        

    
]]>
```

The following table describes the terms used in the sample:

| Element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | A unique plugin id. id is used to form a descriptor file name. This id must be unique. To prevent duplicates, use a number starting from 3000 or contact the No Magic support at http://www.nomagic.com/support.html. |
| name | A plugin name. A name is used to form a descriptor file name. The plugin name must be unique between all program resources. |
| product | A product name. Used only for the “Plugin (commercial)” resource type. |
| type | A type may be one of the following types: Custom Diagram, Plugin, Plugin (commercial), Profile, Sample, Template. |
| version resource | This number is used to identify the resource build/release. For example, a new resource build must be released, while the resource human and internal versions should not change. A suggested value is a version internal value multiplied by 10. version resource should be a number. |
| version internal | This version number is not visible to the user and may be used for an internal count. version internal should be a number. |
| version human | A human readable version number of the resource. This version number is visible to users. The version human number may consist of numbers and/or words. |
| edition | Supported program editions. |
| installation | installation includes files, which will be copied from the custom plugin archive to the program folder.Do not use "*.*" ! If a file name includes “*.*“, all defined files will be removed when uninstalling the plugin. For example, if "samples/*.*" is defined, all files from the “samples” folder will be removed after uninstalling the resource. |
| critical | If the resource is selected as critical, it cannot be removed because MagicDraw does not allow this. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>If you are planning to import and export your resources with the Resource Manager, you should read this section. For importing resources to your modeling tool, the resource manager descriptor file should be created. In this section you will find information about the descriptor file naming, location, and a sample of this file. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74e6be16-6307-406c-919c-b44b0ffc07f0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>For more information about importing resources with Resource Manager, see </span><em>MagicDraw UserManual.pdf</em><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><h3>Resource Manager descriptor file naming</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The Resource Manager descriptor file has a specific naming. The structure of the name is </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MDR_&lt;type&gt;_&lt;plugin name&gt;_&lt;plugin id&gt;_descriptor.xml.  </em>For example, <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xm</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">l</em>.</p><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f1661a8-55a6-42e9-9e03-aab847223bd8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>  All spaces are replaced with the underscore symbol “_“.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><h3>Resource Manager descriptor file location</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The file location is </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\data\resourcemanager</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">.</span></p><p> </p><h3>Resource Manager descriptor file content and sample</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">This section presents a basic sample of the Resource Manager descriptor file structure. This sample represents a plugin distribution, which also includes a custom diagram, profile, template, sample, and documentation.</span></p><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">You can also distribute custom diagrams, profiles, templates, or samples separately, you only need to change the “</span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">type</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">“ value.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dc3b1dc-2ac2-4410-93a8-813a41720afb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<resourceDescriptor 
    critical="false" 
    date="2007-12-24+02:00" 
    description="My Plug-in"
    homePage="http://www.nomagic.com" 
    id="3001"
    mdVersionMax="higher" 
    mdVersionMin="15.0" 
    name="CustomPlugin" 
    product="CustomPlugin" 
    type="Plugin">
 
    <version human="1.0 beta" internal="1" resource="10"/>
    <provider name="No Magic" />
 
    <edition>Enterprise</edition>
    <edition>Architect</edition>
    <edition>Standard</edition>
    <edition>Professional Java</edition>
    <edition>Professional C++</edition>
    <edition>Professional C#</edition>
    <edition>Professional ArcStyler</edition>
    <edition>Reader</edition>
    <edition>OptimalJ</edition>
 
    <installation>
        <file from="data/defaults/data/diagrams/CustomDiagram/descriptor.xml" to="data/defaults/data/diagrams/CustomDiagram/descriptor.xml" />
        <file from="profiles/CustomProfile.xml.zip" to="profiles/CustomProfile.xml.zip" />
        <file from="templates/CustomTemplate.xml.zip" to="templates/CustomTemplate.xml.zip" />
        <file from="samples/CustomPluginSample.mdzip" to="samples/CustomPluginSample.mdzip" />
        <file from="manual/CustomPluginManual.pdf" to="manual/CustomPluginManual.pdf" />
        <file from="plugins/customPlugin/*.*" to="plugins/customPlugin/*.*" />
        <file from="data/resourcemanager/MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xml" to="data/resourcemanager/MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xml" />

    </installation>
</resourceDescriptor>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following table describes the terms used in the sample:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Element</p></th><th><p>Description</p></th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>id</strong></p></td><td><p>A unique plugin id. <em>id </em>is used to form a descriptor file name. This id must be unique. To prevent duplicates, use a number starting from 3000 or contact the No Magic support at <a href="http://www.nomagic.com/support.html">http://www.nomagic.com/support.html</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>name</strong></p></td><td><p>A plugin name. A name is used to form a descriptor file name. The plugin name must be unique between all program resources.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>product</strong></p></td><td><p>A product name. Used only for the “Plugin (commercial)” resource type.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>type</strong></p></td><td><p>A type may be one of the following types: Custom Diagram, Plugin, Plugin (commercial), Profile, Sample, Template.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>version resource</strong></p></td><td><p>This number is used to identify the resource build/release. For example, a new resource build must be released, while the resource human and internal versions should not change. A suggested value is a <em>version internal </em>value multiplied by 10. <em>version resource </em>should be a number.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>version internal</strong></p></td><td><p>This version number is not visible to the user and may be used for an internal count. <em>version internal </em>should be a number.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>version human</strong></p></td><td><p>A human readable version number of the resource. This version number is visible to users. The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">version human </em>number may consist of numbers and/or words.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>edition</strong></p></td><td><p>Supported program editions.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>installation</strong></p></td><td><p><em>installation </em>includes files, which will be copied from the custom plugin archive to the program folder.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="227c4851-c138-46de-84f3-55f8f05f6997"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not use &quot;*.*&quot; ! If a file name includes “*.*“, all defined files will be removed when uninstalling the plugin. For example, if &quot;samples/*.*&quot; is defined, all files from the “samples” folder will be removed after uninstalling the resource. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>critical</strong></td><td colspan="1">If the resource is selected as critical, it cannot be removed because MagicDraw does not allow this.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00463: (2026x) REST API Change Log

- page_id: `254437390`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437390/2026x+REST+API+Change+Log
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:25.163+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x) REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Changed data types

###### arrayOfUuid

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/workspaces
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/tags
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/elements
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/revisions/{revision}/elements
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/elements
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/elements
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/tags
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/elements
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/elements
- /osmc/admin/usergroups
- /osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/resync

###### arrayOfString

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/locks
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/locks
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/users
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/locks
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/locks
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/users
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/roles/{roleId}/users
- /osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/resync

###### osmc/admin/config/put

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/admin/config/{key}

###### osmc/admin/roles/roleID/userGroups/post

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/usergroups
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/usergroups
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/usergroups
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/roles/{roleId}/usergroups

###### osmc/admin/license/apply/put

Parameter “port” removed.

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/admin/license/apply

##### Changed endpoints

###### /osmc/admin/license/query/{licenseServer}

###### GET

Query parameters added:

| Name | Type | Remark |
| --- | --- | --- |
| licenseFramework | String | The licensing framework (DSLS or FlexNet) |
| licensingMode | String | The licensing mode (ORGANIZATION_DEFINED or CUSTOM, for DSLS only) |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437175 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00464: (2026x) REST APIs

- page_id: `254437175`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437175/2026x+REST+APIs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:10.084+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Teamwork Cloud provides a set of REST APIs for server management. REST APIs are essential properties in Teamwork Cloud that allow you to query data about user accounts, roles, projects, and that provide access and basic operations for the resources. REST API also provides an administrator-friendly way to manage the server because it can be run in the text mode and in a shell script.

The documentation of REST API is provided in Swagger, available with Teamwork Cloud installation, where you can find the description of the APIs inboth machine-readable and human-readable formats. Teamwork Cloud REST API documentation in Swagger is accessible at [https://osmc.nomagic.com](https://osmc.nomagic.com/). The API document is also bundled with Teamwork Cloud installation. You can access it from https://<Teamwork Cloud IP>:8111/osmc/swagger.

Having Swagger come with the Teamwork Cloud installation makes it the most convenient way to issue REST API to the server. It allows logging in and calling the server from a web browser. While calling to the server, Swagger also shows a cURL command that the user can copy to and execute in the command line or use in a batch script instead.

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

REST APIs are essential properties in Teamwork Cloud that allow you to query data about user accounts, roles, projects, and that provide access and basic operations for the resources. REST API also provides an administrator-friendly way to manage the server because it can be run in the text mode and in a shell script.</p><p>The documentation of REST API is provided in Swagger, available with Teamwork Cloud installation, where you can find the description of the APIs in<span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"> both machine-readable and human-readable formats.</span> Teamwork Cloud REST API documentation in Swagger is accessible at <a href="https://osmc.nomagic.com/">https://osmc.nomagic.com</a>. The API document is also bundled with Teamwork Cloud installation. You can access it from <span style="color: rgb(0,0,128);">https://&lt;Teamwork Cloud IP&gt;:8111/osmc/swagger</span>. </p><p>Having Swagger come with the Teamwork Cloud installation makes it the most convenient way to issue REST API to the server. It allows logging in and calling the server from a web browser. While calling to the server, Swagger also shows a cURL command that the user can copy to and execute in the command line or use in a batch script instead.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="808393af-a8e7-4889-a3c1-4d4c7eeabe89" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437506 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00466: (2026x) Retrieving meta information about element

- page_id: `254437506`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437506/2026x+Retrieving+meta+information+about+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:37.045+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every Element is identified by it's "metaclass". Element metaclass is mapped into java interface (for example *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class*).

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.getClassType()* to retrieve the element metaclass.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassTypes* for managing the metaclasses - collecting all available, collecting derived and etc.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Every Element is identified by it's &quot;metaclass&quot;. Element metaclass is mapped into java interface (for example <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class</em>). </p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.getClassType()</em> to retrieve the element metaclass.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassTypes</em> for managing the metaclasses - collecting all available, collecting derived and etc.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437370 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00467: (2026x) Reusing Teamwork Cloud session

- page_id: `254437370`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437370/2026x+Reusing+Teamwork+Cloud+session
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:24.305+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x) REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When using cURL, user credentials (user name/password or a token) can be provided in a command-line argument. A new session is created during authentication. It is **very** important to note that a Teamwork Cloud session remains open after a REST API returns. This implies that a Teamwork Cloud session is not closed automatically. If the user’s credentials are still in use (without sending a valid cookie), the license count will be used up, and new sessions cannot be created. The user needs to wait until the session is timed out. The default timeout is 15 minutes.

The correct way to use REST API is to log in only once, reuse the session for all subsequent calls, and log out once finished.

The following example is a Bash script used for creating a comma-separated list of email addresses for all users in Teamwork Cloud, to facilitate notification sending.

```text
users.json
sed 's/\"\,\"/\n/g' users.json > users.txt
sed -i 's/\[\"//g' users.txt
sed -i 's/\"\]//g' users.txt
while read username; do
		curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL/$username" -H "accept: application/json" >> email.txt
done < users.txt
curl -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt $LOGOUTURL
grep -E -o "\b[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\b" email.txt > email.lst
sed -i 's/\n/;/g' email.lst
sort email.lst | uniq > email_unique.lst
tr '\n' ',' < email_unique.lst > email_list.csv
truncate -s-1 email_list.csv

]]>
```

The first execution of cURL logs in to the server. The server returns a session ID in a cookie. The cookie is saved into the **cookie.txt** file. The remaining cURL calls send the cookie back to the server to reuse the open session.

The example below shows how to use PowerShell to create internal users from a CSV file.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When using cURL, user credentials (user name/password or a token) can be provided in a command-line argument. A new session is created during authentication. It is <strong>very</strong> important to note that a Teamwork Cloud session remains open after a REST API returns. This implies that a Teamwork Cloud session is not closed automatically. If the user’s credentials are still in use (without sending a valid cookie), the license count will be used up, and new sessions cannot be created. The user needs to wait until the session is timed out. The default timeout is 15 minutes.</p><p>The correct way to use REST API is to log in only once, reuse the session for all subsequent calls, and log out once finished.</p><p>The following example is a Bash script used for creating a comma-separated list of email addresses for all users in Teamwork Cloud, to facilitate notification sending.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="154a6874-c10f-4833-bd14-899a0068625c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash
# Script to export a unique list of email address for all users in Teamwork Cloud into a singe CSV list (email_list.csv)
# Author:  Benjamin Krajmalnik
#
# 
SERVER=127.0.0.1
RESTBASEURL=https://$SERVER:8111/osmc
USERURL=${RESTBASEURL}/admin/users
LOGINURL=${RESTBASEURL}/login
LOGOUTURL=${RESTBASEURL}/logout
username=Administrator
password=Administrator
rm -f email*.*
curl -k -s -c cookie.txt --insecure -u $username:$password $LOGINURL
curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL" -H "accept: application/json" > users.json
sed 's/\"\,\"/\n/g' users.json > users.txt
sed -i 's/\[\"//g' users.txt
sed -i 's/\"\]//g' users.txt
while read username; do
		curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL/$username" -H "accept: application/json" >> email.txt
done < users.txt
curl -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt $LOGOUTURL
grep -E -o "\b[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\b" email.txt > email.lst
sed -i 's/\n/;/g' email.lst
sort email.lst | uniq > email_unique.lst
tr '\n' ',' < email_unique.lst > email_list.csv
truncate -s-1 email_list.csv

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The first execution of cURL logs in to the server. The server returns a session ID in a cookie. The cookie is saved into the <strong>cookie.txt</strong> file. The remaining cURL calls send the cookie back to the server to reuse the open session.</p><p>The example below shows how to use PowerShell to create internal users from a CSV file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bcf44a99-0ec2-4a02-8871-c1632d46fd92"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# -----------------------------------------------------------------
# Script to import users from CSV file into Teamwork Cloud
# Author:  Benjamin Krajmalnik
# Format: non-quoted CSV, no headers
#  userLogin,userPassword,fullUserName,departmentName,emailAddress
#
# Usage:
# PowerShell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ImportUsersFromCSV.ps1
# -----------------------------------------------------------------
Add-Type @"
  using System.Net;
  using System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates;
  public class TrustAllCertificatesPolicy : ICertificatePolicy 
  {
     public bool CheckValidationResult
     (
       ServicePoint srvPoint, X509Certificate certificate
       ,
       WebRequest request, int certificateProblem
     ) 
     {
       return true;
     }
  }
"@
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::CheckCertificateRevocationList = $false;
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::ServerCertificateValidationCallback += { $true; };
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::CertificatePolicy = New-Object TrustAllCertificatesPolicy
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [System.Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12
$Server = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Server Name or IP address'
$Admin = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Administrator User'
$Passwd = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Server Administrator Password'
$Userfile = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the full path name of the file containing the users'
$RESTBASEURL = "https://"+$Server+":8111/osmc/"
$ADDUSERURL = $RESTBASEURL + "admin/users"
$LOGINURL = $RESTBASEURL   + "login"
$LOGOUTURL = $RESTBASEURL + "logout"
$pair="$($Admin):$($Passwd)"
$bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes($pair)
$base64 = [System.Convert]::ToBase64String($bytes)
$basicAuthValue = "Basic $base64"
$headers = @{ Authorization = $basicAuthValue }
$RestError = $null
Try {
$response = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "$LOGINURL" -Method Post -Headers $headers -SessionVariable 'Session'
} Catch {
	$RestError = $_
}
foreach($line in [System.IO.File]::ReadLines($Userfile))
{
                  $login,$password,$username,$department,$email = $line.split(',').trim()
                  
                  $JSON=@"
                  {
                                             "userName": "$login",
                                             "password": "$password",
                                             "otherAttributes": {
                                                            "mobile": "",
                                                            "name": "$username",
                                                            "department": "$department",
                                                            "email": "$email"
                                             },
                                             "enabled": true
                              }
"@
                              $response = Invoke-WebRequest  -Uri "$ADDUSERURL" -Method Post -Body $JSON -ContentType "application/json"  -WebSession $Session
}
 
$response = Invoke-WebRequest  -Uri "$LOGOUTURL" –Method Get -WebSession $Session]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437660 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00468: (2026x) Running action with progress

- page_id: `254437660`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437660/2026x+Running+action+with+progress
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:50.483+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Adding new functionality
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For long lasting actions, there is a need to show a progress and run the asynchronous code.

There are two options to execute these actions:

- a foreground task - use com.nomagic.task.RunnableWithProgress or com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.MagicDrawProgressStatusRunner
- a background task - use com.nomagic.magicdraw.task.BackgroundTaskRunner

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling tool**installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\progressstatus*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Multi-threading' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>For long lasting actions, there is a need to show a progress and run the asynchronous code.</p><p>There are two options to execute these actions:</p><ul><li>a foreground task - use <em>com.nomagic.task.RunnableWithProgress</em> or <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.MagicDrawProgressStatusRunner</em></li><li>a background task - use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.task.BackgroundTaskRunner</em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;<em>modeling tool</em> </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">installation direc</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">tory&gt;\openapi\examples\progressstatus</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74b88a6d-75d0-4f9c-9077-d7b780313a90"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Multi-threading" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437299 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00469: (2026x) Running programs in batch mode

- page_id: `254437299`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437299/2026x+Running+programs+in+batch+mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:19.571+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are cases when custom task must be executed in the batch (command line) mode. For example, if you want to run MagicDraw or other modeling tool application, open some project, execute code generation and exit application.

API provides two ways to run the tool in the batch mode depending on the required functionality.

1. Functionality is provided by an application core. You need to extend the com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine class.
2. Functionality is provided by a plugin. Plugin loading is managed by the application core so it is undesirable to add core and plugin classes into the same class path. The different approach accessing plugin functionality is used. You need to implement the com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction interface.

****You can find the code examples in

1. <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\*imagegenerator* . It takes a project file and destination directory as arguments and generates images for all diagrams.
2. <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin . Just prints the program arguments to the application log file.

The application cannot be run on the headless device even in a batch mode. The graphical environment is required.

For more information on the launching batch mode programs, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties' space=''].

For more information on creation of batch mode programs, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Implementing command line launchers' space=''].

More information is available in javadoc.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You need to extend the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine</em> class.</li><li>Functionality is provided by a plugin. You need to implement the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction</em> interface.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ol><li><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\<em>imagegenerator</em></em>. ### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5478fe15-51af-4122-aaa4-1a0111f53781" /></h3><h3>Performing the forward engineering</h3><p>The example shows how to perform a simple java code generation.</p><p><strong>Step #1. Creating <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c8fe96a-5d37-4773-afd7-e09335dc20e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject(); 
    String name = "sample CE project";
    String workingDir = OPENAPI_DATA_DIRECTORY_PATH;
 
    // create a working package
    ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
    Package workingPackage = ef.createPackageInstance();
    workingPackage.setName("my working package");
    workingPackage.setOwner(project.getModel());
 
    // creating a code engineering set
    CodeEngineeringSet javaGenerationSet = CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(
				CodeEngineeringConstants.Languages.JAVA, null, name, project, workingPackage, workingDir);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Step #2. Adding model elements to <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="142f730a-857e-439e-91e1-e2d9f727c166"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject();
 
 	// create a new element
 	ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
 	Class classA = ef.createClassInstance();
 	classA.setName("ClassA");
	classA.setOwner(project.getModel());
	List<BaseElement> modelsForSample = new ArrayList<BaseElement>();
	modelsForSample.add(classA);
	javaGenerationSet.addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet(modelsForSample);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><strong>Step #3. Performing the <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em> generation</strong></div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1a7d6f5-8cf4-4795-960a-8fc7e1d2d6d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    CodeEngineeringManager.generate(javaGenerationSet);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Performing the reverse engineering</h3><p>The example shows how to perform a simple java code reverse.</p><p><strong>Step #1. Creating <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9933ea61-dd6a-4c46-b658-9eef350f9f35"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject(); 
    String name = "sample CE project";
    String workingDir = OPENAPI_DATA_DIRECTORY_PATH;// e.g C:\myworkingPackage
 
    // create a working package
    ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
    Package workingPackage = ef.createPackageInstance();
    workingPackage.setName("my working package");
    workingPackage.setOwner(project.getModel());
 
    // creating a code engineering set
    CodeEngineeringSet ces = CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(
				CodeEngineeringConstants.Languages.JAVA, null, name, project, workingPackage, workingDir);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Here the null dialect is used for the java language, because java doesn't have any dialect.</p><p><strong>Step #2. Adding the source code to <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eccd7ee9-f67e-4c1a-95af-e288323986bd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ 	ces.addAllFilesRecursivelyToCES(new File(workingDir + File.separator + "test directory")); // starting from C:\myworkingPackage\test directory\]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This sets the given instance of the code engineering set working directory and adds all files from that directory. Set java classpaths for the project:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6ba7648-854c-4ea6-a859-f54175d7b845"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    String[] claspath = new String[] { path1, path2, path3, path4 }; 
    JavaCodeEngineeringManager.setJavaClasspath(project, claspath);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Step #3. Performing the reverse of <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d41202c-94aa-42f4-bf2e-6a98fb396354"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[     CodeEngineeringManager.reverse(ces, false);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437238 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00471: (2026x) Script validation rule

- page_id: `254437238`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437238/2026x+Script+validation+rule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:14.382+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Groovy, Javascript, or Jython scripting language can be used to create a validation rule which are used to validate elements in your model. The advantage of using script validation rules is that it is not necessary to compile and store them in a classpath. Oftentimes, the code is compact and simple.

###### How to create a a script validation rule

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Constraints' space='MT'].
2. Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.
3. Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.
4. Specify constrained element(s).
5. Specify the specification language (Groovy, Javascript, or Jython).
6. Enter the validation code in the selected language.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating validation rules' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Groovy, Javascript, or Jython scripting language can be used to create a validation rule which are used to validate elements in your model. The advantage of using script validation rules is that it is not necessary to <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="448794bc-8c8a-476a-8727-833273737189">compile and store them</ac:inline-comment-marker> in a classpath. <span>Oftentimes,</span> the code is compact and simple.</p><p> </p><h4>How to create a  a script validation rule</h4><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b66a4fbf-3ce3-492a-a322-9533858e6cfc"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Constraints" ri:space-key="MT" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a constraint]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.</li><li>Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.</li><li>Specify constrained element(s).</li><li>Specify the specification language (Groovy, Javascript, or Jython).</li><li>Enter the validation code in the selected language.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f368d5fe-805a-447b-a01b-fca122ddd589"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation" ri:space-key="MT" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating validation rules" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437443 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00473: (2026x) Session management

- page_id: `254437443`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437443/2026x+Session+management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:31.321+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Working with UML model
- labels: ['create-session', 'close-session']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager* is the singleton manager used for editing model Elements. All modifications to model elements should be performed between the *createSession(Project, java.lang.String)*and *closeSession(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/openapi/uml/SessionManager.html#closeSession-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*method calls.

To edit some *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*, a session with this manager must be created. After editing a model element, a session must be closed. After that, all changes will be applied to the model and registered in the command history (for undo/redo) as one command with a session name. Only one session can be active.

The following code can be used for accessing, checking, and creating the session:

```java

```

If other session is already created and not closed*,*the *createSession(Project, java.lang.String)* method throws the *java.lang.IllegalStateException* runtime exception.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager</em> is the singleton manager used for editing model Elements. All modifications to model elements should be performed between the <em>createSession(Project, java.lang.String) </em>and <em>closeSession(Project<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/openapi/uml/SessionManager.html#closeSession-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-">)</a> </em>method calls.</p><p>To edit some <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em>, a session with this manager must be created. After editing a model element, a session must be closed. After that, all changes will be applied to the model and registered in the command history (for undo/redo) as one command with a session name. Only one session can be active.</p><p>The following code can be used for accessing, checking, and creating the session:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36c9feac-4bc9-49c3-9d17-a0ca33e20401"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // access a singleton instance by using getInstance()
    // only one session can be active, so check this.
    if (!SessionManager.getInstance().isSessionCreated(project))
    {
        // create a new session. 
        SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Edit");
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If other session is already created and not closed<em>, </em>the <em>createSession(Project, java.lang.String) </em> method throws the <em>java.lang.IllegalStateException</em> runtime exception.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00475: (2026x) Showing Element seletion dialog

- page_id: `254437119`

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437119/2026x+Showing+Element+seletion+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:04.270+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Using and extending other UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### **Selectin****g Elements via Element Selection Dialog**

```java

```

You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>/openapi/examples/elementselection*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h4><strong>Selectin</strong><strong>g Elements via Element Selection Dialog</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cda0261-8c8f-4386-b713-bff08cdad38c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // Use ElementSelectionDlgFactory.create(...) methods to create the element selection dialog.
    Frame dialogParent = MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent(); 
    ElementSelectionDlg dlg = ElementSelectionDlgFactory.create(dialogParent);
 
    // Use ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initSingle(...) methods to initialize the dialog with a single element selection mode.
    ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initSingle(....);
 
    // Use ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initMultiple(...) methods to initialize the dialog with a multiple element selection mode. 
    ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initMultiple(....);
 
    // Display the dialog for the user to select elements. 
    dlg.setVisible(true);
 
    // Check if the user has clicked "Ok". 
    if (dlg.isOkClicked())
    {
         // Get the selected element in a single selection mode. 
        BaseElement selected = dlg.getSelectedElement();
 
        // Get selected elements in a multiple selection mode. 
        BaseElement selected = dlg.getSelectedElements();
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65809b8c-f725-4fb0-8cf4-7b92378b5837"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;/openapi/examples/elementselection</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437208 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00476: (2026x) Showing notifications, adding text into Message Window

- page_id: `254437208`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437208/2026x+Showing+notifications+adding+text+into+Message+Window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:12.483+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Using and extending other UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use API described bellow to show Notifications (a balloon windows on the bottom right corner of application window) and appending text into a Message Window.

**NotificationManager**

This is an entry point to display notifications in MagicDraw. Two types of notifications can be displayed: application level and container level. Container lever notifications are displayed in special container that implements *com.nomagic.ui.notification.NotificationsContainer* interface.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.NotificationManage.*

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.GUILog.log(java.lang.String) t*o add some information into a Message Window,

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.Notification.getContext()* controls where notification is logged in the Message Window - into Environment or Project tab.

****

**GUILog**

Use *GUILog.log(java.lang.String)* to put a text into Project Message Window message.

Use *GUILog.log(Notification, boolean)* to log information from notification into a Message Window.

Use *GUILog.openLog()* to open Message Window.

For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>**/openapi/examples/notifications*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Showing question, error, warning dialogs' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use API described bellow to show Notifications (a balloon windows on the bottom right corner of application window) and appending text into a Message Window.</p><p><strong>NotificationManager</strong></p><p>This is an entry point to display notifications in MagicDraw. Two types of notifications can be displayed: application level and container level. Container lever notifications are displayed in special container that implements <em>com.nomagic.ui.notification.NotificationsContainer</em> interface. </p><p>Use <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.NotificationManage. </em></p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.GUILog.log(java.lang.String) t</em>o add some information into a Message Window,</p><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.Notification.getContext()</em> controls where notification is logged in the Message Window - into Environment or Project tab.</p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">GUILog</strong></p><p>Use <em>GUILog.log(java.lang.String)</em> to put a text into Project Message Window message.</p><p>Use <em>GUILog.log(Notification, boolean)</em> to log information from notification into a Message Window.</p><p>Use <em>GUILog.openLog()</em> to open Message Window.</p><p><br /></p><p>For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em><em>/openapi/examples/notifications</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c6dfaab-2bb5-4c81-aaca-b9acf1614fa2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Showing question, error, warning dialogs" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437121 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00477: (2026x) Showing question, error, warning dialogs

- page_id: `254437121`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437121/2026x+Showing+question+error+warning+dialogs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:04.924+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Using and extending other UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.GUILog* class to show various simple UI dialogs.

**Error dialog**

Use *GUILog.showError(java.lang.String)*

Use*GUILog.showError(java.awt.Frame, java.lang.String, java.lang.Throwable)*to show error dialog with exception stack trace

**Warning dialog**

Use *GUILog.showWarning(java.lang.String)*

**Question dialog**

Use *GUILog.showQuestion(java.lang.String)*

**Message dialog**

Use *GUILog.showMessage(java.lang.String)*

Use *GUILog.showHTMLMessage(java.lang.String,**GUILog.URLActionHandler)* to show html message with some active links

**Log messages into Message Window**

Use *GUILog.log(java.lang.String) t*o add some information into a Message Window

See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Showing notifications, adding text into Message Window' space=''] for more information about showing Notifications and appending text into a Message Window

For more information**about other API choices see javadoc*.*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Showing notifications, adding text into Message Window' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

## PAGE 00478: (2026x) Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties

- page_id: `254437301`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437301/2026x+Specifying+batch+mode+program+classpath+and+required+system+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:19.735+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Running programs in batch mode
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw or other modeling tools run as a simple Java program.

In order to launch the program from the command line, the launcher is started using a *java* command line. The classpath has to include jar files from the lib folder and its subfolders (additional requirements exist for different command line implementations).

There is one mandatory property that has to be specified as well:

- -Desi.system.config=data/application.conf

The property is specified as a relative path to*<modeling tool installation directory>, therefore,* you either have to make your current directory before launching your program or specify absolute paths for the above-listed properties.

There are two types of command lines.

1. Core related batch program: A program that does not call/depend on any functionality from any plugins. Should only depend/call core tool functionality.
  - Must extend *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine*
  - Must contain the Java *main* method and implement *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine#execute*
2. Plugin related batch program: A program that can call/depend on any running modeling tool plugin
  - `com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action` - specifies the batch mode action (implementation of *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction*) you want to execute, for example, `-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample`
  - Must launch using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher*
  - The *CommandLineAction* must be registered in *CommandLineActionManager.getInstance().addAction(new CommandLineActionExample());* e.g. in your plugin *init()* method
  - The plugin where *CommandLineAction* is registered must load and run, in order for the command line program to launch it. (this means plugin.xml and plugin .jar files must be in the /plugins directory)

Also, it is possible to set up the command line to use the floating license server or license file - see "Providing licensing information" at [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Licensing information' space=''].

The implemented examples can be found in :

- Core batch program: <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\imagegenerator
- Plugin batch program: <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin

The examples of complete command lines that launch the batch mode programs:

```text
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/*;openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]>
```

```text
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/* ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]>
```

```bash
cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-cp lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/*\:openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]>
```

```bash
cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-cp lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/* \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]>
```

You can modify the previous examples and create more sophisticated batch files. For example, you can define the directory to run the tool from, or specify to use the same java, as the tool uses itself. The exemplary scripts that can be used to start the command line program are shown in the following script examples. The scripts read and use the same classpath as the modeling tool when launching it manually(this is the preferred way to define classpaths).

These scripts can be launched from any directory. Can be modified to read other properties files (e.g. csm.properties, cea.properties)

A script that starts the Windows Core batch command line program on Windows:

```text

A script that starts the Windows Plugin batch command line program on Windows:

The shell script that starts a Plugin batch command line program on Mac OS-X, Linux, and Windows cygwin/msys:
