````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw or other modeling tools run as a simple Java program.</p><p>In order to launch the program from the command line, the launcher is started using a <em>java</em> command line. The classpath has to include jar files from the lib folder and its subfolders (additional requirements exist for different command line implementations).</p><p>There is one mandatory property that has to be specified as well:</p><ul><li><code>-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf</code></li></ul><p>The property is specified as a relative path to<em> &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;, therefore,</em> you either have to make your current directory before launching your program or specify absolute paths for the above-listed properties.</p><p>There are two types of command lines.</p><ol><li>Core related batch program:<br />A program that does not call/depend on any functionality from any plugins. Should only depend/call core tool functionality.<br /><ul><li><p>Must extend <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine</em></p></li><li><p>Must contain the Java <em>main</em> method and implement <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine#execute</em></p></li></ul></li><li>Plugin related batch program:<br />A program that can call/depend on any running modeling tool plugin<br /><ul><li><p><code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action</code> - specifies the batch mode action (implementation of <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction</em>) you want to execute, for example, <code>-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample</code> </p></li><li><p>Must launch using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher</em> </p></li><li><p>The <em>CommandLineAction</em> must be registered in <em>CommandLineActionManager.getInstance().addAction(<span style="color: rgb(0,0,128);">new <span>CommandLineActionExample</span></span>());</em> e.g. in your plugin <em>init()</em> method</p></li><li><p>The plugin where <em>CommandLineAction</em> is registered must load and run, in order for the command line program to launch it. (this means plugin.xml and plugin .jar files must be in the /plugins directory)</p></li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Also, it is possible to set up the command line to use the floating license server or license file - see &quot;Providing licensing information&quot; at <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Licensing information" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The implemented examples can be found in :</p><ul><li>Core batch program: <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\imagegenerator</em></li><li>Plugin batch program: <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin</em></li></ul><p>The examples of complete command lines that launch the batch mode programs:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99475978-37bf-4c51-bc15-1f59efb94d7f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Core batch program command line for Windows</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\Program Files\MagicDraw>java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/*;openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68eeab44-55ae-4fbd-a0c6-034bea80270a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Plugin batch program command line for Windows</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\Program Files\MagicDraw>java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/* ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63cfa508-e9b8-49ee-b849-2f19c8a54bf4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Core batch program command line for Mac OS-X and Linux</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[> cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-cp lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/*\:openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee546ddc-2f6b-40a3-b63f-52533f990fbb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Plugin batch program command line for Mac OS-X and Linux</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[> cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-cp lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/* \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>You can modify the previous examples and create more sophisticated batch files. For example, you can define the directory to run the tool from, or specify to use the same java, as the tool uses itself. The exemplary scripts that can be used to start the command line program are shown in the following script examples. The scripts read and use the same classpath as the modeling tool when launching it manually(this is the preferred way to define classpaths). </p><p>These scripts can be launched from any directory. Can be modified to read other properties files (e.g. csm.properties, cea.properties)</p><p><br /></p><p>A script that starts the Windows Core batch command line program on Windows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dce90f91-c03a-4ceb-9799-f0f15ffdcb23"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Core batch program Windows batch file that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[@echo off
setlocal EnableExtensions
 
:: either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
set "MAGICDRAW_HOME=C:\Program Files\MagicDraw"

if "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%" == "" (
    echo MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder
    exit /B 1
)

setlocal enableDelayedExpansion
:: change slashes if needed
set MAGICDRAW_HOME=!MAGICDRAW_HOME:\=/!
setlocal disableDelayedExpansion
  
pushd "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%" 
  
:: Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
For /F "tokens=1* delims==" %%A IN (bin\magicdraw.properties) DO (
    IF "%%A"=="CLASSPATH" set MD_CLASSPATH=%%B
)

:: Modify classpath to a valid Windows style classpath. Replace \: with ;
set MD_CLASSPATH=%MD_CLASSPATH:\:=;%
  
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
     -cp "%MD_CLASSPATH%;openapi\examples\imagegenerator\imagegenerator.jar" ^
	 -Desi.system.config="data\application.conf" ^
     -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
     @bin/vm.options ^
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages %*
	 
popd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>A script that starts the Windows Plugin batch command line program on Windows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6a8e4ea4-9487-4856-a3be-e70f1477257a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Plugin batch program Windows batch file that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[@echo off
setlocal EnableExtensions
  
:: either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
set "MAGICDRAW_HOME=C:\Program Files\MagicDraw"
 
if "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%" == "" (
    echo MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder
    exit /B 1
)
 
setlocal enableDelayedExpansion
:: change slashes if needed
set MAGICDRAW_HOME=!MAGICDRAW_HOME:\=/!
setlocal disableDelayedExpansion
   
pushd "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%"
   
:: Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
For /F "tokens=1* delims==" %%A IN (bin\magicdraw.properties) DO (
    IF "%%A"=="CLASSPATH" set MD_CLASSPATH=%%B
)
 
:: Modify classpath to a valid Windows style classpath. Replace \: with ;
set MD_CLASSPATH=%MD_CLASSPATH:\:=;%
   
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
     -cp "%MD_CLASSPATH%" ^
     -Desi.system.config="data\application.conf" ^
	 -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
     @bin/vm.options ^
     -Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample ^
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher %*
      
popd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The shell script that starts a Core batch command line program on Mac OS-X, Linux, and Windows cygwin/msys:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5be5d473-9a0b-48d2-a3c0-0a76396dbbe3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Core batch program shell script that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash

# either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
MAGICDRAW_HOME="/home/myuser/Desktop/MagicDraw"

if [ -z "$MAGICDRAW_HOME" ]; then
    echo "MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder"
    return
fi
 
cd "$MAGICDRAW_HOME"

# Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
MD_CLASSPATH=`grep "CLASSPATH" bin/magicdraw.properties | cut -d'=' -f 2`

if [ "$OS" = Windows_NT ]; then
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/;/g")
    cp_delim=";"
else
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/:/g")
    cp_delim=":"
fi
 
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
     -cp "$MD_CLASSPATH${cp_delim}openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar" \
     -Desi.system.config="data/application.conf" \
     -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
     @bin/vm.options \
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages "$@"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The shell script that starts a Plugin batch command line program on Mac OS-X, Linux, and Windows cygwin/msys:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85328b8b-15bf-4f75-8650-aaf9919a4c60"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Plugin batch program shell script that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash

# either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
MAGICDRAW_HOME="/home/myuser/Desktop/MagicDraw"

if [ -z "$MAGICDRAW_HOME" ]; then
    echo "MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder"
    return
fi
 
cd "$MAGICDRAW_HOME"

# Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
MD_CLASSPATH=`grep "CLASSPATH" bin/magicdraw.properties | cut -d'=' -f 2`

if [ "$OS" = Windows_NT ]; then
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/;/g")
else
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/:/g")
fi
 
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
     -cp "$MD_CLASSPATH" \
     -Desi.system.config="data/application.conf" \
     -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
     @bin/vm.options \
     -Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample \
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher "$@"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Licensing information" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437338 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00479: (2026x) Specifying the appearance of cells and the row or column headers

- page_id: `254437338`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437338/2026x+Specifying+the+appearance+of+cells+and+the+row+or+column+headers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:22.724+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Working with diagrams > (2026x) Working with Dependency Matrix > (2026x) Customizing a dependency matrix
- labels: ['cell-renderer', 'column-header-cell-renderer', 'row', 'header-cell-renderer', 'dependency-matrix-configurator', 'table-cell-renderer', 'matrix-cell-view', 'abstract-matrix-cell']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To customize the appearance of a dependency matrix, you have to register cell renderer components. There are three types of the renderers:

- A cell renderer
- A column header cell renderer
- A row header cell renderer

To register a cell renderer component, use the instance of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator* class, created while registering a custom dependency matrix. All these renderers should implement the standard *javax.swing.table.TableCellRenderer* interface.

The cell renderer is used to represent dependencies in a dependency matrix. The renderer component of a single cell gets either the value of the *Integer* class or the instance of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.MatrixCellView* class. The *java.lang.Integer* class represents dependency count. And the *MatrixCellView* class has a reference to the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell* class, which has all the information about the dependencies in the cell.

The row and column cell renderers works the same way. To get the particular row or column header element inside the cell, use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.ui.table.renderer.RendererHelper.getHeaderElement(javax.swing.JTable, int, int)* method.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To customize the appearance of a dependency matrix, you have to register cell renderer components. There are three types of the renderers:</p><ul><li>A cell renderer</li><li>A column header cell renderer</li><li>A row header cell renderer</li></ul><p>To register a cell renderer component, use the instance of the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator</em> class, created while registering a custom dependency matrix. All these renderers should implement the standard <em>javax.swing.table.TableCellRenderer</em> interface.</p><p>The cell renderer is used to represent dependencies in a dependency matrix. The renderer component of a single cell gets either the value of the <em>Integer</em> class or the instance of the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.MatrixCellView</em> class. The <em>java.lang.Integer</em> class represents dependency count. And the <em>MatrixCellView</em> class has a reference to the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell</em> class, which has all the information about the dependencies in the cell.</p><p>The row and column cell renderers works the same way. To get the particular row or column header element inside the cell, use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.ui.table.renderer.RendererHelper.getHeaderElement(javax.swing.JTable, int, int)</em> method.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437554 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00480: (2026x) Standard profiles implementation

- page_id: `254437554`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437554/2026x+Standard+profiles+implementation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:43.215+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) UML model > (2026x) Working with stereotypes and tagged values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Standard profiles implementation are available as part of API.

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile* to access "MagicDraw Profile" elements

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile* to access "StandardProfile" elements

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ValidationProfile***to access "Validation Profile" elements

MagicDraw plugins (like SysML) can also expose implementations of profiles. Check their APIs.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Standard profiles implementation are available as part of API.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile</em> to access &quot;MagicDraw Profile&quot; elements</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile</em> to access &quot;StandardProfile&quot; elements</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ValidationProfile</em><em> </em>to access &quot;Validation Profile&quot; elements </p><p><br /></p><p>MagicDraw plugins (like SysML) can also expose implementations of profiles. Check their APIs.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437163 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00481: (2026x) Starting execution

- page_id: `254437163`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437163/2026x+Starting+execution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:09.570+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > (2026x) Java APIs > (2026x) Execution
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

With Cameo Simulation Toolkit, you can start executing an element using the following Open APIs

- SimulationManager.execute(Element element) : SimulationSession
- SimulationManager.execute(Element element, Boolean isMainSession, Boolean start) : SimulationSession

Once you use the aforementioned Open APIs, Cameo Simulation Toolkit creates corresponding simulation sessions. The conditions when using the Open APIs to execute an element are as follows

- The specified element could be any executable element, such as a Class, a Behavior, or an simulation configuration element.
- If the executed element is intended to be the main element of the execution, set the "isMainSession" flag to true. Then, the created session will become the main session of the execution. The main session is the first session created once the execution starts. If the main session is terminated, all executions will be terminated accordingly.
- If the "start" flag is set to true, the created session will start itself automatically (similar to the 'autorun' option in an SimulationConfig).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>With Cameo Simulation Toolkit, you can start executing an element using the following Open APIs</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="12a1efe7-0e1f-4d71-b787-b371ebf298cc"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7ee8cf1d-fc1b-45b8-96f9-007dadbadfc4">SimulationManager.execute(Element element) : SimulationSession</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="21e3b246-3329-4c35-ad35-45db8faa59ca"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9dd29a01-11b3-4d69-a624-de481b5d9573">SimulationManager.execute(Element element, Boolean isMainSession, Boolean start) : SimulationSession</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><p>Once you use the aforementioned Open APIs, Cameo Simulation Toolkit creates corresponding simulation sessions. The conditions when using the Open APIs to execute an element are as follows</p><ul><li>The specified element could be any executable element, such as a Class, a Behavior, or an simulation configuration element.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="469dc2f0-9737-41d3-a2a0-95d515839ccc"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0e768531-ccd4-45a4-a498-fc8a90ebe099">If the executed element is intended to be the main element of the execution, set the &quot;isMainSession&quot; flag to true. Then, the created session will become the main session of the execution. The main session is the first session created once the execution starts. If the main session is terminated, all executions will be terminated accordingly.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>If the &quot;start&quot; flag is set to true, the created session will start itself automatically (similar to the 'autorun' option in an SimulationConfig).</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437421 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00482: (2026x) Starting MagicDraw or other modeling tool as part of another application

- page_id: `254437421`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437421/2026x+Starting+MagicDraw+or+other+modeling+tool+as+part+of+another+application
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:28.312+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To integrate MagicDraw or other modeling tool into your application you should launch MagicDraw directly from your Java application. This would allow you to interact with MagicDraw or other modeling tool classes directly.

In order to do that you must:

- Include lib/ folder and all of its subfolder into your project classpath
- Import class com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application and call the appropriate start(...) method. (see Javadoc)
- Make sure there is only one instance of the modeling tool running in the same JVM(multiple instances cannot be started in the same JVM)

For launching the modeling tool straight from the command line, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Running programs in batch mode' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To integrate MagicDraw or other modeling tool into your application you should launch MagicDraw directly from your Java application. This would allow you to interact with MagicDraw or other modeling tool classes directly.</p><p>In order to do that you must:</p><ul><li>Include lib/ folder and all of its subfolder into your project classpath</li><li>Import class com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application and call the appropriate <em>start(...)</em> method. (see Javadoc)</li><li>Make sure there is only one instance of the modeling tool running in the same JVM(multiple instances cannot be started in the same JVM) </li></ul><p><br /></p><p>For launching the modeling tool straight from the command line, please refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Running programs in batch mode" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437161 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00483: (2026x) Stopping execution

- page_id: `254437161`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437161/2026x+Stopping+execution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:09.480+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > (2026x) Java APIs > (2026x) Execution
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can stop an execution by terminating its main session. Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to terminate any simulation session using the following Open API

- SimulationManager.terminateSession(SimulationSession session) : void

Terminating a session will also cause all children of the specific session to be terminated.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can stop an execution by terminating its main session. Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to terminate any simulation session using the following Open API</p><ul><li>SimulationManager.terminateSession(SimulationSession session) : void</li></ul><p>Terminating a session will also cause all children of the specific session to be terminated.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437134 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00484: (2026x) Supported Changes from UML 2.4.1 to UML 2.5

- page_id: `254437134`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437134/2026x+Supported+Changes+from+UML+2.4.1+to+UML+2.5
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:07.300+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins migration > (2026x) Plugins migration to MagicDraw 18.0 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

33

##### Metamodel Changes

All the changes are grouped by the categories:

44

###### New property names added

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Classifier::conveyingFlow | - |
| DeployedArtifact::deploymentForArtifact |  |
| PackageableElement::import |  |

###### Properties renamed

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| NamedElement::classifierinheritingClassifier | - |
| Classifier::classnestingClass |  |
| ParameterableElement::parameterSubstitutionowningTemplateParameterSubstitution |  |

###### Derivation changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Property::/classifier : Classifier [0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Feature::featuringClassifier, subsets RedefinableElement::redefinitionContext} | All these properties have been made automatically derived and read-only unions. |
| InputPin::/action : Action[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| Action::/input : InputPin[*]{ordered, readOnly, union, subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| OutputPin::/action : Action[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| NamedElement::/namespace : Namespace[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| DirectedRelationship::/source : Element [1..*]{readOnly, union, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} | Both properties have been made automatically derived and read-only unions. This feature has been supported in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |
| DirectedRelationship::/target : Element [1..*]{readOnly, union, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} |  |
| NamedElement::/clientDependency | The Client Dependency property of a named element has been made automatically derived. This feature has been supported in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |

###### Order changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| AcceptEventAction::result : OutputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Action::output} | The ability to order output pins holding the values received from an event occurrence, has been available in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |
| Association::/endType : Type [1..*]{ordered, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} | The set of classifiers that are used as end types of the association, is no longer ordered. |
| Classifier::/attribute : Property [0..*]{ordered, union, subsets Classifier::feature} | The ability to order the attributes, which are direct properties of a classifier has been available in earlier versions of MagicDraw.Though the Attribute property cannot be edited, its value displays the same order of the attributes as is defined in other places of the classifier specification, such as the Owned Attribute property or the Attributes property group. Thus to change the Attribute property value, change the value of a relevant property. |
| ConnectableElement::/end : ConnectorEnd [0..*]{ordered} | A set of connector ends that attach to the particular connectable element, is no longer ordered. |
| DurationObservation::event : NamedElement [1..2]{ordered} | Event elements are now automatically ordered. |
| ReplyAction::♦ replyValue : InputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Action::input} | The set of input pins can now be ordered. In the Reply Value property value cell, click the button to open the dialog for reordering. |
| UnmarshallAction::♦result : OutputPin [1..*]{ordered, subsets Action::output} | The set of output pins can now be ordered. In the Result property value cell, click the button to open the dialog for reordering. |

###### Other changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| LoopNode::♦loopVariable : OutputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Element::ownedElement} | The Loop Variable property has been made composite. |
| OutputPin::loopNode : LoopNode[0..1]{subsets Element::owner} |  |
| Activity::structuredNode : StructuredActivityNode [0..*]{readOnly, subsets Activity::node, Activity::group} | The property has been made read-only in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |

###### Properties removed

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Property::/default : String[0..1] | The Default property no longer appears in a property specification. The value of the Default Value property will be used instead of the removed property value. |

##### Notation Changes

From now on, the inherited members are denoted with the caret "^" sign.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Profile changes

Standard/system profiles *StandardProfileL2* and *StandardProfileL3* have been merged into a single one.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><hr /><h3 class="bold">Metamodel Changes</h3><p>All the changes are grouped by the categories:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437155 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00485: (2026x) Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1

- page_id: `254437155`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437155/2026x+Supported+UML+Specification+Changes+from+Version+2.3+to+2.4.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:09.276+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x) Plugins migration > (2026x) Plugins migration to MagicDraw 17.0.1 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Supported UML specification changes include:

44

###### Metamodel Changes

| Metamodel Change | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| DATA TYPES ADDED |  |
| Real | Added to the PrimitiveTypes package. You can now use values of a real type in your models. |
| METACLASSES ADDED |  |
| LiteralReal | Specifies the real value. |
| METACLASSES CHANGED |  |
| DestructionEvent → DestructionOccurrenceSpecification | The DestructionEvent class has been renamed to DestructionOccurrenceSpecification. It represents the destruction of an instance described by the lifeline that contains this instance.DestructionOccurrenceSpecification is a specialization of MessageOccurrenceSpecification. |
| METACLASSES REMOVED |  |
| ExecutionEvent | These metaclasses have been removed from the Interactions package due to unsupported event types. |
| CreationEvent |  |
| ReceiveOperationEvent |  |
| ReceiveSignalEvent |  |
| SendOperationEvent |  |
| SendSignalEvent |  |
| PROPERTIES ADDED |  |
| Package::URI:String[0..1] {id} | Provides the package with an identifier that can be used for many purposes. URI is the universally unique identification of the package following the IETF URI specification, RFC 2396 http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt and it must comply with those syntax rules. You can now specify this property for packages, profiles, and models. For the notation of the URI property, see . |
| Property::isID:Boolean=False | Indicates that the property can be used to uniquely identify an instance of the containing class, when the value is set to true. The default value is false. For the notation of the isID property modifier, see . |
| InteractionUse::returnValue:ValueSpecification [0..1] | Specifies the value returned by the executed interaction. |
| InteractionUse::returnValueRecipient:Property [0..1] | Specifies the recipient of the value which is returned by the executed interaction. |
| EnumerationLiteral::/classifier:Enumaration[1] | The classifier of this EnumerationLiteral should now be equal to the enumeration that contains this enumeration literal. Redefines InstanceSpecification::classifier. |
| ASSOCIATIONS REMOVED |  |
| ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification::event: ExecutionEvent[1] | The association from ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification to ExecutionEvent has been removed, as the ExecutionEvent metaclass does not exist in the UML 2.4.1 |
| OccurenceSpecification::event:Event[1] | The specification of the occurring event is not referenced any more. |
| BehavioredClassifier::ownedTrigger:Trigger[0..*] | Trigger descriptions owned by a classifier are not referenced any more. |
| SUBSETTING CHANGES |  |
| Interface::redefinedInterface {subsets redefinedElement → redefinedClassifier} | An interface now references all the interfaces that are redefined by this interface. |
| SUBSETTING ADDEDNote: These features were already available in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |  |
| Duration::expr:ValueSpecification[0..1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} | Element or elements can be selected or created only under the owned element’s scope. |
| LinkEndData::qualifier:QualifierValue[*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| LinkAction::endData:LinkEndData[2..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| CreateLinkAction::endData:LinkEndCreationData[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| DestroyLinkAction::endData:LinkEndDestructionData[2..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| TimeExpression::expr:ValueSpecification[0..1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| ValuePin::value:ValueSpecification[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| State::deferrableTrigger:Trigger[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::edge:ActivityEdge[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::node:ActivityNode[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| ValueSpecificationAction::value: ValueSpecification[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| AcceptEventAction::trigger:Triger[1..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Stereotype::icon:Image[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| TimeEvent::when:TimeExpression[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| InteractionUse::argument:ValueSpecification[*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::node:ActivityNode [0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| SequenceNode::executableNode: ExecutableNode[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Transition::trigger:Trigger[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Classifier::/feature {subsets Namespace::member} | The /feature property of Classifier is now included as a part of the member property. |
| Feature::/featuringClassifier {subsets NamedElement::memberNamespace} | The /featuringClassifier property of Feature is now included as a part of the memberNamespace property. |
| Property::owningAssociation {subsets RedefinableElement::redefinitionContext} | A property now references the owning association of this property. |
| DEFAULT VALUE CHANGES |  |
| DurationObservation::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] =True | The default value has been removed. In effect, the value of the firstEvent property is now by default empty. |
| DurationConstraint::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] =True |  |
| PackageableElement::visibility:VisibilityKind[1] =False → Public | The default value of this property has been changed to Public.This means that packageable elements are now by default public. |
| MULTIPLICITY CHANGES |  |
| EnumerationLiteral::enumeration:Enumeration [0..1] → [1] | The Enumeration must now be specified. It has become mandatory. |
| Multiplicity of ExecutionSpecification association with ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification: [1] → [0..2] | ExecutionSpecification is now associated with two ExecutionOccurrenceSpecifications:The start ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the start of an action or behavior.The finish ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the finish of an action or behavior. |
| DERIVATION CHANGES |  |
| Constraint::/context → context | The context property of the constraint is no longer derived. It is now a settable property. |
| Message::/signature → signature | The signature of the message is no longer derived. However, it remains read-only. |

###### Notation changes

1. If there is a value defined for the URI property of a package, model, or profile, it is automatically displayed on the corresponding shape. The following figure depicts an example of the package notation with the URI property value defined: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. If the isID property is set to true for the selected attribute, the {id} modifier is displayed in the property modifiers group on the class shape. The following figure depicts an example of the attribute notation when the isID property is set to true: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. The name of a synchronous message is now displayed on a diagram pane, even if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the synchronous message name notation: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Brackets “()” are no more added to a call message name if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the call message name notation: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Supported UML specification changes include:</h3><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="170747f4-008e-4e01-8a1b-db29bbb3d7ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h4 class="bold">Metamodel Changes</h4><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><strong>Metamodel Change</strong></th><th><strong>Description / Comment</strong></th></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>DATA TYPES ADDED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Real</td><td>Added to the PrimitiveTypes package. You can now use values of a real type in your models.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>METACLASSES ADDED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>LiteralReal</td><td>Specifies the real value.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>METACLASSES CHANGED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>DestructionEvent → DestructionOccurrenceSpecification</td><td>The DestructionEvent class has been renamed to DestructionOccurrenceSpecification. It represents the destruction of an instance described by the lifeline that contains this instance.<br />DestructionOccurrenceSpecification is a specialization of MessageOccurrenceSpecification.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>METACLASSES REMOVED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>ExecutionEvent</td><td colspan="1" rowspan="6">These metaclasses have been removed from the Interactions package due to unsupported event types.</td></tr><tr><td>CreationEvent</td></tr><tr><td>ReceiveOperationEvent</td></tr><tr><td>ReceiveSignalEvent</td></tr><tr><td>SendOperationEvent</td></tr><tr><td>SendSignalEvent</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>PROPERTIES ADDED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Package::URI:String[0..1] {id}</td><td>Provides the package with an identifier that can be used for many purposes. URI is the universally unique identification of the package following the IETF URI specification, RFC 2396 <a href="http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt">http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt</a> and it must comply with those syntax rules. You can now specify this property for packages, profiles, and models. For the notation of the URI property, see <ac:link ac:anchor="Notation changes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Notation Changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td>Property::isID:Boolean=False</td><td>Indicates that the property can be used to uniquely identify an instance of the containing class, when the value is set to true. The default value is false. For the notation of the isID property modifier, see <ac:link ac:anchor="Notation changes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Notation Changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td>InteractionUse::returnValue:ValueSpecification [0..1]</td><td>Specifies the value returned by the executed interaction.</td></tr><tr><td>InteractionUse::returnValueRecipient:Property [0..1]</td><td>Specifies the recipient of the value which is returned by the executed interaction.</td></tr><tr><td>EnumerationLiteral::/classifier:Enumaration[1]</td><td>The classifier of this EnumerationLiteral should now be equal to the enumeration that contains this enumeration literal. Redefines InstanceSpecification::classifier.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>ASSOCIATIONS REMOVED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification::event: ExecutionEvent[1]</td><td>The association from ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification to ExecutionEvent has been removed, as the ExecutionEvent metaclass does not exist in the UML 2.4.1</td></tr><tr><td>OccurenceSpecification::event:Event[1]</td><td>The specification of the occurring event is not referenced any more.</td></tr><tr><td>BehavioredClassifier::ownedTrigger:Trigger[0..*]</td><td>Trigger descriptions owned by a classifier are not referenced any more.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>SUBSETTING CHANGES</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Interface::redefinedInterface {subsets <em>redefinedElement</em> → <em>redefinedClassifier</em>}</td><td>An interface now references all the interfaces that are redefined by this interface.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>SUBSETTING ADDED<br /></strong><strong>Note:</strong> These features were already available in earlier versions of MagicDraw.</td></tr><tr><td>Duration::expr:ValueSpecification[0..1] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td><td colspan="1" rowspan="18">Element or elements can be selected or created only under the owned element’s scope.</td></tr><tr><td>LinkEndData::qualifier:QualifierValue[*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>LinkAction::endData:LinkEndData[2..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElemen</em>t}</td></tr><tr><td>CreateLinkAction::endData:LinkEndCreationData[0..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>DestroyLinkAction::endData:LinkEndDestructionData[2..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>TimeExpression::expr:ValueSpecification[0..1] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>ValuePin::value:ValueSpecification[1] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>State::deferrableTrigger:Trigger[0..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>StructuredActivityNode::edge:ActivityEdge[0..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>StructuredActivityNode::node:ActivityNode[0..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>ValueSpecificationAction::value: ValueSpecification[1] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>AcceptEventAction::trigger:Triger[1..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>Stereotype::icon:Image[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement}</td></tr><tr><td>TimeEvent::when:TimeExpression[1] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>InteractionUse::argument:ValueSpecification[*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>StructuredActivityNode::node:ActivityNode [0..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>SequenceNode::executableNode: ExecutableNode[0..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>Transition::trigger:Trigger[0..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>Classifier::/feature {subsets <em>Namespace::member</em>}</td><td>The /feature property of Classifier is now included as a part of the member property.</td></tr><tr><td>Feature::/featuringClassifier {subsets <em>NamedElement::memberNamespace</em>}</td><td>The /featuringClassifier property of Feature is now included as a part of the memberNamespace property.</td></tr><tr><td>Property::owningAssociation {subsets <em>RedefinableElement::redefinitionContext</em>}</td><td>A property now references the owning association of this property.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>DEFAULT VALUE CHANGES</strong></td></tr><tr><td>DurationObservation::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] <span>=True</span></td><td colspan="1" rowspan="2">The default value has been removed. In effect, the value of the firstEvent property is now by default empty.</td></tr><tr><td>DurationConstraint::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] <span>=True</span></td></tr><tr><td>PackageableElement::visibility:VisibilityKind[1] =False → Public</td><td>The default value of this property has been changed to Public.This means that packageable elements are now by default public.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>MULTIPLICITY CHANGES</strong></td></tr><tr><td>EnumerationLiteral::enumeration:Enumeration [0..1] → [1]</td><td>The Enumeration must now be specified. It has become mandatory.</td></tr><tr><td>Multiplicity of ExecutionSpecification association with ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification: [1] → [0..2]</td><td>ExecutionSpecification is now associated with two ExecutionOccurrenceSpecifications:<br /><ul><li>The start ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the start of an action or behavior.</li><li>The finish ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the finish of an action or behavior.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>DERIVATION CHANGES</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Constraint::/context → context</td><td>The context property of the constraint is no longer derived. It is now a settable property.</td></tr><tr><td>Message::/signature → signature</td><td>The signature of the message is no longer derived. However, it remains read-only.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4 class="bold">Notation changes</h4><ol><li><p>If there is a value defined for the URI property of a package, model, or profile, it is automatically displayed on the corresponding shape. The following figure depicts an example of the package notation with the URI property value defined:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_uri_notation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>If the isID property is set to true for the selected attribute, the {id} modifier is displayed in the property modifiers group on the class shape. The following figure depicts an example of the attribute notation when the isID property is set to true:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="131"><ri:attachment ri:filename="isID_property_notation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>The name of a synchronous message is now displayed on a diagram pane, even if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the synchronous message name notation:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchCall_message_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>Brackets “()” are no more added to a call message name if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the call message name notation:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="153"><ri:attachment ri:filename="call_message_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437511 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00486: (2026x) SysML classes for open API

- page_id: `254437511`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437511/2026x+SysML+classes+for+open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:37.714+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) SysML Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Classes which are available for open API are included in SysML plugin open API documentation. Find these in *<SysML plugin installation directory>\openapi\docs*.

The*com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLUtilities* class was added to the open APIs. It provides utility methods for easier work with SysML projects.

Methods and classes marked as deprecated do not support the development of external plugins.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Classes which are available for open API are included in SysML plugin open API documentation. Find these in <em>&lt;SysML plugin installation directory&gt;\openapi\docs</em>.</p><p>The<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLUtilities</em> class was added to the open APIs. It provides utility methods for easier work with SysML projects.</p><p>Methods and classes marked as deprecated do not support the development of external plugins.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437500 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00487: (2026x) SysML Developer Guide

- page_id: `254437500`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437500/2026x+SysML+Developer+Guide
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-28T15:11:30.154+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home
- labels: ['open-api']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Standard stereotypes in SysML plugin are defined in SysML Profile and MD Customization for SysML Profile. Both profiles have their corresponding API classes: *[com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/SysMLProfile.html)*and *[com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.html)*, respectively. Each class allows you to:

- Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag).
- Get a stereotype element.
- Check if an element is stereotyped.

Find JavaDoc of SysML plugin in *<modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/docs/sysml_javadoc.zip*

For example, *<modeling_tool_version_number>_no_install\openapi\docs\sysml_javadoc.zip*

Find JavaDoc of Requirements plugin in <modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/docs/requirements_javadoc.zip

For example, *<modeling_tool_version_number>**_no_install\openapi\docs\requirements_javadoc.zip*

Extract a .jar file and double-click the*index.html*to open it.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

#### PANEL: Related docs

Related docs

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modeling Tools Developer Guide' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437502 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00488: (2026x) SysML Profile

- page_id: `254437502`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437502/2026x+SysML+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:36.775+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) SysML Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to import *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to use this API class.

****

**Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)**

Usage includes “SysMLProfile.Stereotype.*STEREOTYPE_NAME*”.

For example, SysMLProfile.RequirementStereotype.*ID*returns a string of “Id”.

****

**Get a stereotype element**

Usage includes:

“SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(project).stereotype().getStereotype()” where project refers to a project that uses SysML Profile.

“SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(element).stereotype().getStereotype()“ where element refers to the element in a project that uses SysML Profile.

For example, SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(project).block().getStereotype() returns the reference to the «Block» stereotype object.

**Check if an element is stereotyped**

Usage includes SysMLProfile.Stereotype.*isInstance*(Elem) where "Elem" is the element you want to check.

For example, given an element “Elem”, SysMLProfile.BlockStereotype.*isInstance*(Elem) returns *True* if the element “Elem” has «Block» stereotype applied and returns *False* otherwise.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You need to import <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to use this API class.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Usage includes “SysMLProfile.Stereotype.</span><em style="text-align: left;">STEREOTYPE_NAME</em><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">”.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">For example, SysMLProfile.RequirementStereotype.</span><em>ID</em><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>returns a string of “Id”.</span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Get a stereotype element</strong></p><p style="text-align: left;">Usage includes:</p><p style="text-align: left;">“SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(project).stereotype().getStereotype()” where project refers to a project that uses SysML Profile.</p><p style="text-align: left;">“SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(element).stereotype().getStereotype()“ where element refers to the element in a project that uses SysML Profile.</p><p style="text-align: left;">For example, SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(project).block().getStereotype() returns the reference to the «Block» stereotype object.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Check if an element is stereotyped</strong></p><p style="text-align: left;">Usage includes  SysMLProfile.Stereotype.<em>isInstance</em>(Elem) where &quot;Elem&quot; is the element you want to check.</p><p style="text-align: left;">For example, given an element “Elem”, SysMLProfile.BlockStereotype.<em>isInstance</em>(Elem) returns <em>True</em> if the element “Elem” has «Block» stereotype applied and returns <em>False</em> otherwise.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254437509 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00489: (2026x) SysML Profile API Changes

- page_id: `254437509`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/254437509/2026x+SysML+Profile+API+Changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:50:37.216+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x) Developer Guides Home > (2026x) SysML Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysML Profile API changes were made in relation to the SysML 1.4 support.

The following constants were moved from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile*:

public static final String CONSTRAINTPROPERTY_STEREOTYPE = "ConstraintProperty";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_STEREOTYPE = "QuantityKind";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DEFINITIONURI_PROPERTY = "definitionURI";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DESCRIPTION_PROPERTY = "description";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_SYMBOL_PROPERTY = "symbol";

public static final String UNIT_STEREOTYPE = "Unit";

The following methods were moved from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile*:

getConstraintProperty()

getQuantityKind()

getUnit()

isQuantityKind()

isUnit()

isConstraintProperty()

The constant NESTEDCONNECTOREND_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY changed to ELEMENTPROPERTYPATH_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>SysML Profile API changes were made in relation to the SysML 1.4 support.</p><p>The following constants were moved from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</em>:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String CONSTRAINTPROPERTY_STEREOTYPE = &quot;ConstraintProperty&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_STEREOTYPE = &quot;QuantityKind&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DEFINITIONURI_PROPERTY = &quot;definitionURI&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DESCRIPTION_PROPERTY = &quot;description&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_SYMBOL_PROPERTY = &quot;symbol&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String UNIT_STEREOTYPE = &quot;Unit&quot;;</p><p>The following methods were moved from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</em>:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getConstraintProperty()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getQuantityKind()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getUnit()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isQuantityKind()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isUnit()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isConstraintProperty()</p><p>The constant NESTEDCONNECTOREND_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY changed to ELEMENTPROPERTYPATH_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
