<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=309365583 space=DEVG version=27 -->
## PAGE 00577: Creating Custom Validation Rules and Suites

- page_id: `309365583`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/309365583/Creating+Custom+Validation+Rules+and+Suites
- version_number: 27
- version_date: `2026-06-30T05:41:47.122+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Model Validation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page explains how to create custom validation rules and validation suites by using the SysML v2 Open API.

A custom validation rule is implemented as a Java class that implements [Constraint](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/Constraint.html). Rules can be grouped into a custom suite by implementing [ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite.html) and registering the suite with [ValidationSuiteRegistry](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationSuiteRegistry.html).

#### Overview

Creating custom validation consists of three steps:

1. Implement a custom Constraint .
2. Group one or more constraints into a custom ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite .
3. Register the suite using ValidationSuiteRegistry .

Run validation using [ValidationService](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html).

#### Creating a Custom Constraint

The following example validates [PartDefinition](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/PartDefinition.html) elements.

```text
{
    private static final String CONSTRAINT_NAME = "validatePartDefinitionName";

    @Override
    public String getName()
    {
        return CONSTRAINT_NAME;
    }

    @Override
    public Class getElementClassType()
    {
        return PartDefinition.class;
    }

    @Override
    public SeverityLevel getSeverity()
    {
        return SeverityLevel.ERROR;
    }

    @Override
    public Stream validateElement(PartDefinition element, Context context)
    {
        String message = getValidationMessage(element);

        if (message == null)
        {
            return Stream.empty();
        }

        return Stream.of(ValidationFailureFactory.create(element, element, this, message));
    }

    @CheckForNull
    private static String getValidationMessage(Element element)
    {
        if (element instanceof PartDefinition partDefinition)
        {
            String name = partDefinition.getDeclaredName();

            if (name == null || name.isBlank())
            {
                return "Part definition must have a name.";
            }

            if (containsWhitespace(name))
            {
                return "Part definition '" + name + "' must not contain whitespace.";
            }

            if (!startsWithUppercaseLetter(name))
            {
                return "Part definition '" + name + "' must start with an uppercase letter.";
            }
        }

        return null;
    }

    private static boolean startsWithUppercaseLetter(String text)
    {
        return Character.isUpperCase(text.codePointAt(0));
    }

    private static boolean containsWhitespace(String text)
    {
        return text.codePoints().anyMatch(Character::isWhitespace);
    }
}
]]>
```

The most important methods are:

| Method | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| getName() | Returns the unique name of the constraint. |
| getElementClassType() | Defines the model element type supported by the constraint. |
| getSeverity() | Returns the severity level reported when validation fails. |
| validateElement(...) | Executes validation logic and returns validation failures. |

Validation failures are created using [ValidationFailureFactory](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailureFactory.html).

```text

```

#### Creating a Custom Validation Suite

Validation suites group related constraints together.

To create a custom suite, implement [ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite.html).

```text
getConstraints()
    {
        return List.of(new ValidatePartDefinitionName());
    }
}
]]>
```

The suite must provide:

| Method | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| getQualifiedName() | Returns the qualified name of the corresponding validation suite in the model, represented by a PartDefinition. |
| getConstraints() | Returns the constraints included in the suite. |

#### Registering a Custom Validation Suite

```text
new CustomValidationSuite());
]]>
```

The provided factory is invoked for each project and returns the validation suite instance that should be contributed to the validation framework. If multiple suites with the same qualified name are registered, they will be merged.

#### INFO: Custom Validation Suite Must Have a Match in the Model!

Custom Validation Suite Must Have a Match in the Model!

A custom validation suite defined in code must have a corresponding validation suite in the model, represented by a [PartDefinition](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/PartDefinition.html) that specializes Suite from the DassaultSystemesValidation library.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Unregistering a Custom Validation Suite

```text

```

This is typically used during plugin shutdown or unload operations to remove custom validation contributions.

#### Complete Example

The following example demonstrates registration and execution of a custom validation suite.

```text
new CustomValidationSuite());

// Validate anywhere in plugin code.
Namespace namespace = ...; // Root namespace in the model where validation suite exists.
PartDefinition suiteDefinition = Finder.byQualifiedName().find(namespace, "Model::CustomValidationSuite", PartDefinition.class);
ValidationSuite customValidationSuite = ValidationService.createValidationSuite(suiteDefinition);

List elementsToValidate = ...;
ValidationService validationService = ValidationService.getInstanceByProject(getProject());
ValidationService.Params params = validationService.createParams(elementsToValidate).addSuite(customValidationSuite);
Collection failures = validationService.validate(params);
]]>
```

#### Related Open API Classes

| Class | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Constraint | Base interface for custom validation rules. |
| ValidationFailure | Represents a validation failure. |
| ValidationFailureFactory | Creates validation failures. |
| ValidationService | Executes validation. |
| ValidationService.Params | Validation request configuration. |
| ValidationSuite | Represents a validation suite defined in the model. |
| ValidationSuiteRegistry | Registers and unregisters custom validation suite factories. |
| SeverityLevel | Validation severity enumeration. |

#### Best Practices

- Keep constraints focused on a single validation responsibility.
- Return the most specific element type from getElementClassType() .
- Use ValidationFailureFactory when creating validation failures.
- Group related constraints into reusable validation suites.
- Register validation suites during plugin initialization and unregister them during plugin shutdown when appropriate.
- Use ValidationService.Params to explicitly control which suites participate in validation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page explains how to create custom validation rules and validation suites by using the SysML v2 Open API.</p><p>A custom validation rule is implemented as a Java class that implements <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/Constraint.html">Constraint</a>. Rules can be grouped into a custom suite by implementing <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite.html">ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite</a> and registering the suite with <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationSuiteRegistry.html">ValidationSuiteRegistry</a>.</p><h2>Overview</h2><p>Creating custom validation consists of three steps:</p><ol><li>Implement a custom <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/Constraint.html">Constraint</a>.</li><li>Group one or more constraints into a custom <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite.html">ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite</a>.</li><li>Register the suite using <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationSuiteRegistry.html">ValidationSuiteRegistry</a>.</li></ol><p>Run validation using <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html">ValidationService</a>.</p><h2>Creating a Custom Constraint</h2><p>The following example validates <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/PartDefinition.html">PartDefinition</a> elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33074c82-f9ea-4021-9fea-510c21a82bd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.Constraint;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.Context;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.SeverityLevel;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationFailure;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationFailureFactory;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;

import javax.annotation.CheckForNull;
import java.util.stream.Stream;

public class ValidatePartDefinitionName implements Constraint<PartDefinition>
{
    private static final String CONSTRAINT_NAME = "validatePartDefinitionName";

    @Override
    public String getName()
    {
        return CONSTRAINT_NAME;
    }

    @Override
    public Class<PartDefinition> getElementClassType()
    {
        return PartDefinition.class;
    }

    @Override
    public SeverityLevel getSeverity()
    {
        return SeverityLevel.ERROR;
    }

    @Override
    public Stream<ValidationFailure> validateElement(PartDefinition element, Context context)
    {
        String message = getValidationMessage(element);

        if (message == null)
        {
            return Stream.empty();
        }

        return Stream.of(ValidationFailureFactory.create(element, element, this, message));
    }

    @CheckForNull
    private static String getValidationMessage(Element element)
    {
        if (element instanceof PartDefinition partDefinition)
        {
            String name = partDefinition.getDeclaredName();

            if (name == null || name.isBlank())
            {
                return "Part definition must have a name.";
            }

            if (containsWhitespace(name))
            {
                return "Part definition '" + name + "' must not contain whitespace.";
            }

            if (!startsWithUppercaseLetter(name))
            {
                return "Part definition '" + name + "' must start with an uppercase letter.";
            }
        }

        return null;
    }

    private static boolean startsWithUppercaseLetter(String text)
    {
        return Character.isUpperCase(text.codePointAt(0));
    }

    private static boolean containsWhitespace(String text)
    {
        return text.codePoints().anyMatch(Character::isWhitespace);
    }
}
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The most important methods are:</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><p>Method</p></th><th><p>Purpose</p></th></tr><tr><td><p><code>getName()</code></p></td><td><p>Returns the unique name of the constraint.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>getElementClassType()</code></p></td><td><p>Defines the model element type supported by the constraint.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>getSeverity()</code></p></td><td><p>Returns the severity level reported when validation fails.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>validateElement(...)</code></p></td><td><p>Executes validation logic and returns validation failures.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p>Validation failures are created using <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailureFactory.html">ValidationFailureFactory</a>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="956720c6-8895-408a-8c06-48cff24fee8d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ValidationFailure failure = ValidationFailureFactory.create(element, element, this, message);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Creating a Custom Validation Suite</h2><p>Validation suites group related constraints together.</p><p>To create a custom suite, implement <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite.html">ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite</a>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="879f23de-33fb-463f-86cf-ddca2c322118"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.Constraint;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationSuiteRegistry;

import java.util.List;

public class CustomValidationSuite implements ValidationSuiteRegistry.Suite
{
    @Override
    public String getQualifiedName()
    {
        return "Model::CustomValidationSuite";
    }

    @Override
    public List<Constraint> getConstraints()
    {
        return List.of(new ValidatePartDefinitionName());
    }
}
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The suite must provide:</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><p>Method</p></th><th><p>Purpose</p></th></tr><tr><td><p><code>getQualifiedName()</code></p></td><td><p>Returns the qualified name of the corresponding validation suite in the model, represented by a <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/PartDefinition.html">PartDefinition</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>getConstraints()</code></p></td><td><p>Returns the constraints included in the suite.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h2>Registering a Custom Validation Suite</h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dd8c38f-0951-41ff-9ae5-56a43c92954a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ValidationSuiteRegistry.registerFactory(project -> new CustomValidationSuite());
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The provided factory is invoked for each project and returns the validation suite instance that should be contributed to the validation framework. If multiple suites with the same qualified name are registered, they will be merged.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a7a4dbb-7e0f-44cc-b68a-baf1698ac2d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Custom Validation Suite Must Have a Match in the Model!</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A custom validation suite defined in code must have a corresponding validation suite in the model, represented by a <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/PartDefinition.html">PartDefinition</a> that specializes Suite from the DassaultSystemesValidation library.</p><p><ac:image ac:width="561"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2026-6-22_12-6-50.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Unregistering a Custom Validation Suite</h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2d39712-f6d8-40f9-9ae8-9e242b3f64a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ValidationSuiteRegistry.unregisterFactory(factory);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This is typically used during plugin shutdown or unload operations to remove custom validation contributions.</p><h2>Complete Example</h2><p>The following example demonstrates registration and execution of a custom validation suite.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53c438fe-cc84-4d3c-a1d5-27fabd288401"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationFailure;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationService;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationSuite;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationSuiteRegistry;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.find.Finder;

import java.util.Collection;
import java.util.List;

// Register the suite in plugin initialization code.
ValidationSuiteRegistry.registerFactory(project -> new CustomValidationSuite());

// Validate anywhere in plugin code.
Namespace namespace = ...; // Root namespace in the model where validation suite exists.
PartDefinition suiteDefinition = Finder.byQualifiedName().find(namespace, "Model::CustomValidationSuite", PartDefinition.class);
ValidationSuite customValidationSuite = ValidationService.createValidationSuite(suiteDefinition);

List<Element> elementsToValidate = ...;
ValidationService validationService = ValidationService.getInstanceByProject(getProject());
ValidationService.Params params = validationService.createParams(elementsToValidate).addSuite(customValidationSuite);
Collection<ValidationFailure> failures = validationService.validate(params);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Related Open API Classes</h2><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><p>Class</p></th><th><p>Description</p></th></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/Constraint.html">Constraint</a></p></td><td><p>Base interface for custom validation rules.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html">ValidationFailure</a></p></td><td><p>Represents a validation failure.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailureFactory.html">ValidationFailureFactory</a></p></td><td><p>Creates validation failures.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html">ValidationService</a></p></td><td><p>Executes validation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html">ValidationService.Params</a></p></td><td><p>Validation request configuration.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationSuite.html">ValidationSuite</a></p></td><td><p>Represents a validation suite defined in the model.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationSuiteRegistry.html">ValidationSuiteRegistry</a></p></td><td><p>Registers and unregisters custom validation suite factories.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/SeverityLevel.html">SeverityLevel</a></p></td><td><p>Validation severity enumeration.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h2>Best Practices</h2><ul><li>Keep constraints focused on a single validation responsibility.</li><li>Return the most specific element type from <code>getElementClassType()</code>.</li><li>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailureFactory.html">ValidationFailureFactory</a> when creating validation failures.</li><li>Group related constraints into reusable validation suites.</li><li>Register validation suites during plugin initialization and unregister them during plugin shutdown when appropriate.</li><li>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html">ValidationService.Params</a> to explicitly control which suites participate in validation.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573509 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00578: Creating diagram images

- page_id: `249573509`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573509/Creating+diagram+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:37:55.084+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Open API of a modeling tool provides the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.export.image.ImageExporter* class for creating images from the whole diagram or just a part of it.

Image formats are predefined as constants in *ImageExporter*.

To create an image from the whole diagram, use the *export(DiagramPresentationElement, int, java.io.File)*method.

To create an image from selected symbols in the diagram, use the *export(DiagramPresentationElement**, int, java.io.File, boolean)*method.

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\imagegenerator*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Open API of a modeling tool provides the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.export.image.ImageExporter</em> class for creating images from the whole diagram or just a part of it.</p><p>Image formats are predefined as constants in <em>ImageExporter</em>. </p><p>To create an image from the whole diagram, use the <em>export(DiagramPresentationElement, int, java.io.File) </em>method.</p><p>To create an image from selected symbols in the diagram, use the <em>export(DiagramPresentationElement</em><em>, int, java.io.File, boolean) </em>method.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\imagegenerator</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573346 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00579: Creating JUnit test case

- page_id: `249573346`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573346/Creating+JUnit+test+case
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:57:03.070+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins > Creating test cases
- labels: ['magicdraw-test-case']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the creation of JUnit test cases.

###### JUnit 3 test case

The JUnit test case should be created by extending the abstract *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestCase* class from Open API (see the following *MyTest* sample). The *MagicDrawTestCase* class starts MagicDraw automatically for each test case method defined in *MagicDrawTestCase* and performs the memory leak test after each completed test case.

*MagicDrawTestCase* provides default and specific constructors for creating a test case instance with a specific custom name for the specific test case method. Test cases with specific names might be helpful when several instances of the same test case are created and analyzed.

The *MagicDrawTestCase* initialization and tear down should be implemented in overridden *setUpTest()* and *tearDownTest()* methods. The standard JUnit method *suite* might be used for preparing the tests suite with several instances of test cases which may use different test data.

```java

```

*MagicDrawTestCase* also provides methods for opening, saving, and closing MagicDraw projects. It also performs the memory leak test after the test case has been completed and the project has been closed. The memory leak test for the whole test case can be disabled using the*setMemoryTestReady(boolean)* method, while the *setSkipMemoryTest(boolean)* method can be used to disable the memory leak test on closing the MagicDraw project.

The list of required MagicDraw plugins for the test case can be configured by overriding the *getRequiredPlugins()* method of *MagicDrawTestCase*. The overridden method implementation should return the list of required plugins IDs. Textual information about required plugins and their loading status can be obtained using *getRequiredPluginsDescription()* and *isRequiredPluginsLoaded()* methods.

Textual information about the test process can be logged using the Log4j logger. The test case logger for the TEST category can be accessed using the *getLogger()* method of *MagicDrawTestCase*. More information about the Log4j logger configuration and usage can be found at[http://logging.apache.org/log4j/1.2/manual.html.](http://logging.apache.org/log4j/1.2/manual.html)

###### JUnit 4 test case

The JUnit4 test class should be annotated to use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestRunner* from Open API (see a code sample bellow). The** *MagicDrawTestRunner* class starts MagicDraw automatically and performs the memory leak test after each completed test case.

```java

```

###### JUnit 5 test case

The JUnit5 test class should be annotated to extend *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawApplication* from Open API (see a code sample bellow). The**MagicDrawApplication**extension starts MagicDraw automatically before running the test cases.

```java

```

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the creation of JUnit test cases.</p><h4>JUnit 3 test case</h4><p>The JUnit test case should be created by extending the abstract <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestCase</em> class from Open API (see the following <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MyTest</em> sample). The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MagicDrawTestCase</em> class starts MagicDraw automatically for each test case method defined in <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> MagicDrawTestCase </em> and performs the memory leak test after each completed test case.</p><p><em> MagicDrawTestCase </em> provides default and specific constructors for creating a test case instance with a specific custom name for the specific test case method. Test cases with specific names might be helpful when several instances of the same test case are created and analyzed.</p></div><p>The  <em>MagicDrawTestCase</em> initialization and tear down should be implemented in overridden <em> setUpTest() </em> and <em>tearDownTest()</em> methods. The standard JUnit method <em>suite</em> might be used for preparing the tests suite with several instances of test cases which may use different test data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4627c32-1eab-4e4f-b541-30e9277442d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    import com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestCase;
    public class MyTest extends MagicDrawTestCase
    {
        public MyTest(String testMethodToRun, String testName)
        {
            super(testMethodToRun, testName);
        }
 
        @Override
        protected void setUpTest() throws Exception
        {
            super.setUpTest();
            //do setup here
        }
 
        @Override
        protected void tearDownTest() throws Exception
        {
            super.tearDownTest();
            //do tear down here
        }
 
        public void testSomething()
        {
              //implement the unit test here
        }
 
        public static Test suite() throws Exception
        {
            //you may create a test suite with several instances of the test. 
            TestSuite suite = new TestSuite();
            suite.addTest(new MyTest("testSomething", "MagicDraw Test Sample"));
            suite.addTest(new MyTest("testSomething", "Another MagicDraw Test Sample"));
            return suite;
        }
    }

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><em>MagicDrawTestCase</em> also provides methods for opening, saving, and closing MagicDraw projects. It also performs the memory leak test after the test case has been completed and the project has been closed. The memory leak test for the whole test case can be disabled using the<em> setMemoryTestReady(boolean) </em> method, while the <em>setSkipMemoryTest(boolean)</em> method can be used to disable the memory leak test on closing the MagicDraw project.</p><p>The list of required MagicDraw plugins for the test case can be configured by overriding the <em>getRequiredPlugins()</em> method of <em>MagicDrawTestCase</em>. The overridden method implementation should return the list of required plugins IDs. Textual information about required plugins and their loading status can be obtained using <em> getRequiredPluginsDescription() </em> and <em>isRequiredPluginsLoaded()</em> methods.</p><p>Textual information about the test process can be logged using the Log4j logger. The test case logger for the TEST category can be accessed using the <em>getLogger()</em> method of <em> MagicDrawTestCase</em>. More information about the Log4j logger configuration and usage can be found at<a href="http://logging.apache.org/log4j/1.2/manual.html"> http://logging.apache.org/log4j/1.2/manual.html.</a></p><h4>JUnit 4 test case</h4><p>The JUnit4 test class should be annotated to use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestRunner</em> from Open API (see a code sample bellow). The<em> </em> <em>MagicDrawTestRunner</em> class starts MagicDraw automatically and performs the memory leak test after each completed test case.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="473ea7c3-30e9-4279-8205-7333d03d41eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestRunner;
import org.junit.*;
import org.junit.runner.RunWith;

@RunWith(MagicDrawTestRunner.class)
public class MyTest
{
   @Before
   public void beforeTestBegins()
   {
      //do test case setup here, e.g. load project
   }

   @Test
   public void testSomething()
   {
      //implement the unit test case here
   }

   @After
   public void afterTestDone()
   {
      //do tear down here, e.g. close project
   }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>JUnit 5 test case</h4><p>The JUnit5 test class should be annotated to extend <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawApplication</em> from Open API (see a code sample bellow). The<em> <em>MagicDrawApplication</em> </em>extension starts MagicDraw automatically before running the test cases.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28dbef5b-f677-453a-8c52-c95a6b72c731"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawApplication;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.*;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtendWith;

@ExtendWith(MagicDrawApplication.class)
public class MyTest
{
   @BeforeEach
   void beforeTestBegins()
   {
      //do test case setup here, e.g. load project
   }

   @Test
   void testSomething()
   {
      //implement the unit test case here
   }

   @AfterEach
   void afterTestDone()
   {
      //do tear down here, e.g. close project
   }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f0c867d-301c-4da9-a3c2-78929aa6fca7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00bd8cc7-7484-43a6-a882-888fff6df655" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><br /><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p> </ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573379 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00580: Creating new actions

- page_id: `249573379`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573379/Creating+new+actions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:41:47.793+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Adding new functionality
- labels: ['create-command', 'create-button', 'custom-command', 'custom-button']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

****You can find the code examples in

- < modeling tool installation direc tory>\openapi\examples\actiontypes
- < modeling tool installation direc tory>\openapi\examples\simpleconfigurators
- < modeling tool installation direc tory>\openapi\examples\selectionactions

##### Step #1: Create a new action class

All actions used in a modeling tool must be subclasses of the*com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MDAction* class (seejavadocfor more details). The following*MDAction*subclasses that are used for basic purposes are already created in a modeling tool:

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.actions.DefaultBrowserAction – an action class, used for a browser action. It enables to access some browser tree and nodes and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected browser nodes.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.actions.DefaultDiagramAction – an action class for a diagram action. It enables to access some diagram elements and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected diagram elements.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.PropertyAction – an action for changing some element or application property. It can be used for changing properties defined by a user.

You must override at least the *actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)* method and implement in it what this actions is going to do.

###### **Example #1****: A simple action**

```java

```

###### **Example #2****: An action for a browser**

```java

```

##### Step #2: Specify action properties

| Property | Function |
| --- | --- |
| ID | ID is a unique String for identifying an action. If the action ID is set to null, a new ID is generated. An action name can be used as ID. All MagicDraw (or another modeling tool developed by us) default actions IDs are defined in the com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID class (for more details, see JavaDoc). You can use these constants for accessing actions. Do not use it as new actions ID. |
| Name | The action name is visible in all GUI elements. |
| Shortcut and mnemonic | Every action can have an assigned keyboard shortcut. Shortcuts can be customized from the Environment Options dialog box.(see MagicDraw User Manual, Section “Environment Options”.)The action must have an ID assigned (not null). In other case, shortcuts cannot be restored after restarting an application. |
| Icon | Every action can have a small and large icon. A small icon is described as javax.swing.Action.SMALL_ICON and can be used in menu items. A large icon is used in toolbar buttons. The action for a toolbar must have a large icon, otherwise it is displayed as a button with an action name. java |
| Description | The action’s description that is shown as a tool tip text. |

****

##### Step #3: Describe Enabling/Disabling logic

There are two ways for controlling the update of the actions state:

1. Add an action to the predefined actions group.

Actions can be added into one of predefined actions groups. All actions of the one group are disabled/enabled together. Conditions for groups enabling/disabling and status updating are predefined and cannot be changed.

Example:

```java

```

2. **Implement the*****updateState()*********method for the action**.

Here you may describe all conditions when an action must be enabled and disabled. Example of the *updateState()* method for the browser shortcut menu action:

```java

```

If the action is not added to any group, the *updateState()*****method for all such actions will be invoked after executing any command and after closing/opening a project or window.

When some actions need to refresh their state, all actions without a group can be updated manually:

```java

```

##### Step #4: Configure actions

Every action must be added into an *com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory* class. *ActionsCategory* is a small group for actions. It can be represented as a separator or submenu (a nested category).

Categories are added into the *com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager* class, which is some kind of an actions container. One *ActionsManager* represents one GUI element – a menu bar, a shortcut menu, or a toolbar.

The following table explains how classes of a modeling tool maps into GUI elements:

|  | ActionsManager | Category | Action |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Menu | Menu bar | Menu | Menu item |
| Toolbar | All toolbars | One toolbar | Button |
| Shortcut Menu | Shortcut menu | Submenu | Menu item |

Actions in *ActionsManagers* are configured by many *Configurators*. A *Configurator* is responsible for adding or removing the action into a strictly defined place and position between other actions.

There are three types of configurators:

- com.nomagic.actions.AMConfigurator - a configurator for a general purpose. It is used for menus, toolbars, browser, and diagrams shortcuts.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.BrowserContextAMConfigurator - a configurator for configuring managers for a browser shortcut (pop-up) menu. It can access a browser tree and nodes.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.DiagramContextAMConfigurator - a configurator for configuring shortcut menus in a diagram. It can access a diagram, selected diagram elements, and an element that requests a shortcut menu.

Once *ActionsManagers* for the main menu and all toolbars are created and configured, the actions can be only disabled but not removed later.

Shortcut menus are created on every invoking, so *ActionsManagers* are created and configured every time and actions can be added or removed every time.

###### **Exampl****e #1: Add some action into a browser’s shortcut menu**

```java

```

###### **Exampl****e #2: add some action into main menu, after creating a new project**

```java

```

##### Step #5: Register Configurator

All configurators are registered in the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsConfiguratorsManager* class. *ActionsConfiguratorsManager* enables to add or remove many configurators to every configuration predefined by a modeling tool.

All available configurations are accessible in the following way:

```java
Configurator(configurator);]]>
```

###### **Example: Add new configurator for CONTAINMENT_BROWSER_CONTEXT configuration**

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li>&lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\actiontypes</em></li><li>&lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\simpleconfigurators<br /></em></li><li>&lt;<em>modeling tool</em> <em>installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\selectionactions</em><em><br /></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Step #1: Create a new action class</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">All actions used in a modeling tool must be subclasses of the </span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MDAction</em> class (see </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">javadoc</span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> for more details). The following </span><em>MDAction</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">subclasses that are used for basic purposes are already created in a modeling tool:</span></p><div class="WordSection1"><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.actions.DefaultBrowserAction</em> – an action class, used for a browser action. It enables to access some browser tree and nodes and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected browser nodes.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.actions.DefaultDiagramAction</em> – an action class for a diagram action. It enables to access some diagram elements and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected diagram elements.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.PropertyAction</em> – an action for changing some element or application property. It can be used for changing properties defined by a user.</li></ul><p>You must override at least the <em>actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)</em> method and implement in it what this actions is going to do.</p><h4><strong>    Example #1</strong><strong>: A simple action</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e84c3c8f-3f54-499e-bfae-e88922872360"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    class SimpleAction extends MDAction
    { 
        public SimpleAction(String id, String name)
        {
             super(id, name, null, null); 
        }
        /**
        * Shows message.
        */
        public void actionPerformed(ActionEvent e)
        { 
            JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent(), "This is:" + getName()); 
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection2"><h4><strong>    Example #2</strong><strong>: An action for a browser</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ebd762e-1a8d-44d2-87a1-0f4cbe4aa4a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    public class BrowserAction extends DefaultBrowserAction
    {
        /**
        * Creates action with name "ExampleAction"
        */
        public BrowserAction()
        { 
          super("", "ExampleAction", null, null); 
        }
        public void actionPerformed(ActionEvent e)
        {
            Tree tree = getTree();
            String text="Selected elements:";
            for (int i = 0; i < tree.getSelectedNodes().length; i++)
            { 
                Node node = tree.getSelectedNodes()[i]; 
                Object userObject = node.getUserObject(); 
                if (userObject instanceof BaseElement)
                {
                    BaseElement element = (BaseElement) userObject;
                    text += "\n"+element.getHumanName(); 
                }
            }
            JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent(), text);
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Step #2: Specify action properties</h3><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>ID</strong></td><td><p>ID is a unique String for identifying an action. If the action ID is set to <em>null, a </em>new ID is generated. An action name can be used as ID. All MagicDraw (or another modeling tool developed by us) default actions IDs are defined in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID</em> class (for more details, see <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">JavaDoc</span>). You can use these constants for accessing actions. Do not use it as new actions ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Name</strong></td><td>The action name is visible in all GUI elements.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Shortcut and mnemonic</strong></td><td><p>Every action can have an assigned keyboard shortcut. Shortcuts can be customized from the<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Environment Options </strong>dialog box.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-only" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d87b1ccd-736c-405f-9134-fe8e415c923b"><ac:rich-text-body>(see MagicDraw User Manual, Section “Environment Options”.)</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54fcf749-a24f-4355-9268-69fdc52cfa4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The action must have an ID assigned (not null). In other case, shortcuts cannot be restored after restarting an application.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Icon</strong></td><td><p>Every action can have a small and large icon. A small icon is described as <em>javax.swing.Action.SMALL_ICON</em> and can be used in menu items. A large icon is used in toolbar buttons. The action for a toolbar must have a large icon, otherwise it is displayed as a button with an action name.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exampleAction_button_with_name.png" /></ac:image> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exampleAction_button.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2a386e7-c748-41d6-8a28-2ec2ae67f5c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[// setting an icon. On the toolbar, the button with an icon looks better than with the text.
action.setLargeIcon( new ImageIcon(getClass().getResource("main_toolbar_icon.gif") ) );]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Description</strong></td><td>The action’s description that is shown as a tool tip text.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">         </strong></p><div class="WordSection1"><h3>Step #3: Describe Enabling/Disabling logic</h3><p> There are two ways for controlling the update of the actions state:</p><ol><li><strong> Add an action to the predefined actions group.</strong></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Actions can be added into one of predefined actions groups. All actions of the one group are disabled/enabled together. Conditions for groups enabling/disabling and status updating are predefined and cannot be changed.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2c5c9b7-cfc9-44a7-844d-a298d4bed16a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[MDAction action = new MDAction("Example","Example",KeyEvent.VK_E, ActionsGroups.PROJECT_OPENED_RELATED);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>    2.  <strong>Implement the </strong><em><strong>updateState()</strong></em><strong> </strong><strong>method for the action</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Here you may describe all conditions when an action must be enabled and disabled. Example of the <em>updateState()</em> method for the browser shortcut menu action:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b09cb2a1-c539-47ae-bce8-d397356a100b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[        public void updateState()
        { 
           //action will be enabled only if there are some selected nodes. 
			Tree tree = getTree();
			if(tree != null)
			{
   				setEnabled(tree.getSelectedNode() != null);
			}
        }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>If the action is not added to any group, the <em>updateState()</em><strong> </strong>method for all such actions will be invoked after executing any command and after closing/opening a project or window.</p><p>When some actions need to refresh their state, all actions without a group can be updated manually:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96206eaa-b210-4a79-8dbf-476532529d1a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ActionsStateUpdater.updateActionsState();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br style="margin-left: 30.0px;" /><h3>Step #4: Configure actions</h3><p>Every action must be added into an <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory</em> class. <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ActionsCategory</em> is a small group for actions. It can be represented as a separator or submenu (a nested category).</p></div><p>Categories are added into the <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager</em> class, which is some kind of an actions container. One <em>ActionsManager</em> represents one GUI element – a menu bar, a shortcut menu, or a toolbar. </p><div class="WordSection2">The following table explains how classes of a modeling tool maps into GUI elements:</div><div class="WordSection2"><p><br /></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th style="text-align: left;">ActionsManager</th><th style="text-align: left;">Category</th><th style="text-align: left;">Action</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Menu</strong></td><td><span>Menu bar</span></td><td><span>Menu</span></td><td><span>Menu item</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>T</strong><strong>oolbar</strong></td><td><span>All toolbars</span></td><td><span>One toolbar</span></td><td><span>Button</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Shortcut Menu</strong></td><td><span>Shortcut menu</span></td><td><span>Submenu</span></td><td><span>Menu item</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>Actions in <em>ActionsManagers</em> are configured by many <em>Configurators</em>. A <em>Configurator</em> is responsible for adding or removing the action into a strictly defined place and position between other actions.</p></div><p>There are three types of configurators:</p><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.actions.AMConfigurator</em> - a configurator for a general purpose. It is used for menus, toolbars, browser, and diagrams shortcuts.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.BrowserContextAMConfigurator</em> - a configurator for configuring managers for a browser shortcut (pop-up) menu. It can access a browser tree and nodes.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.DiagramContextAMConfigurator</em> - a configurator for configuring shortcut menus in a diagram. It can access a diagram, selected diagram elements, and an element that requests a shortcut menu.</li></ul><p>Once <em>ActionsManagers</em> for the main menu and all toolbars are created and configured, the actions can be only disabled but not removed later.</p><p>Shortcut menus are created on every invoking, so <em>ActionsManagers</em> are created and configured every time and actions can be added or removed every time.</p><p><br /></p><h4><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">    Exampl</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">e #1: Add some action into a browser’s shortcut menu</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02415b63-5905-407e-aa6e-fb1a2128fdc8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    final DefaultBrowserAction browserAction = ...
    BrowserContextAMConfigurator brCfg = new BrowserContextAMConfigurator()
    {
        // Implement configuration.
        // Add or remove some actions in ActionsManager.
        // A tree is passed as an argument, provides ability to access nodes.
       public void configure(ActionsManager mngr, Tree browser)
       {
           	// Actions must be added into some category.
           	// So create the new one, or add an action into the existing category.
           	MDActionsCategory category = new MDActionsCategory("", "");
          	category.addAction(browserAction);
            // Add a category into the manager.
          	// A category isn't displayed in a shortcut menu.
          	mngr.addCategory(category);
       }
       public int getPriority()
       {
          return AMConfigurator.MEDIUM_PRIORITY;
       }
   };]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection5"><h4><strong>    Exampl</strong><strong>e #2: add some action into main menu, after creating a new project</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f42d1b97-1134-4495-bb00-36125c230ed4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // Create an action.
    final MDAction someAction = ...
    AMConfigurator conf = new AMConfigurator()
    {
        public void configure(ActionsManager mngr)
        {
           // Searching for an action after which an insertion should be done. 
           NMAction found= mngr.getActionFor(ActionsID.NEW_PROJECT);
 
           // The action found, inserting 
           if( found != null )
           {
              	// find the category of the "New Project" action. 
              	ActionsCategory category = (ActionsCategory)mngr.getActionParent(found);


             	// Get all actions from this category (menu). 
             	List actionsInCategory = category.getActions();


             	//Add the action after the "New Project" action.
             	int indexOfFound = actionsInCategory.indexOf(found);
             	actionsInCategory.add(indexOfFound+1, someAction);
 
             	// Set all actions. 
             	category.setActions(actionsInCategory);
       		}
    	}

    	public int getPriority()
    	{
        	return AMConfigurator.MEDIUM_PRIORITY;
    	}
   	};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Step #5: Register Configurator</h3><p>All configurators are registered in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsConfiguratorsManager</em> class. <em>ActionsConfiguratorsManager</em> enables to add or remove many configurators to every configuration predefined by a modeling tool.</p><p>All available configurations are accessible in the following way:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91f767b2-ebe7-457b-a37d-dba86f541a99"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ActionsConfiguratorsManager.getInstance().add<configuration_name>Configurator(configurator);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><h4>    <strong>Example: Add new configurator for CONTAINMENT_BROWSER_CONTEXT configuration</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dffdfcae-5c92-4e62-bec1-7fb553cdd076"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    //See previous examples, how to create a configurator for browser actions.
    //Add the configurator into ActionsConfiguratorsManager.
    ActionsConfiguratorsManager.getInstance().addContainmentBrowserContextConfigurator(brCfg);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573416 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00581: Creating new model elements

- page_id: `249573416`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573416/Creating+new+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:57:42.054+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ElementsFactory*class for creating model elements. To create a model element, a session with the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager* must be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The *create<model element type>Instance(*) method creates a new model element instance. The figure above shows only a subset of all available *create<...>Instance()* methods.

All changes in the UML model will be registered and, on the session closing, will be added into the command history.

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling tool installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\accessors*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SessionManager API' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ElementsFactory </em>class for creating model elements. To create a model element, a session with the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager</em> must be created.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ElementsFactory.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">create&lt;model element type&gt;Instance(</em>) method creates a new model element instance. The figure above shows only a subset of all available <em>create&lt;...&gt;Instance()</em> methods.</p><p>All changes in the UML model will be registered and, on the session closing, will be added into the command history.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a82fadd-6c32-4d08-845e-a0c4c29e39ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project =...;
    ElementsFactory f = project.getElementsFactory(); 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Create a package");
    Package packageA = f.createPackageInstance();
    //add created package into a root of the project
    packageA.setOwner(project.getPrimaryModel());
    ...
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history.
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in &lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\accessors</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8340d8e4-37a3-49ae-b9bb-080d4acd05d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating a diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573419 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00582: Creating new relationship objects

- page_id: `249573419`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573419/Creating+new+relationship+objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:06:54.697+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A model element is a relationship, if it implements one of the following interfaces:

- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Relationship,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.activities.mdbasicactivities.ActivityEdge ,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.statemachines.mdbehaviorstatemachines.Transition ,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.activities.mdextrastructuredactivities.ExceptionHandler ,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdinternalstructures.Connector .

For checking if the model element is a relationship, call the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.CoreHelper.isRelationship(Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#isRelationship-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)* method.

For getting supplier and client elements of a relationship, use *CoreHelper**class getSupplierElement(Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#getSupplierElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)*, *getClientElement(Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#getClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)*methods.

Use *CoreHelper**class**setSupplierElement(**Element, Element)* , *setClientElement(Element[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)*methods to set supplier and client elements of a relationship.

To create a new Relationship

1. Create a new relationship model element.
2. Set client and supplier model elements by using *CoreHelper* class *setSupplierElement(**Element, Element)* , *setClientElement(Element[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)* methods.
3. Add a new relationship into some parent by using the com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element.setOwner( Element) method.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SessionManager API' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Checking Model Element Permissions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

interfaces:</p><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Relationship, </em></li><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.activities.mdbasicactivities.ActivityEdge</em>,</li><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.statemachines.mdbehaviorstatemachines.Transition</em>,</li><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.activities.mdextrastructuredactivities.ExceptionHandler</em>,</li><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdinternalstructures.Connector</em>.</li></ul><p>For </em>methods to set supplier and client elements of a relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a new Relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a new relationship model element.</li><li>Set client and supplier model<em> </em>elements by using <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><em>CoreHelper</em> </em>class<em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> <em>setSupplierElement(</em><em>Element, Element)</em></em>, <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><em>setClientElement(Element<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-">, </a>Element<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-">)</a></em> </em>methods.</li><li>Add a new relationship into some parent by using the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element.setOwner(</em><em>Element)</em><em> </em>method.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d40e65f0-cdce-4093-96e2-0854e2be2a93"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Checking Model Element Permissions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573481 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00583: Creating path elements

- page_id: `249573481`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573481/Creating+path+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:39:47.117+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathElement* for given *ModelLink* between given client and supplier elements, use method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.createPathElement(**Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement)*:

```java

```

The diagram is not passed into *createPathElement(**Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement)*method. A new path element is created in the same diagram as client or supplier presentation elements.

****You can find the code examples in

- *<installation_directory>*\openapi\examples\symbolcreation
- **<installation_directory>*\openapi\examples\sequencecreation*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathElement</em> for given <em>ModelLink</em> between given client and supplier elements, use method <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.createPathElement(</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement)</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18114fb6-b146-4506-ab53-7645667de387"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mddependencies.Dependency link = ...; 
    PresentationElement clientPE = ...;
    PresentationElement supplierPE = ...; 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test"); 
    PathElement path = PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().createPathElement(link, clientPE, supplierPE);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The diagram is not passed into <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">createPathElement(</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement) </em>method. A new path element is created in the same diagram as client or supplier presentation elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em>\openapi\examples\symbolcreation</em></li><li><em><em><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em>\openapi\examples\sequencecreation</em></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fab8a4f-e60b-4600-ac77-cdd7ef0ea882"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573555 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00584: Creating PopupMenu or other menu

- page_id: `249573555`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573555/Creating+PopupMenu+or+other+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:52:56.364+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Using and extending UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

API provides a way to create a popup menu from actions. Of course, you can use the standard java api for that, but we provide our own creators if you use our Actions.

Use*com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MenuCreatorFactory.getMenuCreator()* to get an instance of a creator.

Use *com.nomagic.actions.ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu(**ActionsManager)* to create a popup menu.

Steps to create a popup menu would be

1. Create an instance of com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager.
2. Add com.nomagic.actions.NMAction actions and com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory categories into ActionsManager .
3. Call ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu( ActionsManager) to create a popup for your actions.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new actions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Of course, you can use the standard java api for that, but we provide our own creators if you use our Actions.</p><p>Use<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MenuCreatorFactory.getMenuCreator()</em> to get an instance of a creator.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu(</em><em>ActionsManager)</em> to create a popup menu.</p><p><br /></p><p>Steps to create a popup menu would be</p><ol><li>Create an instance of <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager.</em></li><li>Add<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.actions.NMAction</em> actions and<em> com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory</em> categories into <em>ActionsManager</em>.</li><li>Call <em>ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu(</em><em>ActionsManager)</em>  to create a popup for your actions.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bac02bc-b67f-4173-a430-6fd772fe6374"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating new actions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573362 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00585: Creating required files and folders structure

- page_id: `249573362`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573362/Creating+required+files+and+folders+structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:34.267+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Distributing Resources
- labels: ['distributed-resources', 'distributing-diagrams', 'distributing-profiles', 'distributing-templates', 'distributing-samples', 'distributing-documentation', 'distributing-plugins']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

960869435

960869448

960869437

To distribute resources, you must create the required files and folders for a particular resource type. Some of the resource file names should match the standard names.

For each resource file, there should be a created folders structure, which should match the folders structure of the program installation folder.

To distribute resources, you must create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource manager descriptor file' space=''].

##### Distributing**Custom** Diagrams

A required file for the custom diagram distribution is *descriptor.xml* - a Custom Diagram descriptor provided by MagicDraw Resource Manager. The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The custom diagram type (in diagram descriptor.xml file) should match the folder name it is placed in.

For example, the custom diagram with type *My Custom Structure Diagram* should be placed in a folder named **My Custom Structure Diagram**.

on about creating new diagram types,

UML Profiling and DSL UserGuide.pdf

##### Distributing Profiles

You can distribute your profile as the following structure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For more information about workingwithProfiles see *MagicDraw UserManual.pdf*and *UML Profiling and DSL UserGuide.pdf*.

##### Distributing Templates

Files for the template distribution are as follows:

- CustomTemplate.mdzip (required) - the template project file.
- CustomTemplate.html - the description of the template project.
- CustomTemplate.html - the description of the template group.

The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:**

*[IMAGE alt='' src='']*

****

##### Distributing Samples and Documentation

You can distribute your created samples and documentation and import them into your modeling tool with the MagicDraw ResourceManager. The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Distributing Plugins

Required files for a plugin distribution are as follows:

- plugins.xml - A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin descriptor' space=''] .
- customPlugin.jar - jarred [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin classes' space=''] . You may select any title for this file.

The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The plugin term may include all resources that could be distributed, such as custom diagrams, profiles, templates, samples, and others.

960869434

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource manager descriptor file' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin descriptor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin classes' space='']
