<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573603 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00586: Creating script

- page_id: `249573603`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573603/Creating+script
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:54:34.628+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Jython Scripting
- labels: ['plugin-descriptor', 'python', 'jython']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Let's describe creating a script in an example. We will create a script showing a message on a program startup. The creation process consists of three steps:

1. Creating a directory
2. Writing a script descriptor
3. Writing a script code

****

**Ste****p #1: Create Directory**

In the *plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython*folder, create a *scripts*sub-folder, and then a folder for the particular script. For example, *plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython/scripts/example*

**Ste****p #2: Write Script Descriptor**

A script descriptor is a file written in XML and named *script.xml*. The script descriptor provides information about a script file to run, version of script, ID, and other.

In the created directory, create a *script.xml*file:

```xml
]]>
```

The following table describes the *script.xml* file structure:

| Element | Description |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| script | Attributes |  |
| Name | Description |  |
| id | A scrip ID, should be unique. Used to identify a script. Example: “my.first.script.0” |  |
| name | A script name. No strict rules applied to this attribute. Example: “Example script” |  |
| version | A script version. Allows numbers separated with one dot value. Examples: “1.0”, “0.1” |  |
| provider-name | A script provider name. A company or an author name. Example: "No Magic" |  |
| script-file | A relative path to a script file. This file will be executed. Example:”main.py” |  |
| requires-api | A program API version required by a script. Example:”1.0” |  |

**Ste****p #3: Write Script Code**

Then in the same directory, create the *main.py*file:

```py

```

After saving files, restart your modeling tool. On a program startup, a message dialog should appear.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**V****ariable****s Passed to Script**

The program passes the one variable to the script *pluginDescriptor*. This variable contains information from the parsed *script.xml* file. A variable is an instance of a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython.PythonPluginDescriptor* class.

A script can retrieve the script directory and other necessary information from the *pluginDescriptor* variable. There is no need to change any other fields for this variable.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>Let's describe creating a script in an example. We will create a script showing a message on a program startup. The creation process consists of three steps:</p><ol><li>Creating a directory</li><li>Writing a script descriptor</li><li>Writing a script code</li></ol><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Ste</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">p #1: Create Directory</strong></p><p>In the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython </em>folder, create a <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">scripts </em>sub-folder, and then a folder for the particular script. For example, <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython/scripts/example</em></p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Ste</strong><strong>p #2: Write Script Descriptor</strong></p><p>A script descriptor is a file written in XML and named <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">script.xml</em>. The script descriptor provides information about a script file to run, version of script, ID, and other.</p><p>In the created directory, create a <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">script.xml </em>file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76342eea-3493-4cb5-8db8-87712549bd66"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    <script
        id="example 1"
        name="Simple menu item"
        version="1.0"
        provider-name="No Magic"
        script-file="main.py"
        requiresApi="1.0">
    </script>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p>The following table describes the <em>script.xml</em> file structure:</p><table><tbody><tr><th><strong>Element</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th><th><br /></th></tr><tr><td rowspan="8">script<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td colspan="2"><strong>Attributes</strong></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Name</strong></td><td><strong>Description</strong></td></tr><tr><td>id</td><td><span>A scrip ID, should be unique. Used to identify a script. Example: “my.first.script.0”</span></td></tr><tr><td>name</td><td><span>A script name. No strict rules applied to this attribute. Example: “Example script”</span></td></tr><tr><td>version</td><td><span>A script version. Allows numbers separated with one dot value. Examples: “1.0”, “0.1”</span></td></tr><tr><td>provider-name</td><td><span>A script provider name. A company or an author name. Example: &quot;No Magic&quot;</span></td></tr><tr><td>script-file</td><td><span>A relative path to a script file. This file will be executed. Example:”main.py”</span></td></tr><tr><td>requires-api</td><td><span>A program API version required by a script. Example:”1.0”</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><strong>Ste</strong><strong>p #3: Write Script Code</strong></p><p>Then in the same directory, create the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">main.py </em>file:  </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fee9da0d-14c0-4e5a-95fa-f61a2209d811"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">py</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[from javax.swing import JOptionPane

# Script starts here
print "Starting script, descriptor", pluginDescriptor
JOptionPane.showMessageDialog( None, "I am a script!!!")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>After saving files, restart your modeling tool. On a program startup, a message dialog should appear.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="I_am_script.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><p><strong>V</strong><strong>ariable</strong><strong>s Passed to Script</strong></p><p>The program passes the one variable to the script <em>pluginDescriptor</em>. This variable contains information from the parsed <em>script.xml</em> file. A variable is an instance of a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython.PythonPluginDescriptor</em> class.</p><p>A script can retrieve the script directory and other necessary information from the <em>pluginDescriptor</em> variable. There is no need to change any other fields for this variable.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573480 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00587: Creating shape elements

- page_id: `249573480`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573480/Creating+shape+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:40:32.740+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement* for given *ModelElement* in the given***com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement*, use method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager#createShapeElement(...)*. The location of the created shape will be (0,0).

The following code example shows how to do this:

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\symbolcreation
- <installation_directory*>\openapi\examples\sequencecreation*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>To create a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement</em> for given <em>ModelElement</em> in the given<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement</em>, use method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager#createShapeElement(...)</em>. The location of the created shape will be (0,0).</p><p>The following code example shows how to do this:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39605a8a-f11b-4266-98b9-f68effb6285e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class clazz = ...; 
    DiagramPresentationElement diagram = ...; 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test");
    ShapeElement shape = PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().createShapeElement(clazz, diagram); 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\symbolcreation</em></li><li><em><span>&lt;installation_directory</span><em>&gt;\openapi\examples\sequencecreation</em></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b78620b-9e53-477a-b97f-fe624969d0a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573343 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00588: Creating test cases

- page_id: `249573343`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573343/Creating+test+cases
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:22:17.961+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools, including MagicDraw, provide a JUnit ([www.junit.org)](http://www.junit.org/)-based test frameworks. The main purpose of the test framework is to simplify the automatic unit and integration tests development for the program and its plugins. The test framework can be used by developers for testing their own plugins or standard essential program features.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

###### JUnit 3 test cases

The test framework consists of an abstract JUnit test case implementation (*com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestCase*) and a number of tools (*com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.TestEnvironment*, *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.ProjectsComparator*) that can be used for the following purposes:

- Starting the application
- Managing projects
- Checking program for memory leaks

###### JUnit 4 test cases

The test framework also has test class runner implementations (*com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestRunner*, *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.ParametrizedMagicDrawTestRunner*) that:

- Start the application
- Check program for memory leaks.

###### JUnit 5 test cases

Extend your test class with *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawApplication* to start the application before running the test methods.

****You can find the examples of the code in <*modeling tool**installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\testframework*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

## PAGE 00589: Creating textual element representation

- page_id: `249573436`
- space_key: `DEVG`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.RepresentationTextCreator* to create a textual representation of Element. This utility class is used for creating a textual element representation in various UI - trees, list and etc.

```java

```

**Providing a custom representation text**

You can override default custom text creation for the specific element.

Use *RepresentationTextCreator*class method *addProvider(**RepresentationTextCreator.RepresentationTextProvider)* to register a custom representation text provider.

## PAGE 00590: Creating Use Case Scenario

- page_id: `249573580`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573580/Creating+Use+Case+Scenario
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:43:40.322+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Modeling tools provide an API for creating and manipulating a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case Scenario sketch' space='MT'] for a use case:

- The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.Scenario* class represents a Use Case Scenario. It contains methods for manipulating it.
- The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.ScenarioFactory* class is used for creating use case scenarios.
- The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.ScenarioManager* class contains utility methods for creating and manipulating scenarios.
- The scenario management calls should be wrapped with the session management calls.

See an example of how to create the use case scenario with the basic, alternative, and exceptional flows. After the use case scenario is created, the Activity diagram is opened. In the Activity diagram, the use case scenario is represented as an action flow.

```java

```

More information is available in javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\scenario

For more information about the use case scenario, see Use Case Scenario in MagicDraw UserManual.pdf.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>Modeling tools provide an API for creating and manipulating a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case Scenario sketch" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Case Scenario]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for a use case:</p><ul><li><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.Scenario</em> class represents a Use Case Scenario. It contains methods for manipulating it.</p></li><li><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.ScenarioFactory</em> class is used for creating use case scenarios.</p></li><li><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.ScenarioManager</em> class contains utility methods for creating and manipulating scenarios.</p></li><li><p>The scenario management calls should be wrapped with the session management calls.</p></li></ul><p>See an example of how to create the use case scenario with the basic, alternative, and exceptional flows. After the use case scenario is created, the Activity diagram is opened. In the Activity diagram, the use case scenario is represented as an action flow.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="340c6200-35e2-4d2d-86c8-0214c646855c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(...);
	// Creates a use case scenario.
    Scenario scenario = ScenarioManager.createScenario(useCase);
    // Sets the scenario name.
    scenario.setName("Extract money from ATM.");
 
    // Adds a basic flow step.
    FlowStep flowStep1 = scenario.addFlowStep();
    // Sets a name for the basic flow step.
    flowStep1.setName("Insert card");
 
    FlowStep flowStep2 = scenario.addFlowStep();
    flowStep2.setName("Enter pin");
 
    FlowStep flowStep3 = scenario.addFlowStep();
    flowStep3.setName("Good bye");
 
    // Adds an alternative condition for the basic flow step.
    AlternativeCondition condition = scenario.addAlternativeCondition(flowStep2);
    // Sets a condition guard.
    condition.setIfCondition("Pin correct");
 
    // Sets a name for the alternative flow step.
    FlowStep flowStep = condition.getAlternativeFlowSteps().get(0);
    flowStep.setName("Extract money");
    // Adds an exception type to the basic flow step.
    ExceptionType exceptionType = scenario.addExceptionType(flowStep2);
 
    // Sets a name for the exception type. 
    exceptionType.setName("Card expired");
    // Sets a name for the exceptional flow step.
    FlowStep exceptionalFlowStep = exceptionType.getExceptionalFlowSteps().get(0);
    exceptionalFlowStep.setName("Inform customer about expired card");
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();

    // Opens and layouts the Activity diagram. 
    ScenarioManager.displayScenario(scenario, true, true, "Open ATM Scenario");]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\scenario</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-only" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="237b7985-afd5-4c27-8a62-1d6b1bd3e60e"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about the use case scenario, see Use Case Scenario in MagicDraw UserManual.pdf.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573558 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00591: Creating validation rules

- page_id: `249573558`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573558/Creating+validation+rules
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:34.209+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based
- labels: ['validation-suite', 'validation-rule', 'active-validation-suite', 'annotation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Validation rules and validation suites specify what will be validating and how. They also specify how a problem that is found by a validation rule can be solved.

A**v****alidation rule**is a constraint with the applied stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule». Validation rules can validate model elements and non model elements (for example, presentation elements) as well. The UML metaclass specified as a constrained element defines the validation rule that validates elements of the specified metaclass. The stereotype specified as a constrained element specifies the validation rule that validates elements having the stereotype applied. A classifier specified as a constrained element specifies the validation rule that validates instances of the specified classifier. Validation rule’s implementation can be OCL2.0 based or binary. Binary validation rules can be implemented in Java. OCL2.0 based validation rules are described using the OCL2.0 language. The validation rule can be global or local one. Global validation rules are executed only once per validation session. Local validation rules are executed for each model element. The validation rule is treated as global if:

- it is the OCL2.0 based validation rule, the OCL2.0 specification is valid, and the OCL2.0 specification does not use the “self” variable (explicitly or implicitly by using the property of a constrained element only)
- it is binary based, has the specified implementation class name, and it has no specified constrained elements.

A**v****alidation suite**is a package with the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationSuite». The validation suite organizes several validation rules into a unit that can be used for performing the validation. Validation rules can be added or imported into the validation suite.

An**active validation suite**is a package with the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::activeValidationSuite». Active validation rules can be checked constantly or on the model elements change. OCL2.0 or binary validation rules can be used in the active validation. We suggest to prefer binary, because they give the better performance.

An**annotation**defines the validation result. It contains information about what is incorrect, severity of the problem, and possible actions that can be used to solve the problem.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Annotating the elements' space='']

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Validation rules and validation suites specify what will be validating and how. They also specify how a problem that is found by a validation rule can be solved.</p><p>A<strong> v</strong><strong>alidation rule </strong>is a constraint with the applied stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule». Validation rules can validate model elements and non model elements (for example, presentation elements) as well. The UML metaclass specified as a constrained element defines the validation rule that validates elements of the specified metaclass. The stereotype specified as a constrained element specifies the validation rule that validates elements having the stereotype applied. A classifier specified as a constrained element specifies the validation rule that validates instances of the specified classifier. Validation rule’s implementation can be OCL2.0 based or binary. Binary validation rules can be implemented in Java. OCL2.0 based validation rules are described using the OCL2.0 language. The validation rule can be global or local one. Global validation rules are executed only once per validation session. Local validation rules are executed for each model element. The validation rule is treated as global if:</p><ul><li>it is the OCL2.0 based validation rule, the OCL2.0 specification is valid, and the OCL2.0 specification does not use the “self” variable (explicitly or implicitly by using the property of a constrained element only)</li><li>it is binary based, has the specified implementation class name, and it has no specified constrained elements.</li></ul><p>A<strong> v</strong><strong>alidation suite </strong>is a package with the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationSuite». The validation suite organizes several validation rules into a unit that can be used for performing the validation. Validation rules can be added or imported into the validation suite.</p><p>An<strong> active validation suite </strong>is a package with the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::activeValidationSuite». Active validation rules can be checked constantly or on the model elements change. OCL2.0 or binary validation rules can be used in the active validation. We suggest to prefer binary, because they give the better performance.</p><p>An<strong> annotation </strong>defines the validation result. It contains information about what is incorrect, severity of the problem, and possible actions that can be used to solve the problem.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58027d06-d3f1-4061-922c-52b0ca0eb8d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Annotating the elements" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="75652ee4-97db-427f-a361-a972a67a65cb"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573508 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00592: Custom diagram painters

- page_id: `249573508`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573508/Custom+diagram+painters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:42:13.367+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams
- labels: ['diagram-surface']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Open API provides a way to add your own custom diagram painters for painting some additional stuff on the diagram canvas. A good sample would be some highlighting in the diagram.

The painter can be added only into the opened diagram's *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramSurface*. Only the opened diagram has *DiagramSurface*. A closed diagram returns *null*.

###### Example of the code:

```java

```

```text

```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\customdiagrampainter*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The Open API provides a way to add your own custom diagram painters for painting some additional stuff on the diagram canvas. A good sample would be some highlighting in the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc78d5d3-78d1-479e-a45e-3b1a6bab7f5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The painter can be added only into the opened diagram's <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramSurface</em>. Only the opened diagram has <em>DiagramSurface</em>. A closed diagram returns <em>null</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example of the code:</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="223a2e8d-b1dd-4546-80f1-c344a7640e85"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Application.getInstance().addProjectEventListener(new ProjectEventListenerAdapter()
{
   public void projectOpened(Project project)
   {
      project.addPropertyChangeListener(new PropertyChangeListener()
      {
         public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
         {
            if(evt.getPropertyName().equals(Project.DIAGRAM_OPENED))
            {
               DiagramPresentationElement diagram = Application.getInstance().getProject().getActiveDiagram();
               diagram.getDiagramSurface().addPainter(new DiagramSurfacePainter()
               {
                  public void paint(Graphics g, DiagramPresentationElement diagram)
                  {
                     g.setColor(Color.BLUE);
                     List symbols = diagram.getPresentationElements();
                     for (int i = 0; i < symbols.size(); i++)
                     {
                        PresentationElement o = (PresentationElement)symbols.get(i);
                        if( o instanceof ShapeElement)
                        {
                           Rectangle bounds = o.getBounds();
                           bounds.grow(5,5);
                           ((Graphics2D)g).draw(bounds);
                        }

                     }

                  };

               });

            }

         }

      });

   }

});]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre>    </pre></div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\customdiagrampainter</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180691 space=DEVG version=23 -->
## PAGE 00593: Custom Drag-and-Drop Handlers

- page_id: `314180691`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/314180691/Custom+Drag-and-Drop+Handlers
- version_number: 23
- version_date: `2026-06-24T23:37:16.360+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Custom drag-and-drop handlers allow plugins to provide additional drag-and-drop behavior for SysML v2 diagrams.

#### Overview

A custom drag-and-drop implementation consists of:

- [CustomDragAndDropHandler](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/kerml/diagram/ui/dnd/CustomDragAndDropHandler.html) – defines the drag-and-drop behavior.
- [CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/kerml/diagram/ui/dnd/CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory.html) – creates handler instances.
- [CustomDropDiagramHandlerFactory](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/kerml/diagram/ui/dnd/CustomDropDiagramHandlerFactory.html) – registers handlers with the diagram framework.

#### Drag-and-Drop Lifecycle

When a user drags model elements onto a diagram, the framework invokes registered handlers in the following order:

`willAcceptDrop()` – determines whether the handler can process the operation. 
`drop()` – performs the drop operation if the drop is accepted. 
`willAcceptDrop()` may be called repeatedly while the user moves the mouse. Therefore, it should perform only lightweight validation.

#### Implementing a Handler

Create a class extending `[CustomDragAndDropHandler](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/kerml/diagram/ui/dnd/CustomDragAndDropHandler.html)` and implement the required methods.

```java
draggedElements,
 								  AbstractDiagramPresentationElement diagram)
	{
		// Validate whether the drop operation is allowed.
		return true;
	}

	@Override
	public boolean drop(Point location, @CheckForNull PresentationElement elementOver, @CheckForNull List draggedElements,
			            AbstractDiagramPresentationElement diagram)
	{
		// Perform the drop operation.
		return true;
	}
}

]]>
```

##### Method Descriptions

| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| getDescription() | Returns the text displayed for the drag-and-drop action. |
| willAcceptDrop() | Determines whether the handler can process the current drop operation. Return true if the drop is supported; otherwise return false. |
| drop() | Executes the drag-and-drop operation. Return true if the operation was performed successfully; otherwise return false. |

#### Creating a Handler Factory

Implement `[CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/kerml/diagram/ui/dnd/CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory.html)` to create handler instances.

```java

```

#### Registering a Handler

Register the factory during plugin initialization.

```java

```

#### Example

The following example changes the owner of a dragged element to the namespace represented by the drop target.

```java
draggedElements, AbstractDiagramPresentationElement diagram)
{
    if (elementOver != null)
	{
		Element draggedElement = getSingleDraggedElement(draggedElements);
		if (draggedElement != null)
		{
			Element element = (Element) elementOver.getModelElement();
			return draggedElement.getOwner() != element;
		}
	}
	return false;
}

@CheckForNull
private static Element getSingleDraggedElement(@CheckForNull List draggedElements)
{
	return draggedElements != null && draggedElements.size() == 1 ? draggedElements.getFirst() : null;
}]]>
```

##### Drop Operation

```java
draggedElements, AbstractDiagramPresentationElement diagram)
{
   if (elementOver != null)
	{
		Element draggedElement = getSingleDraggedElement(draggedElements);
		if (draggedElement != null)
		{
			Namespace namespace = (Namespace) elementOver.getModelElement();
			Editing.getInstance(ModelElementProject.getProject(draggedElement))
					.execute("Change owner of element", () -> Elements.setOwningMembership(draggedElement, namespace, null));
			return true;
		}
	}
	return false;
}]]>
```

#### Best Practices

##### Perform Model Changes in a Transaction

Execute all model modifications using [`Editing.execute(...)`](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html#execute(java.lang.String,java.lang.Runnable)) to ensure proper undo and redo support.

##### Keep Validation Lightweight

`willAcceptDrop()` is invoked repeatedly during drag operations. Avoid expensive computations whenever possible.

##### Validate Inputs

Always verify that the target presentation element and dragged elements are valid before performing model changes.

##### Keep Handlers Focused

Each handler should implement a single drag-and-drop behavior to simplify maintenance and troubleshooting.

##### Avoid Side Effects in Validation

`willAcceptDrop()` should not modify the model or perform operations that change application state.

#### Troubleshooting

##### Handler Does Not Appear

- Verify that the handler factory is registered during plugin initialization.
- Confirm that the plugin is loaded successfully.

##### Drop Operation Is Not Available

- Verify that willAcceptDrop () returns true for the expected scenario.
- Check that the dragged and target elements satisfy the validation logic.
- Review application logs for exceptions thrown by willAcceptDrop () .

##### Drop Operation Fails

- Ensure model modifications are executed within an `Editing.execute(...)` transaction.
- Review application logs for exceptions raised during execution.
- Verify that the dragged element can legally be moved to the target namespace.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Custom drag-and-drop handlers allow plugins to provide additional drag-and-drop behavior for SysML v2 diagrams.</p><h2>Overview</h2><p>A custom drag-and-drop implementation consists of:</p><ul><li data-uuid="9c0028e5-c756-4261-ad5d-cd2972976966"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/kerml/diagram/ui/dnd/CustomDragAndDropHandler.html">CustomDragAndDropHandler</a></code> – defines the drag-and-drop behavior.</li><li data-uuid="5ddb351c-5f34-4dc1-a4d8-1470ce1a353a"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/kerml/diagram/ui/dnd/CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory.html">CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory</a></code> – creates handler instances.</li><li data-uuid="29509c46-dc05-4147-ae65-338d99fee134"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/kerml/diagram/ui/dnd/CustomDropDiagramHandlerFactory.html">CustomDropDiagramHandlerFactory</a></code> – registers handlers with the diagram framework.</li></ul><h2>Drag-and-Drop Lifecycle</h2><p>When a user drags model elements onto a diagram, the framework invokes registered handlers in the following order:</p><p><code><a>willAcceptDrop</a>()</code> – determines whether the handler can process the operation.<br /><code><a>drop</a>()</code> – performs the drop operation if the drop is accepted.<br /><code><code><a>willAcceptDrop</a></code>()</code> may be called repeatedly while the user moves the mouse. Therefore, it should perform only lightweight validation.</p><h2>Implementing a Handler</h2><p>Create a class extending <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/kerml/diagram/ui/dnd/CustomDragAndDropHandler.html">CustomDragAndDropHandler</a></code> and implement the required methods.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7f5be9a-902c-4e7e-8742-bef6ac253a93"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.kerml.diagram.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandler;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.AbstractDiagramPresentationElement;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;

import javax.annotation.CheckForNull;
import java.util.List;
import java.awt.Point;

public class MyDragAndDropHandler extends CustomDragAndDropHandler
{
	@Override
	public String getDescription()
	{
		return "My custom drag and drop action";
	}

	@Override
	public boolean willAcceptDrop(Point location, @CheckForNull PresentationElement elementOver, @CheckForNull List<Element> draggedElements,
 								  AbstractDiagramPresentationElement diagram)
	{
		// Validate whether the drop operation is allowed.
		return true;
	}

	@Override
	public boolean drop(Point location, @CheckForNull PresentationElement elementOver, @CheckForNull List<Element> draggedElements,
			            AbstractDiagramPresentationElement diagram)
	{
		// Perform the drop operation.
		return true;
	}
}

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Method Descriptions</h3><table class="wrapped"><thead class=""><tr class=""><th>Method</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/kerml/diagram/ui/dnd/CustomDragAndDropHandler.html#getDescription()">getDescription</a>()</code></td><td>Returns the text displayed for the drag-and-drop action.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><code><code><a>willAcceptDrop</a></code>()</code></td><td>Determines whether the handler can process the current drop operation. Return true if the drop is supported; otherwise return false.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><code><code><a>drop</a></code>()</code></td><td>Executes the drag-and-drop operation. Return true if the operation was performed successfully; otherwise return false.</td></tr></tbody></table><h2>Creating a Handler Factory</h2><p>Implement <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/kerml/diagram/ui/dnd/CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory.html">CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory</a></code> to create handler instances.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47a74c18-827f-43ec-a90b-af9ce6907818"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.kerml.diagram.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandler;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.kerml.diagram.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory;

public class MyDragAndDropHandlerFactory implements CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory
{
    @Override
    public CustomDragAndDropHandler createHandler()
    {
        return new MyDragAndDropHandler();
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2 class="cm-content q9tKkq_readonly m-0">Registering a Handler</h2><p>Register the factory during plugin initialization.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2bd9710-b779-4614-b8d6-773cc4c177e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.kerml.diagram.ui.dnd.CustomDropDiagramHandlerFactory;

@Override
public void init()
{
    CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory factory = MyDragAndDropHandler::new;
    CustomDropDiagramHandlerFactory.register(factory);
}

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2 class="cm-content q9tKkq_readonly m-0">Example</h2><p>The following example changes the owner of a dragged element to the namespace represented by the drop target.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4fec862a-38c5-400b-b0ca-744babe9d4d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.AbstractDiagramPresentationElement;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;

import javax.annotation.CheckForNull;
import java.awt.*;
import java.util.List;

@Override
public boolean willAcceptDrop(Point location, PresentationElement elementOver, List<Element> draggedElements, AbstractDiagramPresentationElement diagram)
{
    if (elementOver != null)
	{
		Element draggedElement = getSingleDraggedElement(draggedElements);
		if (draggedElement != null)
		{
			Element element = (Element) elementOver.getModelElement();
			return draggedElement.getOwner() != element;
		}
	}
	return false;
}

@CheckForNull
private static Element getSingleDraggedElement(@CheckForNull List<Element> draggedElements)
{
	return draggedElements != null && draggedElements.size() == 1 ? draggedElements.getFirst() : null;
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="cm-content q9tKkq_readonly m-0">Drop Operation</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8828c3b-96f6-4034-a5d7-8fc486409d69"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.editing.Editing;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.project.ModelElementProject;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.AbstractDiagramPresentationElement;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;

import javax.annotation.CheckForNull;
import java.awt.*;
import java.util.List;

@Override
public boolean drop(Point location, PresentationElement elementOver, List<Element> draggedElements, AbstractDiagramPresentationElement diagram)
{
   if (elementOver != null)
	{
		Element draggedElement = getSingleDraggedElement(draggedElements);
		if (draggedElement != null)
		{
			Namespace namespace = (Namespace) elementOver.getModelElement();
			Editing.getInstance(ModelElementProject.getProject(draggedElement))
					.execute("Change owner of element", () -> Elements.setOwningMembership(draggedElement, namespace, null));
			return true;
		}
	}
	return false;
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Best Practices</h2><h3>Perform Model Changes in a Transaction</h3><p>Execute all model modifications using <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html#execute(java.lang.String,java.lang.Runnable)"> <code>Editing.execute(...)</code></a> to ensure proper undo and redo support.</p><h3>Keep Validation Lightweight</h3><p><code><code><a>willAcceptDrop</a></code>()</code> is invoked repeatedly during drag operations. Avoid expensive computations whenever possible.</p><h3>Validate Inputs</h3><p>Always verify that the target presentation element and dragged elements are valid before performing model changes.</p><h3>Keep Handlers Focused</h3><p>Each handler should implement a single drag-and-drop behavior to simplify maintenance and troubleshooting.</p><h3>Avoid Side Effects in Validation</h3><p><code><code><a>willAcceptDrop</a></code>()</code> should not modify the model or perform operations that change application state.</p><h2>Troubleshooting</h2><h3>Handler Does Not Appear</h3><ul><li data-uuid="12f13e1a-c04f-4f96-89cb-d778d9fe3761">Verify that the handler factory is registered during plugin initialization.</li><li data-uuid="dad9f2fc-2854-4cf0-a571-c87cd608645c">Confirm that the plugin is loaded successfully.</li></ul><h3>Drop Operation Is Not Available</h3><ul><li data-uuid="70ca4d36-a1cf-414e-816d-bd48d8f09e4b">Verify that <code><a>willAcceptDrop</a></code><code>()</code> returns <code>true</code> for the expected scenario.</li><li data-uuid="2d24f19d-f17a-40fe-b62c-303340ee5375">Check that the dragged and target elements satisfy the validation logic.</li><li data-uuid="a99ba127-e5dd-488b-a750-181541ff48f8">Review application logs for exceptions thrown by <code><a>willAcceptDrop</a></code><code>()</code>.</li></ul><h3>Drop Operation Fails</h3><ul><li data-uuid="953ba4d6-4181-4e02-ac80-7df285b456b3">Ensure model modifications are executed within an<a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html#execute(java.lang.String,java.lang.Runnable)"> <code>Editing.execute(...)</code></a> transaction.</li><li data-uuid="f3c5e9cd-34e7-4d10-b0ef-534f7237ae95">Review application logs for exceptions raised during execution.</li><li data-uuid="7e40af8e-ba13-4007-b1a0-ccad69fafbd8">Verify that the dragged element can legally be moved to the target namespace.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573568 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00594: Custom elements numbering

- page_id: `249573568`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573568/Custom+elements+numbering
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:43:39.967+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The generic numbering open API provides two interfaces of different complexity. They allow a user to modify how the numbering process executes.

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='IJavaNumberPart interface' space=''] interface produces a single part of a number generated by the framework. It does not interfere with the execution of the framework, but simply provides a little piece of information included in the numbering.
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='INumberingAction interface' space=''] allows for generating complete numbers for sets of elements. All sorting and numbering is delegated to the implementing class. The framework will only register the generated numbers internally.

Classes that implement these interfaces can be set as the expression value of an Expression NumberPart. (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Numbering Scheme property values' space='MT']).

These classes have to be specified with the complete package and class name, for example, *com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.sample.SquareNumberPart*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

It does not interfere with the execution of the framework, but simply provides a little piece of information included in the numbering.</li><li>The <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="INumberingAction interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[INumberingAction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em> allows for generating complete numbers for sets of elements. All sorting and numbering is delegated to the implementing class. The framework will only register the generated numbers internally.</li></ul><p>Classes that implement these interfaces can be set as the expression value of an Expression NumberPart. (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Numbering Scheme property values" ri:space-key="MT" /></ac:link>).<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></p><p>These classes have to be specified with the complete package and class name, for example, <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.sample.SquareNumberPart</em>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="918a5c6d-e796-417e-ae7a-a2c46c77d972"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="845f50c5-47fd-4d7e-b95e-17c259663b9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573567 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00595: Custom Legend Item Implementation

- page_id: `249573567`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573567/Custom+Legend+Item+Implementation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:43:39.798+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to create Java implementation describing what model element or presentation element should a Legend Item adorn in a diagram.

1. The Java class should be implemented following the very same rules as when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementing a binary validation rule' space=''] . Each com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.Annotation object returned by the rule will cause the targeted model or presentation element to be adorned in the diagram (but only if the Legend is used in that diagram).
2. Specify the full class name as the value of "Implementation" property for the Legend Item in the modeling application.
3. Compile Java code, resulting in .class files possibly contained by a .jar file.
4. Ensure that your modeling tool can find and load these classes. You can place them in the program classpath or use the modeling tool plugins mechanism.

#### TIP: Example

Example

See *AdornComponentsOfInterestInDiagramValidator.java* example implementation in the *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\legendItem*directory.

Also see: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Each <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.Annotation</em> object returned by the rule will cause the targeted model or presentation element to be adorned in the diagram (but only if the Legend is used in that diagram).</li><li>Specify the full class name as the value of &quot;Implementation&quot; property for the Legend Item in the modeling application. </li><li>Compile Java code, resulting in .class files possibly contained by a .jar file.</li><li>Ensure that your modeling tool can find and load these classes. You can place them in the program classpath or use the modeling tool plugins mechanism.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6964f9b3-6d3c-4d75-bec5-da99cd79962f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>See <em>AdornComponentsOfInterestInDiagramValidator.java</em> example implementation in the <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\legendItem </em>directory.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />Also see: <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573493 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00596: Custom presentation elements rendering

- page_id: `249573493`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573493/Custom+presentation+elements+rendering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:02.409+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols)
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The custom symbol rendering API allows change a default default symbol view. This API includes:

1. Renderer provider API, which allows to provide a custom renderer for the specific presentation element.
2. Renderers, which can modify the default presentation element appearance.

In this section, we will review, how to register the specific presentation element views provider and give the example covering the custom renderers for the package, slots, and dependency link symbols.

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\symbolrendering.*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

This API includes:</p><ol><li>Renderer provider API, which allows to provide a custom renderer for the specific presentation element.</li><li>Renderers, which can modify the default presentation element appearance.</li></ol><p>In this section, we will review, how to register the specific presentation element views provider and give the example covering the custom renderers for the package, slots, and dependency link symbols.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\symbolrendering.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5400cec7-8f81-40d0-809c-38f6bac028a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="05088e21-c8f3-4865-8c3f-35850941b106"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
