````html
Project project = Project.getProject(element);

ViewDefinition viewDefinition =
    Diagrams.getViewDefinition(project, type);

ViewUsage viewUsage =
    Diagrams.createViewUsage(Package, viewDefinition);

Diagram diagram =
    Diagrams.createDiagram(
        viewUsage,
        viewDefinition,
        true,
        false,
        false,
        false
    );
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><hr /><h2>API Summary Used in This Flow</h2><h2>ViewDefinition lookup</h2><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="598adfa6-1a92-4e49-a159-164bbf68187b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Diagrams.getViewDefinition(project, type)
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Resolves a ViewDefinition based on<a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/dsl/ViewTypes.html"> visualization type</a>. </p><hr /><h3>ViewUsage creation</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f03fda1e-a094-4588-b10c-045771ab8097"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Diagrams.createViewUsage(element, viewDefinition)
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Creates a <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ViewUsage.html">ViewUsage </a>instance inside the specified element.</p><hr /><h3>Diagram creation</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3a8b50a-a8dd-4121-994e-a748d31e43a0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Diagrams.createDiagram(viewUsage, viewDefinition, true, false, false, false)
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Creates a diagram from the <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ViewUsage.html">ViewUsage </a>with control over:</p><ul><li>opening behavior</li><li>command execution</li><li>initialization options</li></ul><hr /><h2>Notes</h2><ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ViewDefinition.html">ViewDefinition </a>must exist in the project registry before diagram creation</li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ViewUsage.html">ViewUsage </a>acts as the semantic container for the diagram</li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Diagram.html">Diagram </a>creation can be fully controlled via boolean flags</li><li>Recommended entry point is always <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html">Project </a>→ <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ViewDefinition.html">ViewDefinition </a>→ <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ViewUsage.html">ViewUsage </a>→ <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Diagram.html">Diagram</a></li></ul><hr />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573664 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00614: Display Values in Diagrams

- page_id: `249573664`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573664/Display+Values+in+Diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-10T10:59:58.382+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > SysML v1 > Alf Plugin Developer Guide > Cameo Simulation Toolkit > Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Displaying Runtime Values

When any native model execution is not running in the Cameo Simulation Toolkit, an external tool executes the model. There is a capability to dynamically display any custom value string on parts and/or ports in structure diagrams, without modifying the model in Cameo Simulation Toolkit. Currently, only parts and ports that are typed with a primitive data type are supported.

Start by extending *com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.dashboard.api.ExternalValueProvider*class to create a custom implementation. The returned custom values will be displayed on the diagrams representing the *context*for which the *ExternalValueProvider* instance was constructed*.* The model is not modified in any way when these values are displayed. The color in which the values are displayed is configurable via the **Runtime Value Text Color on Shapes**project option. In the image below, two such values are provided and displayed in a diagram of the *Transmission.mdzip* sample project: **30.0 mi/h** for **carSpeed**and **19.0**for **wheelDiameter**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Image displaying the runtime values

Below is the implementation that was designed specifically for the *Transmission.mdzip* sample and provides just the two values seen in the above image:

```java
pathInContext)
	{
		// values should be pre-collected for diagram related performance reasons. Avoid time demanding implementations (e.g. remote calls) of this method
		if (element == carSpeedElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getCarSpeed() + " mi/h";
		}
		else if (element == wheelDiameterElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getWheelDiameter() + "\""; // double quote to represent inches
		}
		return null; // do not provide custom value for other elements in this context
	}

	/**
	 * To be called every time speed changes to call {@link #getValueText(Element, List)} for that element again
	 * @see #invalidate(Element)
	 * @see #invalidateAll() 
	 */
	public void invalidateCarSpeed()
	{
		invalidate(carSpeedElement);
	}
}]]>
```

To have the effect, an instance of the implemented provider must be registered to the *ExternalValueProviders* service, as illustrated here:

```java
// The root context of the external execution. Values will only be visible in the Diagrams that have this element set as context
	Classifier context = Finder.byQualifiedName().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::PowerTrain", Class.class);
	if (context != null)
	{
		EngineControlUnitValueProvider valueProvider = new EngineControlUnitValueProvider(context);
		ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).register(valueProvider);

		// if later the provider needs to be unregistered, it should be done by calling:
		// ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).unregister(valueProvider);
		// Since providers are registered for specific open project, there is no obligation to unregister manually on project close.
	}]]>
```

****The full code example is available in*<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\externalvalueindiagrams.*

##### Animating flowing information

Additionally, it is possible to display animation for the information that flows via connector from one end to another. For a fixed amount of time, a banner with the custom value will move from the source towards the target. During the full duration of the animation, the connector presentation element will be highlighted in color which is controlled by a project option **Active Color**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Image displaying the animation in between the connectors

```java
// select connector via which to animate
            ConnectorEnd sourceEnd = selectSourceEnd(connector); // pick end from which to start the animation
			String flowingInformation = "myFlowingInformation"; // hardcoded for example, should be some actual data compatible with end/connector/flow types
			FlowingInformationDescriptor descriptor = new FlowingInformationDescriptor(flowingInformation, connector, sourceEnd);

			long duration = 4000; // 4 seconds to reach the other end
			FlowAnimation.getInstance(Project.getProject(connector)).animate(descriptor, duration, finished -> {/* do something when animation completes*/});]]>
```

The full code example for this feature is available in*<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\flowanimation.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Displaying Runtime Values</h3><p>When any native model execution is not running in the Cameo Simulation Toolkit, an external tool executes the model. There is a capability to dynamically display any custom value string on parts and/or ports in structure diagrams, without modifying the model in Cameo Simulation Toolkit. Currently, only parts and ports that are typed with a primitive data type are supported.</p><p>Start by extending <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.dashboard.api.ExternalValueProvider </em>class to create a custom implementation. The returned custom values will be displayed on the diagrams representing the <em>context </em>for which the <em>ExternalValueProvider</em> instance was constructed<em>.</em> The model is not modified in any way when these values are displayed. The color in which the values are displayed is configurable via the  <strong>Runtime Value Text Color on Shapes </strong>project option. In the image below, two such values are provided and displayed in a diagram of the <em>Transmission.mdzip</em> sample project: <strong>30.0 mi/h</strong> for <strong>carSpeed </strong>and <strong>19.0 </strong>for <strong>wheelDiameter</strong>.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displayed_Values.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Image displaying the runtime values</h6><p>Below is the implementation that was designed specifically for the <em>Transmission.mdzip</em> sample and provides just the two values seen in the above image:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ac38052-7ee8-43ed-8f46-f0f3fe974dd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//Engine Control Unit refers to ECU block in the Transmission sample project
public class EngineControlUnitValueProvider extends ExternalValueProvider
{
	private final Element carSpeedElement;
	private final Element wheelDiameterElement;
	
	//Mimics some model execution process that happens outside this tool and produces values to display
	private final MockExternalExecution externalExecution;

	public EngineControlUnitValueProvider(Element context)
	{
		super(context);
		Project project = Project.getProject(context);
		carSpeedElement = FinderByQualifiedName.getInstance().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::ECU::carSpeed", Property.class);
		wheelDiameterElement = FinderByQualifiedName.getInstance().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::ECU::wheelDiameter", Property.class);
		externalExecution = new MockExternalExecution(this);
	}

	@CheckForNull
	@Override
	public String getValueText(Element element, List<? extends Element> pathInContext)
	{
		// values should be pre-collected for diagram related performance reasons. Avoid time demanding implementations (e.g. remote calls) of this method
		if (element == carSpeedElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getCarSpeed() + " mi/h";
		}
		else if (element == wheelDiameterElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getWheelDiameter() + "\""; // double quote to represent inches
		}
		return null; // do not provide custom value for other elements in this context
	}

	/**
	 * To be called every time speed changes to call {@link #getValueText(Element, List)} for that element again
	 * @see #invalidate(Element)
	 * @see #invalidateAll() 
	 */
	public void invalidateCarSpeed()
	{
		invalidate(carSpeedElement);
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To have the effect, an instance of the implemented provider must be registered to the <em>ExternalValueProviders</em> service, as illustrated here:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b0b5143-d3dd-4a29-bfc0-8b89d868f934"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Project project = <...>
	// The root context of the external execution. Values will only be visible in the Diagrams that have this element set as context
	Classifier context = Finder.byQualifiedName().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::PowerTrain", Class.class);
	if (context != null)
	{
		EngineControlUnitValueProvider valueProvider = new EngineControlUnitValueProvider(context);
		ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).register(valueProvider);

		// if later the provider needs to be unregistered, it should be done by calling:
		// ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).unregister(valueProvider);
		// Since providers are registered for specific open project, there is no obligation to unregister manually on project close.
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f9d9d5b-ed4b-48a7-a50a-738aab1b1876"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The full code example is available in<span> </span></span><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\externalvalueindiagrams.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Animating flowing information</h3><p>Additionally, it is possible to display animation for the information that flows via connector from one end to another. For a fixed amount of time, a banner with the custom value will move from the source towards the target. During the full duration of the animation, the connector presentation element will be highlighted in color which is controlled by a project option <strong>Active Color</strong>.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Flow_Animation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Image displaying the animation in between the connectors</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f39e5ca-598c-46ef-a577-bb49460f9e71"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[			Connector connector = <..> // select connector via which to animate
            ConnectorEnd sourceEnd = selectSourceEnd(connector); // pick end from which to start the animation
			String flowingInformation = "myFlowingInformation"; // hardcoded for example, should be some actual data compatible with end/connector/flow types
			FlowingInformationDescriptor descriptor = new FlowingInformationDescriptor(flowingInformation, connector, sourceEnd);

			long duration = 4000; // 4 seconds to reach the other end
			FlowAnimation.getInstance(Project.getProject(connector)).animate(descriptor, duration, finished -> {/* do something when animation completes*/});]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a5b4d2f-fdca-4666-bbd0-7e9d025f71f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The full code example for this feature is available in<span> </span></span><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\flowanimation.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=309365416 space=DEVG version=3 -->
## PAGE 00615: Displaying Elements

- page_id: `309365416`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/309365416/Displaying+Elements
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-05-28T19:58:52.032+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Managing Presentation Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The [Displaying](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html) utility class provides APIs for automatically displaying elements and relationships on diagrams.

These APIs simplify diagram population by allowing developers to programmatically display:

- Relationships between elements
- Nested members and features
- Tree-view structures
- Related model elements

The [Displaying](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html) APIs are intended for automated diagram generation and visualization workflows, helping reduce manual diagram editing and improve consistency.

All Displaying APIs should be invoked when no active session is open. 
These APIs internally perform diagram modifications and layout-related operations that are designed to execute without an active session.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html">Displaying</a> utility class provides APIs for automatically displaying elements and relationships on diagrams.</p><p>These APIs simplify diagram population by allowing developers to programmatically display:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f41a3752-c4ed-4314-99da-9ff7024a9acb">Relationships between elements</li><li data-uuid="b2bbf81f-07dc-4bb8-b8fb-e4e8999b65cb">Nested members and features</li><li data-uuid="3f90033c-6786-4d0d-a73d-b86f82e4cefc">Tree-view structures</li><li data-uuid="2e132272-2b2d-49af-99d0-9a68bf59c165">Related model elements</li></ul><p>The <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html">Displaying</a> APIs are intended for automated diagram generation and visualization workflows, helping reduce manual diagram editing and improve consistency.<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2bded7cb-69f4-4ec6-9162-f430650d837d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All Displaying APIs should be invoked when no active session is open.<br />These APIs internally perform diagram modifications and layout-related operations that are designed to execute without an active session.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````
